Spectroscopic observations of 63 H ii regions in six spiral galaxies (NGC 628, NGC 783, NGC 2336, NGC 6217, NGC 7331, and NGC 7678) were carried out with the 6-meter telescope (BTA) of Russian Special Astrophysical Observatory with the Spectral Camera attached to the focal reducer SCORPIO in the multislit mode with a dispersion of 2.1 /pixel and a spectral resolution of 10 . These observations were used to estimate the oxygen and nitrogen abundances and the electron temperatures in H ii regions through the recent variant of the strong line method (NS calibration). The parameters of the radial distribution (the extrapolated central intercept value and the gradient) of the oxygen and nitrogen abundances in the disks of spiral galaxies NGC 628, NGC 783, NGC 2336, NGC 7331, and NGC 7678 have been determined. The abundances in the NGC 783, NGC 2336, NGC 6217, and NGC 7678 are measured for the first time.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 628 as a face-on spiral test case, separating 8 micron dust emission tied to HII regions from substantial emission heated by older stellar populations.

quotes.quote: NGC 628 We chose to work on the unbarred Sc galaxy NGC 628 because it is relatively nearby (7.2 Mpc; kennicutt11 ), large in angular size ( 10.5 9.5 arcmin ^ 2 ), virtually face on (i=6 ^ ) and observed in all the bands ( , 8 , 3.6 ) we require. NGC 628 is not classified as an AGN either by optical emission lines ho97a or by mid-IR emission lines goulding09 . Its stellar mass is 3.6 10^ 9 skibba11 and its current star formation rate (SFR) is 0.68 yr ^ 1 kennicutt11 . Based on spectral energy distribution fitting, aniano12 determine that 11.6 of NGC 628's infrared luminosity comes from dust heated at high radiation field intensities (U > 10^ 2 ), a typical value among the SINGS sample spirals.

Integrated values Excluding star-forming regions identified by , we arrive at a total (out to ) non-HII region dust fraction of 71 in NGC 628. Then, correcting for other contributions from very young ( < 10 Myr) stars we arrive at a fraction of 62 . Considering the amount of UV produced by 10-100 Myr stars (see Section 3.5), we estimate a lower limit to the amount of 8 dust emission heated by sources other than young stars as 43 .

sources.summary: The paper measures direct abundances for H II regions in NGC 628, deriving its first direct oxygen gradient, a shallower slope than empirical studies, and an outer-disk N/O plateau.

quotes.quote: We have uniformly determined oxygen abundance metallicities for 14 H 2 regions in the local spiral galaxy NGC 628 and seven H 2 regions in NGC 2403. With high-quality spectroscopic observations, we measured the intrinsically faint [ O 3 ] 4363 and/or [ N 2 ] 5755 auroral lines at a strength of 3 or greater, and explicitly determine electron temperatures in all 14 H 2 regions in NGC 628.

Our results provide the first ever oxygen abundance gradient derived solely from direct abundance measurements for H 2 regions in NGC 628. From the 4 data we derive an oxygen abundance gradient of 12 + log(O/H) = (8.43 0.03) + (-0.017 0.002) R_g (dex/kpc), with a dispersion in log(O/H) of = 0.10 dex. This is a significantly shallower slope than found by previous empirical abundance studies.

For the N/O ratio we find a negative gradient with increasing galactocentric radius in the inner disks of both NGC 628 and NGC 2403. Further out in the outer disk, this relationship flattens out in NGC 628, where we find a plateau past R_g = R_ 25 near an oxygen abundance of 12+log(O/H) = 8.3.

sources.summary: NGC 628 is presented as a nearly face-on spiral with regular gas and stars whose residual velocity field is dominated by a strong warped disk rather than significant ram pressure.

quotes.quote: NGC 628 is a nearly face-on spiral galaxy (inclination of the inner disk is 14 ) and exhibits a very regular gas and stellar distribution as shown in Fig. fig:NGC628dpj . However, the major axis of the velocity field shows a strong variation as function of radial distance from the center which is caused by a strongly warped disk Sho84, Kam92 . The residual velocity pattern is consistent with a strong warp which causes the apparent drop in rotation velocity towards larger radii if one would assume a constant P.A. and inclination over the entire disk as plotted in Fig. fig:NGC628rotc .

Our results show an excellent agreement with a strongly warped disk given a dominant m=1 mode with vc_1 36 km s ^ 1 as presented in Fig. fig:NGC628_fig5 and Fig. fig:NGC628_fig6 . The warped disk is characterized by a change in inclination of i=14 and position angle of =73 which occurs at a radius of 16 kpc . This is in good agreement with the warp geometry described in who found a change in inclination of 11 and PA of 75 at a transition radius of 7--11 ( 14--22 kpc). While the fit also indiates small contributions from m=0 and m=2 modes, their significance is too low to make any conclusions about ram pressure interaction in NGC 628.

sources.summary: The paper measures M74's TRGB distance from HST resolved-star photometry and highlights the galaxy as a nearby spiral hosting three core-collapse supernovae.

quotes.quote: M74 (NGC 628) is a famous face-on spiral galaxy, hosting three core-collapse supernovae (SNe):SN Ic 2002ap, SN II-P 2003gd, and SN II-P 2013ej. However its distance is not well known. We present distance estimation for this galaxy based on the Tip of the Red Giant Branch (TRGB) method. We obtain photometry of the resolved stars in the arm-free region of M74 from F555W and F814W images in the Hubble Space Telescope archive. The color-magnitude diagram of the resolved stars shows a dominant red giant branch (RGB) as well as blue main sequence stars, red helium burning stars, and asymptotic giant branch stars.

The I band luminosity function of the RGB stars shows the TRGB to be at I_ TRGB = 26.13 0.03 mag, and T_ RGB =25.97 0.03 . From this we derive the distance modulus to M74 to be 30.04 0.04 (random) 0.12 (systematic) (corresponding to a linear distance, 10.19 0.14 0.56 Mpc).

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 628, a nearby spiral, using LBT/MODS H II-region spectra to derive abundance gradients and infer that its interstellar medium is chemically well mixed at fixed radius.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: Properties of NGC 628 Property & NGC 628 R.A. & 01:36:41.747 Dec. & 15:47:01.18

quotes.quote: We present LBT MODS observations of 62 H 2 regions in the nearby spiral galaxy NGC 628, with an unprecedentedly large number of auroral lines measurements (18 [ O 3 ] 4363 , 29 [ N 2 ] 5755 , 40 [ S 3 ] 6312 , and 40 [ O 2 ] 7320,7330 detections) in 45 H 2 regions. Comparing derived temperatures from multiple auroral line measurements, we find: (1) a strong correlation between temperatures based on [ S 3 ] 6312 and [ N 2 ] 5755 ; and (2) large discrepancies for some temperatures based on [ O 2 ] 7320,7330 and [ O 3 ] 4363 . Both of these trends are consistent with other observations in the literature, yet, given the widespread use and acceptance of [ O 3 ] 4363 as a temperature determinant, the magnitude of the T [ O 3 ] discrepancies still came as a surprise. Based on these results, we conduct a uniform abundance analysis prioritizing the temperatures derived from [ S 3 ] 6312 and [ N 2 ] 5755 , and report the gas-phase abundance gradients for NGC 628.

Relative abundances of S/O, Ne/O, and Ar/O are constant across the galaxy, consistent with no systematic change in the upper IMF over the sampled range in metallicity. These alpha-element ratios, along with N/O, all show small dispersions ( 0.1 dex) over 70 of the azimuthally averaged radius. We interpret these results as an indication that, at a given radius, the interstellar medium in NGC 628 is chemically well-mixed.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 628 with gas-star instability maps, comparing predicted unstable regions with star-forming areas in its nearly face-on, two-armed spiral disk.

quotes.quote: The gas-stars instability criterion for infinitesimally thin disc was applied to the galaxy NGC 628. Instead of using the azimuthally averaged profiles of data the maps of the gas surface densities (THINGS, HERACLES), of the velocity dispersions of stars (VENGA) and gas (THINGS) and of the surface brightness of the galaxy (S ^4 G) were analyzed. All these maps were collected for the same region with a noticeable star formation rate and were superimposed on each other. Using the data on the rotation curve values of Q_ eff were calculated for each pixel in the image.

NGC 628 (M74) is an SA(s) galaxy with two grand-design spiral arms. The galaxy is visible almost face-on under inclination i=7 . Herrmann2008 compared different estimates of the distance and found that D=8.6 Mpc is the most accurate estimate (see figure 5 in Herrmann2008 and more recent paper by Kreckel2017 ), while L08 used D=7.3 Mpc.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 628 as a nearby face-on SAc multiple-arm spiral with no bulge, weak arms, and no significant cluster-age offset across its spiral arms.

quotes.quote: NGC 628 (M74) is the largest galaxy in its group. This nearby galaxy is seen almost face--on ( i = 25.2 ^ ) and is located at a distance of 9.9 Mpc Oliver . It has no bulge cor and is classified as a SAc spiral galaxy. Its star formation rate and stellar mass obtained from the extinction--corrected B--band luminosity are 3.6 M_ yr^ 1 and 1.1 10^ 10 M_ , respectively Lee, Both . NGC 628 is a multiple--arm spiral galaxy Debra with two well--defined spiral arms.

NGC 628 is a multiple--arm spiral galaxy with weak spiral arms consistent with a pattern speed decreasing with radius and multiple corotation radii. In this case we find no significant offset among the azimuthal distributions of star clusters with different ages, which is consistent with the swing amplification theory.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes MUSE spectra of NGC 628, an almost face-on grand-design spiral, and finds spatially resolved star formation self-regulation with a mass-loading factor consistent with galaxy-formation models.

quotes.quote: We have performed stellar population analysis using MUSE optical spectra of the spiral galaxy NGC 628 and find that current maximum star formation in spatially resolved regions is regulated according to the level of star formation in the recent past. We propose a model based on the self-regulator or ``bathtub'' models, but for spatially resolved regions of the galaxy. We name it the ``resolved self-regulator model'' and show that the predictions of this model are in agreement with the presented observations. We observe star formation self-regulation and estimate the mass-loading factor, =2.5 0.5 , consistent with values predicted by galaxy formation models.

NGC 628 is an almost face-on, grand design spiral galaxy, with a SFR =0.15 M_ /yr and a total stellar mass of M_ * =1.2 10^ 10 M_ 2018ApJS..234...35Z , right on the main sequence of star formation. NGC 628 has a disc-like pseudobulge 2011AJ....142...16Z , which helps us in the interpretation of the SFHs since the formation of the disc and the pseudobulge are both via secular evolution. The study would hardly be affected by the presence of a classical bulge since this would be made of stars older than the scale of ages we analyse.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 628's star-cluster and H II region pairs to measure star-formation propagation, treating it as a regular symmetric galaxy with one of the most reliable samples.

quotes.quote: The final sample includes 503 SC-H ii R pairs in NGC 628, 70 pairs in NGC 3184, 254 pairs in NGC 3726, 40 pairs in NGC 5585, and 577 pairs in NGC 6946.

Note that three of five studied galaxies (NGC 628, NGC 3184, and NGC 6946) have a regular symmetrical morphology. NGC 5585 has rather the Large Magellanic Cloud type. NGC 3726 has a distorted asymmetric disc. In addition, it is the most distant galaxy of the sample and, therefore, we were unable to identify a sufficient number of close SC-H ii R pairs with S<80 pc in it (Fig. figure:age_sep ). The most reliable results are those obtained for NGC 628 and NGC 6946. On the one hand, these galaxies have a symmetrical regular structure, on the other hand, they provide us with a large statistical material for analysis -- more than 500 SC-H ii R pairs.

The graphs in Fig. figure:age_sep and the coefficients given in Table table:coeff show closely resembling slopes for cluster samples in three galaxies (NGC 628, NGC 3184, and NGC 6946) with a regular structure. The calculated slopes lie in the range 1.0-1.2 for separations from 40 to 200 pc. In the outer range of S (100-500 pc), the slopes 0.5 for pair samples in NGC 628 and NGC 6946.

sources.summary: The paper studies 65 star-forming complexes in NGC 628, describing it as a nearly face-on classic spiral with old stellar populations, recent SFC islands, and unusual arm asymmetry.

quotes.quote: NGC 628 is a classic spiral galaxy viewed almost side-on, enabling us to isolate the largest number of SFCs. A homogeneous distribution of stellar complexes is observed along its long arm, while this distribution is not homogeneous along its short arm . This does not have a unique explanation, but it would seem to be unusual for a seemingly ordinary spiral galaxy. The stellar population of NGC 628 is mostly old. However, there are small ``islets'' of SFCs formed relatively recently.

The numbers of SFCs studied were 65 in NGC 628, 7 in NGC 2976, and 23 in NGC 3351. The aperture areas were calculated using distance estimates of 9.77 Mpc for NGC 628, 3.63 Mpc for NGC 2976, and 10.57 Mpc for NGC 3351 .

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC0628 among eight nearby face-on grand-design spirals, with DustPedia properties and spatially resolved SED maps showing spiral-arm star formation and a less star-forming bulge.

quotes.quote: The final sample includes 8 objects: NGC0628, NGC3184, NGC3938, NGC4254, NGC4321, NGC4535, NGC5194, NGC5457. Distances, sizes, inclinations and morphologies are those adopted by the DustPedia collaboration, based on the HyperLEDA database The HyperLEDA database is available at http://leda.univ-lyon1.fr/ 2014A&A...570A..13M , and are available on their website (see Tab. tab:sample ). The sample stellar masses are in the range between 10^ 10 10^ 11 M_ , and star formation rates between 1 and 4 M_ yr ^ 1 These sources represent the evolved descendants of high-redshift normal star-forming galaxies, that are rotationally supported systems, at least by z=2 e.g. 2015ApJ...799..209W, 2009ApJ...706.1364F, 2018ApJS..238...21F . Moreover, their regular dynamical properties suggest that they have quiescently assembled their stellar mass through cosmic times.

The central bulge can be less (as in NGC0628, NGC3184, NGC3938, NGC5457) or more (as in NGC4254, NGC4321, NGC5194) starforming than the spiral arms. For those galaxies that have a 'red' bulge, a 'bending' of the MS relation is observed at the high _ end, as the cells corresponding to the bulge component are also the more massive ones. It is worth noting that for the galaxies in our sample few spaxels would be considered passive (1 dex below the MS). This is a consequence of the sample selection, made of grand-design spirals. Also, due to the sample selection, there are no morphological trends in the log _ log _ SFR plane, i.e. in scatter from the global (or individual) MS or in slope.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 628 dwA as a candidate satellite classical dwarf, estimates a stellar mass of 3.4 million solar masses, and finds little current star formation or gas.

quotes.quote: We found 2 satellite galaxy candidates, NGC 628 dwA and NGC 628 dwB with M _ V = -12.2 and M _ V = -7.7 , respectively. Folding in our two candidates and completeness results, the derived luminosity function for this host is in line with CDM expectations. We estimate the stellar masses of both candidates to be 3.4 10^6 M _ and 3.1 10^4 M _ 2.1 10^5 M _ for NGC 628 dwA and dwB, respectively. This puts NGC 628 dwA in the classical dwarf galaxy regime, while NGC 628 dwB straddles the boundary between ultrafaint and classical dwarfs in stellar mass.

We find no evidence of ongoing star formation with upper limits of 1.2 10^ 4 M _ / yr and 5.4 10^ 5 M _ / yr , respectively. We also place upper limits on the total neutral hydrogen gas mass of 5 10^5 M _ . These data indicate that star formation in the candidates has been recently quenched, on the order of 1 Gyr ago rather than very early.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 628 as a nearby grand-design spiral, resolving 300 UV star-forming regions; it finds similar arm properties, no age gradient, and a bright headlight cloud.

quotes.quote: In this study, we focus on the secular evolution of NGC 628, a spiral galaxy in the local universe. We exploit the resolution of UVIT to resolve up to 63 pc in NGC 628 for identification and characterization of the star forming regions. We identify 300 star forming regions in the UVIT FUV image of NGC 628 using ProFound and the identified regions are characterized using Starburst99 models.

We find that there is no significant difference in the star formation properties between the two arms of NGC 628. We also quantify the azimuthal offset of the star forming regions of different ages. Since we do not find an age gradient, we suggest that the spiral density waves might not be the possible formation scenario of the spiral arms of NGC 628. The headlight cloud present in the disk of the galaxy is found to be having the highest star formation rate density ( 0.23 M_ yr^ 1 kpc^ 2 ) compared to other star forming regions on spiral arms and the rest of the galaxy. galaxies: spiral -- star formation -- evolution Star formation in a galaxy is considered one among the best aids to understand the evolution of the galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper maps NGC 628 with JWST/MIRI, characterizing low-surface-density diffuse ISM structure, partial disk coverage, long filaments, and a topology transition near 13 solar masses per pc^2.

quotes.quote: We present a pilot study that explores using PAH emission in this way based on MIRI observations of IC 5332, NGC 628, NGC 1365, and NGC 7496 from the PHANGS-JWST Treasury. Using scaling relationships calibrated in LEROY1_PHANGSJWST , scaled F1130W provides 10--40 pc resolution and 3 sensitivity of _ gas 2 \ pc ^ 2 . We characterize the surface densities of structures seen at <7 \ pc ^ 2 in our targets, where we expect the gas to be HI-dominated. We highlight the existence of filaments, inter-arm emission, and holes in the diffuse ISM at these low surface densities. Below 10 \ pc ^ 2 for NGC 628, NGC 1365, and NGC 7496 the gas distribution shows a ``Swiss cheese''-like topology due to holes and bubbles pervading the relatively smooth distribution of diffuse ISM.

The maps of NGC 628 and NGC 1365 cover only parts of the galaxy disks, and the majority of pixels in these two maps have surface densities above 7 . In NGC 7496, the map extends into the outskirts of the disk, so we can observe the faint, low surface density structures in the outskirts. IC 5332 has low surface density throughout its disk, and a large fraction of the emission in the map is below 7 \ pc ^ 2 . This is in good agreement with the faint CO emission observed in this galaxy leroy2021 . Many of the faint structures in all of the targets appear filamentary. Filaments in the PAH emission are explored in much greater detail in THILKER_PHANGSJWST and MEIDT_PHANGSJWST in this Issue. In some cases, filamentary structures are observed to have widths comparable to a few resolution elements, and lengths that are substantially longer. For instance, in a few cases in NGC 628, we measure lengths of up to 400 pc for filamentary structures with _ gas < 7 \ pc ^ 2 , yielding a 10:1 aspect ratio. Many of these filamentary structures appear as spurs or low surface density continuations of spiral arm structures. While such morphology may be expected as a consequence of the spiral structure of the galaxies, previous \ observations have not had the resolution to characterize these outside the Local Group. Throughout the diffuse ISM, structure is evident down to the noise level of the maps ( 3 2 ). At present, comparing our predicted surface densities from scaled F1130W to measured CO and \ is limited by the available gas observations. The ALMA CO observations from the PHANGS survey have noise limits near this surface density leroy2021,LEROY1_PHANGSJWST . More limiting is the availability and resolution of \ data. NGC 628 was observed as part of the THINGS survey walter2008 and the ``robust'' weighted map has 6'' resolution. NGC 7496 has recent MeerKAT observations at 12'' resolution. Future observations with higher resolution, higher S/N \ and CO maps for the PHANGS-JWST targets will be necessary to measure gas surface densities for comparison to the F1130W emission at these faint levels. One of the distinctive features of the faint regions of the F770W and F1130W maps is the clear presence of holes in the ISM distribution (see Figures fig:contour_ic5332 to fig:contour_7496 ). These holes may be due to dynamical effects or feedback, topics explored in more detail in WATKINS_PHANGSJWST , BARNES_PHANGSJWST , and MEIDT_PHANGSJWST . The pervasive emission in the PAH-tracing bands is one of the reasons these holes appear so clearly in the JWST maps. To quantify the topology of the faint emission, we use the genus statistic. The genus statistic is the difference between the number of connected regions above and below a brightness threshold. A positive genus corresponds to a topology reflecting compact regions or sources that are bright compared to the threshold, surrounded by connected networks of lower brightness regions. Positive genus topology is referred to as source-dominated or a ``meatball''-like. A negative genus is characterized by connected networks of emission above the threshold level and isolated holes below the threshold, which are referred to as a hole-dominated or a ``Swiss-cheese'' topology Coles1988MNRAS.234..509C . By computing the genus through a range of intensity values, the resulting genus curve characterizes variations in the topology. When the genus curve crosses zero, the topology changes from ``Swiss-cheese'' to ``meatball''. The genus statistic is a well-known measure of spatial topology that was developed to study the topology of the cosmic microwave background Coles1988MNRAS.234..509C and has since been used to quantify the topology of the turbulent ISM, both in simulations and observations e.g., KowalLazarian2007ApJ...666L..69K,ChepurnovGordon2008ApJ...688.1021C,BurkhartStanimirovic2010ApJ...708.1204B,KochWard2017MNRAS.471.1506K,Burkhart2021PASP..133j2001B . Here we use the implementation of the Genus statistic in the TurbuStat v1.3; turbustat.readthedocs.io package Koch2019 . We compute the genus curve for each scaled F1130W image at native resolution using a set of intensities chosen by percentiles in the images. We discard all regions smaller than 20 pixels, 4 the F1130W PSF area, in the images to minimize the effect of noise on the genus curves, where a connected region includes pixels adjacent along both the edges and corners. We note that the zero-crossing point is not strongly affected by the choice of minimum area (for 10 - 40 pixels). There is no restriction on the morphology of a region (e.g., round vs. elongated). We additionally impose three masking constraints on the data when computing the genus: We mask the PSF artifacts from the active galactic nuclei in NGC 1365 and NGC 7496. We use PSFs generated with WebbPSF and impose a masking cut-off at 1/1000^ th the maximum of the the normalized PSF. Two negative strips remain in the NGC 1365 (vertical through center, see Figure fig:contour_1365 ) and NGC 7496 (horizontal through center, see Figure fig:contour_7496 ) mosaics after our calibration. We impose by-hand masks to remove the visible negative regions. Finally, we apply a radial mask at R_ gal > 0.4R_ 25 to provide a consistent basis for comparison between the 4 targets. This corresponds to radial cut-offs of 3.2, 5.7, 13.7, 3.6 kpc in IC 5332, NGC 628, NGC 1365 and NGC 7496, respectively. This cut-off was chosen based on the range of R_ gal that is well-sampled across all four galaxies, and where diffuse PAH emission is visually obvious in Figures fig:contour_ic5332 to fig:contour_7496 . Masks applied to the data will reduce the value of the genus statistic, roughly proportional to the total area, but will not change the relative shape within a single closed contour. Because of the wide dynamic range, we empirically choose 600 thresholds linearly sampled from the 0.01 to 98^ th percentiles, and 50 logarithmically sampled thresholds from the 98^ th to 99.99^ th percentiles. This ensures we sample the genus curve well near zero-crossings, tracing transitions from ``Swiss cheese'' to ``meatball'' topologies in the faint emission tail, while still characterizing the high-end intensity tail. Figure fig:genus_1130 shows the genus curves in the F1130W band We note the F770W band has a similar topology and the genus analysis gives similar results, though with lower surface density sensitivity (see sec:obs ). computed with a cut R_ gal < 0.4R_ 25 . There is a clear transition in the topology traced by the 11.3 m emission that varies by galaxy. Table tab:genus_crossings provides the zero crossing points from the genus curves. The genus crossing corresponds to a gas surface density of 8 - 10 \ (scaled from Eq. eq:ratio113 ) for NGC 628, NGC 1365, and NGC 7496. This closely matches the expected H _2 HI transition point of 10 \ based on shielding models at solar metallicity. The genus zero crossing point in IC 5332 is substantially lower, at 1.6 \ (0.3 MJy sr ^ 1 ), 2 significance above the background level of the map, and thus affected by noise fluctuations altering the genus curve. A field of Gaussian noise will produce a similar transition from negative to positive genus, with a zero-crossing at the mean field value Coles1988MNRAS.234..509C . The significant detected structure alters the shape of the genus, as we see in Figure fig:genus_1130 , but for the sensitivity-limited IC 5332, the genus zero-crossing is qualitatively consistent with a noise-like field. The diffuse PAH emission in IC 5332 being sensitivity-limited is a substantially lower filling fraction in the PAH emission compared to the other three galaxies (Figure fig:contour_ic5332 ). Given that IC 5332 is the only dwarf spiral in this sample, with a lower stellar mass, star formation rate, and metallicity relative to the other galaxies, this lower filling fraction at a common sensitivity limit is expected for example, in the Magellenic Clouds Jameson2018,Chevance2020b . The genus zero crossing point for IC 5332 then indicates that the genus is limited to measuring topology changes for tracers with a high filling fraction (see sec:discussion_topology ). l|| Genus zero crossings Target & Intensity & _ gas & (MJy sr ^ 1 ) & (M _ pc ^ 2 ) IC 5332 & 0.3 & 1.6 NGC 628 & 2.5 & 13.0 NGC 1365 & 1.5 & 8.0 NGC 7496 & 1.9 & 10.1 Zero crossings computed from the genus curves in Figure fig:genus_1130 .

sources.summary: The paper studies M74/NGC 628 as a nearby face-on grand-design spiral, classifying 432 PHANGS GMCs by star-formation activity and tying cloud evolution to galaxy-wide star formation.

quotes.quote: We investigated the giant molecular clouds (GMCs) in M74 (NGC 628) obtained by the PHANGS project. We applied the GMC Types according to the activity of star formation: Type I without star formation, Type II with H luminosity ( ) smaller than 10^ 37.5 , and Type III with \ greater than 10^ 37.5 . In total, 432 GMCs were identified, where the individual GMC Types are 65, 203, and 164, for Type I, Type II, and Type III, respectively. The size and mass of the GMCs range from 23 -- 237 pc and 10^ 4.9 - 10^ 7.1 M _ , showing a trend that mass and radius increase from Type I to II to III.

In the present work, we focus on NGC 628 (M74), which is a nearby ( d = 9.7 Mpc , Anand2021 ), SAc type grand-design spiral galaxy. The galaxy has a small inclination angle and is suited for a detailed study of galaxy evolution including star formation and interstellar medium (ISM) evolution. The galaxy is known to exhibit a moderate star formation rate (SFR) of 2 M_ yr^ 1 over the whole galaxy Sanchez2011 .

sources.summary: The paper combines SIGNALS HII-region data and LEGUS star-cluster catalogues for nearby spiral NGC 628 to estimate LyC escape fractions and relate them to cluster and line properties.

quotes.quote: In this work, we present a semi-novel method to estimate the escape fraction by combining broadband photometry of star clusters from the Legacy ExtraGalactic UV Survey (LEGUS) with regions observed by the Star formation, Ionized gas, and Nebular Abundances Legacy Survey (SIGNALS) in the nearby spiral galaxy NGC 628. We first assess the completeness of the combined catalogue, and find that 49 of regions lack corresponding star clusters as a result of a difference in the sensitivities of the LEGUS and SIGNALS surveys. For regions that do have matching clusters, we infer the escape fraction from the difference between the ionising power required to produce the observed luminosity and the predicted ionising photon output of their host star clusters; the latter is computed using a combination of LEGUS photometric observations and a stochastic stellar population synthesis code slug (Stochastically Lighting Up Galaxies). Overall, we find an escape fraction of = 0.09^ +0.06 _ 0.06 across our sample of 42 regions; in particular, we find regions with high are predominantly regions with low luminosity.

NGC 628 is the first galaxy observed by SIGNALS, which uses the SITELLE Imaging Fourier Transform Spectrograph (IFTS) to achieve high spectral resolution over a FoV large enough (11' 11') to enable mapping of full galactic discs. The SIGNALS catalogue for NGC 628 includes 4285 regions imaged at a spatial resolution of 35 pc 2018MNRAS.477.4152R . We combine this catalogue with the LEGUS Legacy ExtraGalactic UV Survey; 2015AJ....149...51C survey, which provides a total of 1648 candidate star clusters in NGC 628 see 2015ApJ...815...93G, 2017ApJ...841..131A .

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 628 as a detailed example of mid-infrared and emission maps, finding much of its 21 emission outside optical regions and only a tiny embedded-source contribution.

quotes.quote: To discuss the relation between \ and 21 \ emission more quantitatively we focus on one of the galaxies in our sample, NGC 628, a grand-design spiral galaxy with log(M_ /M_ ) = 10.3 . Fig. fig:fig_NGC628 (top row) presents a comparison between the 21 \ and \ maps for NGC 628 at matched (copt) resolution. To quantify the spatial differences between \ and 21 \ emission we compute the 21 \ flux within and outside the optically defined \ region masks (blue contours in Fig. fig:fig_NGC628 , bottom left). We find that in NGC 628, 54 of the 21 \ emission, but only 36 of the \ emission, lies outside the \ region masks.

While the vast majority of 21 \ point sources overlap with \ regions, in NGC 628 we find 53 non-overlapping sources, accounting for 0.7 of the total 21 \ emission (shown as red circles in Fig. fig:fig_NGC628 , bottom left). These sources do not overlap with supernova remnants or planetary nebulae in the catalog, so we consider them likely fully embedded star-forming regions. Over the sub-sample of four galaxies in common with we find that the fraction of 21 \ emission in such embedded point sources is of the order of a few percent.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 628 as the template for simulations of an isolated, late-type, nearly face-on spiral, adopting its spiral structure, photometry, gas profile, and rotation-scale constraints.

quotes.quote: Specifically the galaxy NGC 628 is used as a template.

As template for the model galaxy the beautiful face-on spiral NGC 628 is used. It has a regular spiral structure and certainly not a bar. This gives the possibility to compare the structure resulting from the simulations with an actual galaxy in an unambiguous way. It is not intended to make a model of NGC 628 in all its details, but only to generate a galaxy which has roughly the same dimensions. Photometry of NGC 628 is presented by Shostak van der Kruit (1984) and by Natali et al. (1992).
NGC 628 is observed in by Wevers et al. (1986) who give the radial gas density.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 628 as a nearby, nearly face-on grand-design spiral whose dominant two-armed pattern includes secondary and inner three-armed structure.

quotes.quote: The proximity (distance of about 10 Mpc) and the orientation of NGC 628 makes it a convenient object to study the spiral structure. NGC 628 is a grand design galaxy that has two principal spiral arms, and a number of secondary arms. The I-band image taken from Larsen Richtler (1999) and shown in the left panel of Figure 9. Fourier analysis of the spiral structure of NGC 628 by Puerari Dottori (1992) shows that the two-armed spiral pattern is a dominating component. However, there is a considerable admixture of a three-armed spiral in the inner regions of the galaxy.
The rotation curve of NGC 628 was measured by Kamphuis Briggs (1992). They find a maximum rotation velocity of the disk to be about 200 km s ^ 1 taking the inclination of NGC 628 of 6.5 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 628 as the central face-on spiral of a gas-rich group, with companions, a narrow H I profile, extended emission, and no detected HVC-like clouds.

quotes.quote: The group is centred round a large, face-on spiral, NGC 628 and the peculiar spiral NGC 660. NGC 628 is associated with several companions: UGC 1104, UGC 1171, UGC 1176(DDO13), UGC A20 and KDG10.

The recessional velocity of NGC 628 is 660 (Huchra, Vogeley Geller, 1999; de Vaucouleurs, 1991; Kamphuis Briggs, 1992) placing it at approximately 10 Mpc away (assuming no peculiar motion and H_o = 75 Mpc ^ 1 , as used in Briggs, 1986). Supernovae measurements (Hendry 2005) give a distance measurement of 9.3 1.8 Mpc.

NGC 628 has a very narrow velocity width, W_ 50 = 56 (Kamphuis Briggs 1992). It is close enough to allow detection of low-mass objects with only M _ HI = 2 10^6 M _ of neutral hydrogen, but separated enough from local Galactic gas to be able to detect any high velocity gas associated with the galaxy. Kamphuis Briggs (1992) looked at this galaxy at with the VLA and discovered high velocity gas on the outskirts of NGC 628 but nothing that could be considered HVCs.

sources.summary: The paper maps NGC 628 in CO, describing it as an isolated, face-on spiral with an extended disk, clumpy molecular gas along spiral arms, and patchy warm molecular emission.

quotes.quote: NGC 628 (M 74) is an isolated, near face-on spiral of type SA(s). It is known to have an extended disk, extending to approximately three times the optical Holmberg radius kam92 . observations of NGC 628 for BIMA SONG hel03 only cover the inner region of the galaxy, but show that the molecular gas is clumpy and distributed mostly along the spiral arms. It is slightly less luminous than the other two galaxies we are examining here, and also has the lowest H and luminosity of the three.

The integrated intensity maps of NGC 628 (Fig. fig:ngc0628 a,b) show that the warm molecular gas detected in the NGLS line observations is patchy, of relatively low intensity, and mostly follows the spiral arms of the galaxy. The center region shows neither a strong concentration nor a strong dip in the molecular gas.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 628’s outer disk kinematics, finding a cold extended UV disk whose surface mass density is mostly explained by observed gas and stars.

quotes.quote: We study the kinematics of GALEX-selected \ knots in the outer disk (beyond R_ 25 ) of NGC 628 (M74), a galaxy representative of large, undisturbed, extended UV (Type 1 XUV) disks. Our spectroscopic target sample of 235 of the bluest UV knots surrounding NGC 628 yielded 15 \ detections ( 6 ), roughly the number expected given the different mean ages of the two populations. The measured vertical velocity dispersion of the \ knots between 1 - 1.8 R_ 25 ( 13.5 - 23.2 kpc) is < 11 .

Here we present the face-on kinematics of one nearby galaxy, NGC 628 (M74), and compare to both neutral hydrogen and existing stellar measurements at smaller radii. NGC 628 is a prototypical Grand Design spiral galaxy type SA(s), dynamical mass within the studied region 3.3 11 M_ , assuming v_ rot = 200 \ out to 2.3 R_ 25 ; Thilker07, KB92 , and is by far the largest and most massive member of its small group (the brightest member after NGC 628, UGC1176, is 4.5 mag fainter and over 125 kpc away). NGC 628 shows a standard exponential optical light profile to R_ 25 , with only a very slight possible downbending in the profile to 1.3 R_ 25 , the extent of the deep optical observations Natali92 .

sources.summary: The paper finds M74's disk soft X-ray/K-band ratio is four times its bulge value, measures its grand-design arms separately, and constrains supersoft sources to a small SN Ia contribution across most stellar mass.

quotes.quote: To further investigate spiral galaxies we measured L_X/L_K ratios for their bulges and disks. The separation was made based on near-infrared images. We did not find very large difference -- the L_X/L_K ratios typically do not differ by more than a factor of 2 , except for M74 where the disk value is larger by a factor of 4 . There is a number of factors which may affect L_X/L_K ratios: the younger stellar population of the disk will tend to have higher L_X/L_K , whereas the more centrally concentrated hot X-ray emitting gas and the lower intrinsic absorption will increase its value in the bulge. Combination of these -- and possibly other -- effects may be responsible for observed scatter in L_X/L_K values.

Four galaxies in our sample (M51, M74 , M81, and M83) have grand design spiral arms allowing us to measure L_X/L_K ratios of spiral arms and interarm regions separately, obtained values are given in Table tab:lxlk . The arm and interarm regions were separated based on optical and near-infrared images. To our surprise, we did not find large difference between spirals arms and interarm regions, their L_X/L_K ratios being different by no more than 30 .

The plot reveals a large variations between galaxies in our sample. In several of them, M74, M81, M95 and NGC3184, supersoft sources cannot contribute more than 5-10 to the type Ia supernova rate in more than 80-90 of their mass. The other end of the range is represented by NGC2403 and NGC4449, in which supersoft sources could, in principle, be progenitors of a large fraction of type Ia supernovae without contradicting X-ray observations.

sources.summary: The paper studies H II regions in NGC 628, finding generally low arm/interarm efficiency but a possible star-formation triggering increase in one arm.

quotes.quote: Using H direct imaging, we characterize the H II regions from a sample of three grand design galaxies: NGC 5457, NGC 628 and NGC 6946. Broad band images in R and I were used to determine the position of the arms. The H II regions found to be associated with arms were selected for the study.

In Fig. epsi628 we have represented the relative efficiency as a function of the deprojected galactocentric radius for the two arms of NGC 628. For both arms, the efficiency is rather low (of the order of unity) along the entire length of the radius. However, for the SNS arm, in the bin with its centre at 3.5 kpc there is a relative large increase in the efficiency, indicating a possible triggering of star formation.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 628's far-infrared colors switch heating tracers between star-forming regions and older stellar light, and its maps show offset hot spiral structures.

quotes.quote: The 160/250 m colour temperature maps for about half the sample (NGC 628, 925, 2403, 3184, 3938, 4254, 4303, 4501, 4535, 4725, 5055, 5236, 5364, and 5457) appear more strongly associated with star forming regions than with the structures traced by the evolved stellar population.

In contrast, the 250/350 m colour temperatures in several galaxies (NGC 628, 2403, 3031, 3621, 3938, 3953, 5055, and 5457) trace structures that are similar to the emission seen at 3.6 m. We can clearly see that the colour temperatures are not enhanced in star forming regions that appear in the H maps in many of these galaxies. Instead, the colour temperatures tend to vary smoothly with radius. In the case of NGC 3031, 3621, and 3953, this applies to the 160/250 m ratios as well. In NGC 628, 2403, 3938, 5055, and 5457, however, the 160/250 m colours are strongly affected by star forming regions; this result along with the result for the 250/350 m ratios indicates that the transition from dust heated by star forming regions to dust heated by evolved stars occurs between 160 and 250 m for these galaxies.
We also sometimes see hot spiral structures offset from spiral arms in a few galaxies, most notably in the 160/250 m maps for NGC 628, 5236, and 6946.

sources.summary: For NGC 628, the paper compares mapped star-forming regions with supershells, finding leading-edge alignments inside corotation and a changed distribution near and beyond the corotation radius.

quotes.quote: In the case of NGC 628 (Fig. f:NGC628 ), HR15 map regions for two arms, 'A' and 'B', and inside corotation, supershells are indeed found close to and overlapping with the regions, preferentially at their leading edges. Especially for arm 'B', which is located in an rich part of the galaxy, the supershell locations relative to the regions change strikingly near the corotation radius: Inside, three prominent supershells lie towards the leading edge of the arm, extending over about a quarter of a turn. Only one small supershell is located at the trailing edge. From about the corotation radius outwards, the supershells are spread over the widening arm. None is clearly associated with the leading or trailing edges.
The fact that the effect we postulate is consistent with the data in NGC 628 is, however, encouraging.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 628 spiral arms, linking star-formation rates in individual regions to gas inflow velocities and comparing FUV, H-alpha, dust, and H I radial profiles.

quotes.quote: The spiral galaxy NGC 628 (M74) is one of the best studied nearby spiral galaxies viewed almost face-on (Fig. figure:map , Table table:param ). This prototypical grand-design galaxy hosts two principal arms. The first (South arm by rosales2011 Rosales-Ortega et al. 2011) is a long regular arm (Arm A in Fig. figure:map ).

Thirty brightest star formation regions in the ultraviolet band, located in the spiral arms of NGC 628, were identified and studied (see Fig. figure:map ). We found that the star formation regions in Arm A are systematically brighter and larger than the regions in Arm B gusev2014 (Gusev et al. 2014).
We examined the radial distribution of H i column density in NGC 628 based on the 21 cm line image obtained in .

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 628 as an LLAGN host, fitting multiple X-ray observations, finding stable flux and spectral shape, and setting a radio upper limit at its nucleus.

quotes.quote: NGC 628 is located at a distance (distances for all sources are presented in Table sec-spec_x_III ) of 10.2 Mpc 2014ApJ...792...52J . The source has been observed multiple times by both and between 2001 and 2013. Due to its proximity and small inclination 1984A&A...132...20S , NGC 628 has proven to be an ideal backdrop for the discovery of multiple extragalactic X-ray sources, in addition to its central engine e.g., 2002ApJ...572L..33S .

The source flux does not appear to vary significantly (again all values are consistent within 90 errors) between the different observations. The mean unabsorbed luminosity of the central source in the 0.5-10 keV range is 2.49 10^ 38 erg s ^ 1 . No radio source was detected at the position of NGC 628 in any of the sub-bands using weighting down to a root mean square (RMS) noise level of 8.4 Jy.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 628/M74 as a face-on isolated spiral, using JVLA and Effelsberg radio observations to map magnetic fields, Faraday-depth structures, Parker instabilities, and superbubble-driven disk-halo turbulence.

quotes.quote: We perform new observations of the nearby face-on spiral galaxy NGC 628 with the Karl G. Jansky Very Large Array (JVLA) at S-band (2.6--3.6 GHz effective bandwidth) and the Effelsberg 100-m telescope at frequencies of 2.6 GHz and 8.35 GHz with a bandwidth of 80 MHz and 1.1 GHz, respectively. Owing to the large bandwidth of the JVLA receiving system, we obtain some of the most sensitive radio continuum images in both total and linearly polarised intensity of any external galaxy observed so far. The application of RM Synthesis to the interferometric polarisation data over this large bandwidth provides high-quality images of Faraday depth and polarisation angle from which we obtained evidence for drivers of magnetic turbulence in the disk-halo connection. Such drivers include a superbubble detected via a significant Faraday depth gradient coinciding with a hole. We observe an azimuthal periodic pattern in Faraday depth with a pattern wavelength of 3.7 0.1 kpc, indicating Parker instabilities.
NGC 628 = M74 is a large, grand-design, isolated spiral galaxy with a low inclination angle and a disk with a diameter of about 30 , about three times the Holmberg diameter 1992A&A...253..335K .

sources.summary: The paper uses CHAOS H II-region data for NGC 628, finding decreasing softness-parameter trends, a characteristic ionization parameter, and hardening stellar radiation across its disk.

quotes.quote: All the compiled \ regions have \ equivalent widths larger than 10 \ in emission, which ensures they are mostly ionized by very young episodes of star formation cidfer10 Overall, we compiled information from 46 \ regions in NGC 628 [ 44 with a direct determination of 12+log(O/H) ] , 63 (29) in NGC 5194, and 102 (77) in NGC 5457. All these nearby star-forming disc galaxies present a spatial variation of Z and, at the same time, different values of the hardening of the ionizing field of radiation in different positions of the galaxies as already described in .

In this way NGC 628 has a characteristic log U value of -2.96, NGC 5194 has -3.11, and NGC 5457 has -2.56. Regarding T_* we observe radial variations consistent with those observed in the softness parameter, although the sign of the slope observed in cannot be always interpreted as radial variations of T_* in a determined sense. On one hand the two galaxies with clear decreasing slopes of the parameter, NGC 628 and NGC 5457, present at the same time very clear increasing slopes of T_* . Therefore in these two galaxies the observed variation of the softness parameter can be directly associated with a hardening of the stellar radiation across the galactic disc.

sources.summary: The paper applies ITGE to NGC 628 21-cm data, measuring power spectra for whole, inner, and outer regions of the nearly face-on spiral galaxy.

quotes.quote: We have applied the ITGE to estimate the C_ of  21-cm emission from different regions of the galaxy NGC 628. We find that the slope of the measured C_ in the outer region is significantly different as compared with the inner region. This indicates that the statistical properties of ISM turbulence possibly differ in different regions of the galaxy.

For our analysis, we use the interferometric  data of the galaxy NGC 628 from THINGS. NGC 628 is an almost face-on spiral galaxy with an average inclination angle of 15^ at a distance of 7.3 Mpc deblok08 . The  extent of the galaxy is 22.0^ ' 20.0^ ' .

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 628's resolved dust attenuation law, describing it as a nearby grand-design spiral whose median curve is steep, with a sub-Milky-Way UV bump and internal variations.

quotes.quote: In this work, we study the dust attenuation properties on spatially resolved scales of about 325 pc (or 7 ) in NGC 628 (or M74), a nearby ( D 9.59 Mpc , 2017ApJ...834..174K ) grand design spiral galaxy in the constellation Pisces. This galaxy has been classified as an SAc spiral galaxy with a stellar mass of log (M_*/M_ ) = 9.821 , a SFR = 1.07 M_ /yr and a metallicity of 12+ log (O/H)=8.80 2018arXiv180904088H . The main motivation to start with NGC 628 is that it is a well-studied and well-resolved galaxy, for which a lot of ancillary data (including deep SWIFT images, see below) is available.

We find that the median dust attenuation curve of NGC 628 is fairly steep, but not as steep as the SMC curve, and has a sub-MW-type UV bump. We observe intriguing variations within the galaxy, with regions of high A_ V exhibiting a shallower attenuation curve. We argue that the flattening of the curve is due to a dominance of absorption-over-scattering events at higher A_ V .

sources.summary: The paper analyzes star-cluster and H II-region offsets in NGC 628, deriving corotation radii, clockwise rotation, and evidence for coupled first and second spiral density-wave modes.

quotes.quote: A comparative analysis of the 30 brightest in the UV star-forming regions in the A and B arms (see Fig.  figure:map_NGC628 ) in NGC 628, carried out in our earlier paper Gusev2014 , shows that the star-forming regions in the spiral arm A are systematically brighter in the UV and in the H line, larger in size, higher in star formation rate, lower in metallicity, and relatively lower in age than the brightest star-forming regions in the B arm. Given this result, we have examined the behavior of the mean azimuthal offset in the ring zones as a function of galactocentric distance for three samples of objects. First, we plotted the average azimuthal offset versus galactocentric distance for all 503 SC--H ii R pairs.

That is, the substance in the disc of NGC 628 rotates in a clockwise direction. The circle itself with radius R = 7.0 1.0 kpc is the so-called corotation circle, where the rotational velocity of the matter in the disc coincides with the rotational velocity of the spiral pattern. The radial variation of criterion Eq.  equation:criterion_2 (case m=2 ), calculated using Fourier coefficients derived from analysis of the velocity field of the NGC 628, shows that at distance R_ C,m=2 = 6.2 1.0 kpc the spiral pattern rotation speed is equal to that of the disc (Fig.  figure:crit_2_NGC628 ).

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 628 has more small-scale metallicity inhomogeneity than the KT18 model predicts and O3N2-diagnostic behavior linked to an inverted central gradient.

quotes.quote: For other galaxies such as NGC 628, NGC 2835, and NGC 4535, the semivariogram is larger at the small-scale end than is predicted by the KT18 model, indicating that these galaxies contain more inhomogeneities on scales of 1 kpc than predicted by the model.

A more realistic treatment of turbulence may explain not only the normalisation discrepancy in the semivariograms between the KT18 model and observations, but also the increased power of small-scale correlations seen in Figure fig:theory_svgs for NGC 628 and NGC 2835.

For NGC 628 and NGC 4535, large fluctuations are seen in the empirical semivariogram computed using the O3N2 diagnostic. These fluctuations come from a failure of a simple linear metallicity gradient model to capture the large-scale behaviours of these galaxies. For NGC 628, using the O3N2 diagnostic, at distances larger than 2 kpc, the metallicity gradients inferred from the O3N2 diagnostic agree with those inferred from the Scal diagnostic. However, at smaller radii, the metallicity gradient is inverted.

sources.summary: The paper reports 173 NGC 628 globular-cluster candidates, notes previous HST work found only younger clusters, and includes it in incompleteness-corrected luminosity-function analysis.

quotes.quote: Our search, which surpasses the fractional area covered by all previous searches in these galaxies, has resulted in the detection of 158 GC candidates in M81, 1123 in M101, 226 in NGC 4258, 293 in M51 and 173 in NGC 628. The LFs constructed from this dataset, after correcting for relatively small contamination from reddened young clusters, are log-normal in nature, which was hitherto established only for the Milky Way (MW) and Andromeda among spiral galaxies.

NGC 628: The last galaxy of our sample, NGC 628, has also been a target for cluster searches using HST images by Ryon:2017 . However, they reported only young and intermediate-age clusters, and no GCs. We aim to study GC luminosity functions in our sample galaxies. In order to do that, we need to take into account possible sources of incompleteness in our catalogues.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 628 as a PHANGS-MUSE example grand-design spiral whose resolved SFR maps show the same FUV+W4 calibration trends as the sample.

quotes.quote: In Fig. fig:fig1 , we show SFR maps for three example galaxies from the PHANGS--MUSE sample. NGC 5068 is a low-mass galaxy and the closest object in our sample (15 = 400 pc). NGC 628 is an archetypal grand-design spiral galaxy, and finally NGC 1566 is a massive barred Seyfert 1 galaxy.

The comparison between \ and FUV+W4 SFR shows that deviations from equality are spatially correlated with the intensity of star formation activity. In particular, the FUV+W4 SFR is biased high in regions of low , while the trend is inverted for regions of high . Such a trend is evident in all three galaxies shown in Fig. fig:fig1 , despite clear differences in the \ distributions, and is also seen in the other PHANGS--MUSE galaxies in our sample.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 628 PHANGS-JWST imaging as resolving PAH and warm-dust ISM structure, star-forming regions, a background interloper near the center, strong spiral arms, and bubble/cavity analyses.

quotes.quote: It is clear that the increase in resolution (PSF FWHM 1 6 vs. 0 11), which now samples 5 pc at NGC 628 and 10 pc at NGC 7496, enables the study of individual star clusters and associations, and allows embedded stellar populations to be identified. A slightly extended point source southeast of the galactic center in NGC 628 is resolved into two background galaxies, and illustrates how JWST significantly improves the characterization of extended stellar structures and the identification of background ``interlopers.'' For NGC 628, the MIRI F770W and Spitzer/IRAC 8 m images are shown.
Figure fig:judy1 shows the filamentary organization of the gas in NGC 628, which prompted several initial investigations of ISM structure using PAHs as ISM tracers.

As an early demonstration of such analyses, WATKINS_PHANGSJWST create a bubble catalog for NGC 628, and examine their properties, including the size distribution for the ensemble population. BARNES_PHANGSJWST demonstrate the capabilities of the PHANGS multi-wavelength dataset for measuring the expansion rates and understanding the origins of large kpc-scale cavities, focusing on the ``Phantom Void'' in the southeast quadrant of NGC 628.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 628 as a moderate-mass grand design spiral whose JWST filaments are compared with disk-fragmentation scales using PHANGS gas and rotation data.

quotes.quote: JWST/MIRI imaging of the nearby galaxies IC 5332, NGC 628, NGC 1365 and NGC 7496 from PHANGS reveals a richness of gas structures that in each case form a quasi-regular network of interconnected filaments, shells and voids. IC 5332 is a flocculent dwarf spiral, NGC 628 is a moderate-mass grand design spiral, NGC 1365 is a massive, strongly-barred spiral galaxy, and NGC 7496 is a moderate-mass barred spiral LEE_PHANGSJWST .

In this paper we measure the spacing of features in the dusty webs of NGC 628, IC 5332, NGC 1365 and NGC 7496 imaged by PHANGS with JWST/MIRI and examine whether they are consistent with empirical estimates of the Toomre length, the turbulent Jeans length and the disk scale height constructed using PHANGS-ALMA CO(2-1) observations of each galaxy leroy21a . For NGC 628, NGC 1365, and NGC 7496, we adopt the rotation curve measured from the CO velocity field by as our estimate of the circular velocity V_c .

The filaments in NGC 628 and IC 5332 also tend to increase in both separation and length moving outwards in galactocentric radius. We thus measure either the azimuthal separation between filament features at a given radius -- in NGC 628 and IC 5332 -- or the radial separations between filaments at a given azimuth -- in NGC 1365 and NGC 7496.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 628 as a face-on spiral comparison target, fitting UV-to-submillimeter SINGS/KINGFISH photometry with Lightning, Prospector, and BAGPIPES to compare derived properties.

quotes.quote: To show how Lightning compares with other Bayesian sampling SED fitting codes, we fit the global broadband photometry of the face-on nearby spiral galaxy, NGC 628 using Lightning , Prospector https://github.com/bd-j/prospector , and BAGPIPES https://github.com/ACCarnall/bagpipes . These two comparison codes are by no means a complete sample of the other Bayesian SED fitting codes currently available. However, we have chosen to compare directly with Prospector and BAGPIPES due to their inclusion of similar models and algorithms (e.g., non-parametric SFHs and Bayesian sampling algorithms) as Lightning in the interest of a ``fair'' comparison.

From these distributions in the lower left, it can be seen that all three codes have derived parameters, except stellar mass, that are in excellent agreement. The variation in stellar mass between codes is expected and entirely due to the differences in SSPs and IMFs, which dictate the surviving stellar mass of the populations over time. As for the other parameters and SFHs, Lightning and Prospector have near identical results, which is a quality indicator given that both codes were run using almost identical models and were independently developed.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 628 (M74) as a nearly face-on Sc galaxy whose spiral arms show regular young-stellar distributions at characteristic scales around 400-800 pc.

quotes.quote: The objective of this study is to summarize the data regarding regularities in the distribution of young stellar populations and molecular clouds along the spiral arms and rings of galaxies of various types, These findings, obtained by our own research team as well as other scientific groups, will be compared with calculations based on theoretical models. studied the distribution of star-forming regions of various sizes and luminosities (star clusters, complexes, and HII regions) in the grand design galaxy Sc NGC 628 (M74; Fig. figure:fig1 The image of NGC 4321 in the figure obtained in was taken from the ned database; NGC 4324, from the SDSS survey database (http://www.sdss.org/dr13/) albareti2017 ; images of other galaxies are taken from the observations according to the author's programs. ), which is observed at a close distance of 7.2 Mpc according to . The galaxy is observed almost ''face-on'' (inclination i=7 according to and other sources).

The studies of in FUV, U , and H have shown that the young stellar population in both spiral arms of NGC 628 exhibits a regular distribution with a characteristic scale 400 pc, which is four times smaller than the estimates in for this galaxy. At the same time, large stellar complexes observed only in one of the arms of NGC 628 are indeed located at a distance of 4 =1.6 kpc from each other, while samples of smaller stellar groups (clusters and associations) are located at characteristic distances 2 =800 pc from each other in both spiral arms gusev2013 . It was also shown in that the presence or absence of shock waves does not affect the regularity in the distribution of the young stellar population. Signs of shock waves are only observed in one of the arms of NGC 628, where large stellar complexes are absent. However, a regular distribution of star-forming regions persists along both arms with the same characteristic distance. investigated the distribution of IR dust emission sources in the 8 m band, which represent regions of modern star formation with an age <1-2 Myr, in the slightly inclined ( i=24 according to the leda database http://leda.univ-lyon1.fr/ ) and relatively close (distance 16.2 Mpc according to the ned database http://ned.ipac.caltech.edu/ ) SABb-SABbc-type galaxy NGC 4321 (M100).

sources.summary: NGC 628 is modeled as a nearby face-on spiral whose South-East superbubble constrains disk gas scale height and midplane density through 3D simulations.

quotes.quote: NGC 628 is a late-type nearby face-on spiral galaxy at a distance of about 9.8 Mpc 2019ApJ...887...80K, 2021MNRAS.501.3621A . It was extensively observed in the past e.g. 2011MNRAS.410..313S,2015ApJ...815...93G and recently within the frame of the PHANGS (Physics at High Angular resolution in Nearby GalaxieS) survey e.g. 2021ApJS..257...43L, 2022A&A...659A.191E, 2022ApJS..258...10L , THINGS Walter2008 and the PHANGS - JWST Cycle I treasury program 2023ApJ...944L..17L . It presents a rich population of bubbles and holes whose radii vary from tens to thousand parsecs (see also 2020AJ....160...66P ).

The results of our 3D numerical simulations are confronted to the observed gas column density distribution in the largest South-East superbubble in the late-type spiral galaxy NGC 628. We make use of the star formation history inside the bubble derived from the resolved stellar population seen in the HST images to obtain its energy and demonstrate that the results of numerical simulations are in good agreement with the observed gas surface density distribution. We also show that the observed gas column density distribution constraints the gaseous disk scale height and the midplane gas density if the energy input rate could be obtained from observations.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC0628 for resolution tests, showing how smoothing from 12-712 pc blurs the separation between log-normal diffuse emission and power-law star-forming components.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: R.A. & Dec. table entry for NGC0628: 24.17 & 15.78

quotes.quote: Starting at the native F770W ( 0 269 ) for a representative target, NGC0628, we convolve the native image with Gaussian kernels to progressively lower angular resolutions following 2011ANIANO . We include convolutions to our lowest common native resolution at F2100W ( 0 674 ), our slightly coarser current working resolution at 0 85 , 4 0 comparable to the resolution of Spitzer at 8 and 24 m bands, 7 5 and 15 0 , comparable to the resolution of the WISE bands 3 and 4. In NGC0628, these angular resolutions correspond to physical resolutions of 12, 32, 40, 190, 356, and 712 pc respectively. With each successive smoothing, the separation between the log normal and power-law components of the PDF is blurred.

This is especially evident in Figure fig:resolution2100 that shows convolved images of NGC0628 at F2100W, where the power-law component is most prominent at native F2100W (32 pc resolution). At lower resolution ( 200 pc), the PDFs of H 2 and diffuse regions overlap and occupy the same region in intensity space.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 628 as a weakly interacting face-on galaxy with lopsided arms, trailing diffuse HI, and possible shock-produced diffuse gas.

quotes.quote: The weakly interacting, relatively face-on galaxies (NGC 628 and NGC 925) have neighbors, and show lopsided spiral arms, but neither nor optical arms link to their neighbors. In these two systems, the diffuse tends to follow the trailing side of the more open arm.

Secondly, tidal interactions drive spiral arms in galaxies, and produce the diffuse through shocks of gas running into arms. Possible candidates in support of this scenario include NGC 628 and NGC 925. They have lopsided spiral arms, and the diffuse tends to follow the trailing side of the more open arm which is likely rotating faster toward large radius than the other arm. Spiral arms in NGC 628 and NGC 925 have a pattern speed of 41.8 and 7.7 kpc ^ 1 respectively derived in the literature MartinezGarcia14, Elmegreen98 .

sources.summary: The paper includes M 74 among five galaxies with multiple spiral modes, consistent corotation-resonance distributions, available rotation curves, and short winding times.

quotes.quote: To investigate this aspect, we focus on five objects from our dataset that could have multiple spiral modes: IC 342, M 58, M 74, NGC 3583 and NGC 5371. The R_c distributions of these galaxies have consistent measurements and demonstrate the evident existence of several corotation resonances. In addition, the rotation velocity profiles for these objects are also publicly available.

Subsequently, we obtained rotation curve data v_c(R) for these five objects from the following sources:  Crosthwaite2000 (IC 342),  Lang2020 (M 58),  Walter2008 (M 74),  Noordermeer2005 (NGC 3583),  Sanders1996 (NGC 5371). Analogous phenomena have been observed in other selected galaxies as well. The figure illustrates that, for all the galaxies in our sample, the winding up process of the spiral structure takes approximately two or three galactic years.

sources.summary: The paper identifies M74 as the only nearby-galaxy match to an IPN short GRB, but treats the GRB 000420B association as uncertain and possibly coincidental.

quotes.quote: I find a single match of GRB 000420B with M74, which could, however, be due to a chance coincidence. The first, GRB 000420B, coincides with the position of the nearby Sc-type galaxy M74. Fig M74 shows a Galaxy Evolution Explorer (GALEX) near UV-band image, emphasizing the star-forming regions in M74, with the IPN annulus overlayed (solid lines).

Therefore, I cannot securely identify GRB 000420B with M74. Assuming that GRB 000420B is indeed an SGR flare in M74, the fraction of SGRs among short GRBs is about 3 ( = 1 47 _ SFR _ G _ V ). Currently, the best way to test the hypothesis that GRB 000420B arose in M74 is to detect additional SGR flares from this galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper detects a polarized incomplete outer ring in NGC 628, with strongest emission in two interarm regions, low intensity on the receding major axis, and highly ordered magnetic fields.

quotes.quote: NGC 628 (M74) This relatively face-on spiral galaxy shows substantial polarized emission in the form of an incomplete ring near the edge of the optical disk which is brightest at PA 80^ 340^ (Position Angles measured east of north). The minimum in polarized intensity occurs at the PA of the receding major axis (PA = 25 ^ , as tabulated in Table table:summary ). The brightest emission is associated with two inter-arm regions in the outer galaxy; one extending from PA 80 ^ to 210 ^ and the other from PA 200 ^ to 340 ^ . These seem to be continuations into the outer disk of the inner disk interarm regions. The polarized fraction at in these regions is approximately 10--20 at small radii; increasing to 40--50 at the largest radii, indicating an exceptionally well-ordered magnetic field.

sources.summary: NGC 628 is used as an undisturbed spiral comparison; its three channel-range power spectra share slope 1.6, including outer-region HI, unlike NGC 4254.

quotes.quote: To verify that this change in slope is due to harrassment, we also consider the HI power spectrum of the spiral galaies NGC 628 and NGC 1058 DBBC08,DBBC09b which are not undergoing galaxy harassment. Figure fig:DEFM shows the regions of the spiral galaxy NGC 628 corresponding to each of the channel range 108-119 (D), 120-131 (E) and 132-143 (F). We find that the power spectra of these three data cubes (Figure  fig:628PS ) all have the same slope 1.6 , which is also similar to the slope of the central part of NGC 4254.

However, as seen in Figure  fig:DEFM for NGC 628, all three data cubes D, E and F have a significant contribution of HI from the outer region of NGC 628, leading us to believe that the power spectrum has a slope of −1.6 in the outer parts of NGC 628. This is similar to the value of slope seen in the central parts of NGC 4254 and significantly different from the slope in the outer parts of NGC 4254.

sources.summary: The paper describes M74 as a nearby Sc spiral, the brightest member of its group, with structured arms, dust lanes, recent supernovae, and newly identified optical supernova-remnant candidates.

quotes.quote: M74 (NGC 628) is an Sc type spiral galaxy with a 6 ^ inclination angle at an assumed distance of 7.3 Mpc (Sharina et al.1996, Soria et al. 2004). It is the brightest member of the small M74 group in the Pisces constellation. M74 has spiral arms well formed with bright blue star clusters and dark cosmic dust lanes. In the early 2000, two supernovae (SN 2002ap and SN 2003gd) were identified in M74 and studied extensively.
We have identified nine new SNR candidates in M74 with [ S II ] /H 0.4 as the basic criterion.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 628 has a young stellar component affecting V-band light and optical-NIR colors, with TP-AGB-rich models better matching the observations.

quotes.quote: As we are interested in predictions from models that use different treatments of the TP-AGB phase, we focus on two galaxies from our spectroscopic sample (NGC 628 UGC 2124) with the highest quality data and whose derived SFHs indicate significant contribution (in V band light-weight) from a 1 Gyr SP, \ where the TP-AGB NIR signature is expected to be strong.

The contribution to the V band normalized light of the young population of interest is 67 and 33 for NGC 628 and UGC 2124, respectively. Thus, if our SFHs are reliable, according to the Mar05 models, these galaxies would be expected to show signs of the TP-AGB NIR excess Note that we did not perform the population synthesis fits with the Mar05 models as they have a lower resolution than BC03, thus reducing the predictive power of the spectra to disentangle the high frequency features in the fitting process.

Given the above caveats, we now look at the observed versus predicted optical--NIR colors for NGC 628 in Figure fig:colorcolor . In all color combinations, the model fits (red open triangles) predict colors that are too blue compared to the observed (black solid triangles) optical--NIR colors.

sources.summary: The paper examines NGC 628 for angular offsets among spiral-arm tracers, finding only suggestive trends and offsets that are sensitive to fitting choices.

quotes.quote: Of these galaxies, only NGC 628 presents any trend suggestive of the picture of R69. However, it is important to note that many of these offsets are well below the resolution of the images, especially for the case of H _ 2 , which only has 13 '' resolution. We also tried other combinations of these images and divided the images into quadrants to isolate individual arms.

We carried out a careful comparison of NGC 628 also studied by T08. While we were able to reproduce some of their offsets, the offsets measured were sensitive to small changes in the polynomial fit and the way in which the cross-correlation function is calculated ( i.e. use of mean or median for x and y in Eq. ccl ).

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 628 IFU spectroscopy and resolved gas maps to test optical gas-column estimates, selecting regions across the galaxy and comparing model outputs with mapped gas.

quotes.quote: We will also make use of the massive Integral Field Spectroscopy (IFS) mosaic of NGC 628 published by 2011MNRAS.410..313S as part of the PINGS project http://www.ast.cam.ac.uk/ioa/research/pings/html/index.html . This was obtained with the PPAK spectrograph on the 3.5m telescope at Calar Alto. We use the reduced data provided on the PINGS project page and selected a total of 87 regions across the galaxy (see Fig. fig:ngc628-illustration-of-regions ).

To ensure we have a consistent alignment of the images, we tie the IFU mosaic world coordinate system to that of the \ image of NGC 628 provided by the SINGS survey 2003PASP..115..928K . We will also make use of resolved \ maps from the THINGS survey 2008AJ....136.2563W as well as \ maps from the BIMA SONG Downloaded from http://nedwww.ipac.caltech.edu/level5/March02/SONG/SONG.html 2003ApJS..145..259H , Nobeyama Downloaded from http://www.nro.nao.ac.jp/ nro45mrt/COatlas/ 2007PASJ...59..117K and HERACLES 2009AJ....137.4670L Downloaded from http://www.cv.nrao.edu/ aleroy/HERACLES/Overview.html surveys --- we use HERACLES data whenever possible but use the other data sets for reference.

sources.summary: The paper uses SN 2013ej and SN 2002ap to estimate M74 distance, finds negligible reddening from M74, and adopts a final EPM distance near 9 Mpc.

quotes.quote: From the lack of narrow Na I D lines from the host galaxy, showed that the reddening from M74 in the direction toward SN 2013ej is negligible. No evidence of Na I D lines from the host was seen in spectra of and in our own sample. Thus, we do not consider any host extinction.

Recently, the distance to the SN 2013ej host, M74, was subjected to a number of measurements, from the value of D 7 2 Mpc sharina96, vinko04, vandyk06 to D 9.5 0.5 Mpc zasov96, oliva10 ; see Table tab:m74dist for a summary.
By performing an EPM analysis of SN 2013ej, in combination with SN 2002ap, we estimated the distance to the host galaxy M74 to be 9.0_ 0.6 ^ +0.4 Mpc.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 628 as a nearby face-on SAc spiral in Pisces, useful for multi-wavelength studies and notable for hosting three core-collapse supernovae.

quotes.quote: NGC 628 and its observed CCSNe NGC 628 (also known as Messier 74; M 74) is an interesting nearby galaxy situated in the Pisces constellation. It is a face-on spiral galaxy (prototype SAc) with prominent spiral arms and dust lanes. In the sky, NGC 628 is located far away from the Galactic disk and therefore, is a natural target for the multi-wavelength observations cf. and references therein 2002ApJ...572L..33S, 2005ApJ...630..228K, 2006MNRAS.371.1617A, 1994ApJ...426..553C,2005ApJS..159..214K . The study of star formation scenario and supernova remnants in this galaxy are also available in literature e.g. 2006ApJ...644..879E, 2000AJ....120.1306L, 2010A&A...517A..91S . Within a distance of 10 Mpc for latest distance estimation, see and references therein 2014ApJ...792...52J,2015ApJ...807...59H , NGC 628 is one of the nearby galaxies which interestingly hosted three CCSNe (i.e. SN 2002ap, SN 2003gd and SN 2013ej).

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 0628 among isolated unbarred spirals with shallow metallicity gradients, and finds its VENGA radial profiles match prior PINGS measurements.

quotes.quote: The final division of our sample yields two barred isolated galaxies (NGC 2903 5713), three unbarred isolated galaxies (NGC 0628, 3938, 4254), one barred weakly interacting galaxy (NGC 0337 There is some evidence that NGC 0337 might be undergoing an interaction or minor merger. See notes for column 8 in Table tab:venga-subsample . ), and one unbarred weakly interacting galaxy (NGC 5194).
The three isolated unbarred galaxies (NGC 0628, 3938, 4254) also show similarly shallow gradients of -0.44 0.13 in units of dex ( R / ) ^ 1 and -0.034 0.008 in units of dex kpc ^ 1 .

For NGC 0628, this galaxy was observed for the PINGS survey by . Their radial profiles appear qualitatively similar to our own. Correcting for the assumed distance to NGC 0628, find a fibre-to-fibre gradient using the R_ 23 KK04 diagnostic of 0.031 0.003 dex kpc ^ 1 which is nearly identical to our gradient of 0.031 0.001 dex kpc ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 628 as a face-on grand-design spiral case where young-cluster clustering declines with age and associations disappear rapidly.

quotes.quote: NGC 628 is a morphological type SAc face-on grand design spiral galaxy located at a distance of 9.9 Mpc with no apparent bulge. The largest galaxy of the M74 galaxy group with an angular size of 10 5 x 9 5, a central and east pointing were obtained. The clustering of the young stellar clusters in this galaxy was investigated in an earlier paper by grasha15 , where an age dependency for the clustering strength was found with a randomization time scale of 40 Myr, after which the clusters displayed a flatter, more homogenous distribution.
NGC 628 and NGC 1566 also exhibit a substantial decrease in clustering with increasing cluster ages.

Recent work on the number densities of the young stellar clusters in NGC 628 by adamo17 also shows a rapid decline in class 3 associations, disappearing on time scales of 50 Myr, comparable to those of hierarchically structured star-forming regions that we find in this study.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 628 cluster ages and separations, finding mass-cut effects within errors and stochastic age modeling consistent with deterministic results.

quotes.quote: We repeat the analysis in Section sec:delt after applying different mass cuts for the galaxies with enough statistics to do a proper study: NGC 1313, NGC 5194, NGC 628, and NGC 1566 to see if we see a trend for an increase in the age accompanying increases in the mass cut. Figure fig:masscut shows negligible increase in the maximum age of the t - R relation, about 10 or less, which is well within the errors. We do note that NGC 628 does show a higher increase in R_ max at the largest mass cut, about 25 . However, this is still within the error bars, and, as we show in Section sec:tests , the t - R correlation for NGC 628 disappears when we randomize the clusters positions and ages, demonstrating that the correlation is not an effect of selection biases.

We utilize the full posterior PDFs of the cluster candidates in NGC 7793 and NGC 628 to investigate the implications on the t - R relation of deriving the ages stochastically compared to traditionally deterministic SED modeling.
There is a slight decrease in the average age separations for clusters with properties derived stochastically within NGC 7793 and no difference is observed within NGC 628.

sources.summary: The paper maps NGC 628 with ALMA and MUSE at 50 pc scales, finding offset molecular clouds and H II regions, weak cloud-scale correlation, and long depletion times.

quotes.quote: We present first results from these ALMA and MUSE surveys, comparing star formation and molecular gas in NGC 628 at 50  pc resolution. This galaxy is a nearby ( 9.6 Mpc, Kreckel2017 ) face-on ( i=9^ , Blanc2013 ) type SAc grand-design spiral galaxy with a moderate star formation rate ( 2.4 M _ yr ^ 1 ; Sanchez2011 ).
Our ALMA observations map CO (2-1) line emission across the central 3 4 ( 8.5 11.3 kpc ^2 ) star-forming disk (Figure  fig:maps ).

This is apparent in NGC 628 (Figure  fig:maps ) by the clear displacement of the H and CO emission due to time evolution combined with the motion of the spiral pattern around the disk Schinnerer2017 . Figure  fig:scls explores how the inferred depletion times and their scatter change over 50 500  pc scales. Individual data points show the surface density in (Nyquist sampled) apertures detected in both tracers uniformly sampling the emission maps at S/N >3 . The inset histograms show that a significant number of regions (50-75 ) are detected in only one tracer. We measure median gas depletion times of 1 3 Gyr, consistent with what was previously found for NGC 628 on 400  pc Rebolledo2015 and 750  pc Bigiel2008, Leroy2008, Leroy2013 scales.
