sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 628 as a paradigmatic velocity-map case where the inner and outer kinematic axes differ, suggesting rotational inner motions and radial outer motions.

quotes.quote: For example, the case of NGC 628 appears as a paradigmatic case in which the kinematic axis of the inner region is almost orthogonal to that of the outer region. Correspondingly the position angle of the RTRM shows a large variation, almost a step function behavior, as a function of radius. As discussed above (see Fig. toymodels_rotrad ), such a situation is compatible with the presence of two kinds of velocity fields, namely rotational in the inner part and radial in the outer part.

For example, in the case of NGC 628 at about 270 arcsec the position angle makes a step from a value of about 20^ to 90^ (see Fig. ngc628 ). We thus obtain that the inner part is very well fitted by a RDM while the outer part can be fitted by a REM with a reasonably good accuracy (see Fig. ngc628b ) although the RTRM fit still provides a better fit.

sources.summary: The survey paper uses NGC 628 commissioning data to quantify H-region detection limits, line-detection fractions, and its worst-case distance for SIGNALS targets.

quotes.quote: In the commissioning data of NGC 628, located at 9 Mpc, our measured detection threshold for H was of 4.6 10 ^ 17 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 arcsec ^ 2 (slightly lower than for SIGNALS). Figure SB_plot shows the measured peaks' SB function (histogram of the SB of the central pixel) of the regions detected with the identification procedure ( Rousseau-Nepton2018 ) for different subsets of regions on NGC 628 for which there is a detection of a different strong line. From the regions detected with H , 84 were also detected in [ N ] 6583, 91 in [ S ] 6716+6731, 63 in [ O ] 5007, 84 in H , 63 in [ O ] 5007, and 63 in [ O ] 3727. Part of the lower detection of the [ O ] 3727 line is due to dust extinction, a total exposure time of 2.13 hours (lower than for SIGNALS), and the loss of reflectivity of the telescope primary mirror (only a few months before realuminizing). For [ O ] 5007, about 5 of the regions were at a metallicity too high to be detected and, as mentioned before in this section, the other non-detected regions could be older than 5 Myr and/or they have a low ionization parameter. From the distance of NGC 628 to the closest target of the sample, the fraction of detected lines increases as more pixels are available to extract the spectra. NGC 628 is therefore a worst-case scenario for the detection of the lines.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC0628 as a nearby face-on spiral with resolved main-sequence, HI, and H2 maps, finding depressed central HI and concentrated molecular gas.

quotes.quote: This work is based on multiwavelength observations of five nearby face-on spiral galaxies: NGC0628, NGC3184, NGC5194, NGC5457, and NGC6946.

The left panel of Fig. fig:0628 shows, as an example, the _ MS map of NGC0628; cells in red are located below the spatially resolved MS, while the ones in blue are located above the relation (the _ MS maps of the other galaxies in the sample are shown in Appendix sec:a0 ). Within the optical radius (the dashed circle) we are able to recover most of the cells, especially at <0.9 R _ 25 R _ 25 is defined as the length of the projected semi-major axis of a galaxy at the isophotal level 25 mag/arcsec ^2 in the B-band and it is taken from the HyperLEDA data base 2014A&A...570A..13M . .

The central panel of Fig. fig:0628 shows the distribution of _ HI in NGC0628. As in several other spiral galaxies, the HI is centrally depressed e.g. 2017A&A...605A..18C , and it extends on radius that are significantly larger that the optical radius 2002A&A...390..829S, 2013MNRAS.433..270W .

sources.summary: The paper applies LBT-SONG diffuse satellite searches to isolated star-forming host NGC 628, measuring completeness and finding two candidate dwarf satellites consistent with CDM expectations.

quotes.quote: We perform this analysis on NGC 628, an isolated star-forming host with 1/4 of the stellar mass of the MW and the most distant host in our survey (9.77 Mpc). We present our completeness results for this system. Notably, we are 88 complete to old, 10 Gyr, system with M _ V =-9 and _ eff = 25 mag/ '' , as well as over 60 complete to old (10 Gyr) systems with M _ V between 7 and 9 and _ eff = 26 and 27 mag/ '' .

We found 2 satellite galaxy candidates, NGC 628 dwA and NGC 628 dwB with M _ V = -12.2 and M _ V = -7.7 , respectively. Folding in our two candidates and completeness results, the derived luminosity function for this host is in line with CDM expectations.

sources.summary: The paper studies CO line observations at 20 positions across NGC 0628, comparing UV-bright and UV-dim regions to infer molecular gas conditions and star-formation efficiency.

quotes.quote: In this study, we investigate the physical properties of giant molecular clouds (GMCs) using multiple lines of CO, i.e. CO(1--0, 2--1, 3--2) and ^ 13 CO(1--0), obtained at selected 20 positions in the disc of NGC 0628. Eleven positions were selected over the radial cut, including the centre, and remaining nine positions were selected across the southern and northern arms of the galaxy. 13 out of 20 positions are brighter at 24 and ultraviolet (UV) emission and hosting significantly more H II regions compared to the rest of the positions indicating opposite characteristics.

NGC 0628 (M74) is a face-on nearby spiral galaxy ( 7.3 Mpc; kar04 ) and is well studied in both H and far-ultraviolet (FUV) emission adl99,her10 , both indicating intense star formation activity in the arms. In total, we observed 20 selected positions throughout the disc of NGC 0628 in ^ 12 CO(1--0) and ^ 13 CO(1--0) lines.

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 628 through radial abundance analysis, reporting a T_e-based N/O profile break without a T_e O/H break and comparing its temperature-relation behavior with earlier work.

quotes.quote: For four of the galaxies with T_e based abundance profiles (NGC 628, NGC 3621, NGC 5154 and NGC 5457) we could attempt a double linear fit, that results in a significant ( _ ^2 improvement >10 ) break in the (N/O) radial profile.

In the case of NGC 628 and NGC 5457 (M101), a break was detected in the radial (N/O) profile that was not seen in 12+ (O/H). All the galaxies in this sample presenting radial breaks in the 12+ (O/H) profile, with one or several methods, also show a break in the (N/O) profile with either HCM, or the R calibration or with both of them, at approximately the same galactocentric radius. It is also interesting to note that for the four galaxies in which a radial break is detected in the (N/O) profile derived from the T _e based abundances (NGC 628, NGC 3621, NGC 5194 and NGC 5457), such break is not detected in the T _e based 12+ (O/H) profile.
Berg15 observe an increasing dispersion in the T [ ] T [ ] relationship with decreasing temperature for regions in NGC 628 and M101, that is not observed in our derived relations.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 628 as the high-metallicity comparison galaxy, finding its selected star-forming regions are generally larger than those in the peculiar galaxies.

quotes.quote: In this paper, for comparison with our previous results, we consider the high-metallicity galaxy NGC 628. It is further called the comparison galaxy, while the galaxies NGC 660, NGC1512, NGC 4395 and NGC 4618 are collectively called new galaxies.

The largest SFRs ( 1 kpc) are observed in the galaxies NGC 628 and NGC 660 (both in the disk and in the ring), while in the interacting galaxies the size of the SFRs is significantly smaller ( 700 pc). What was said above about the angular sizes of the central and peripheral SFRs in the galaxy NGC 628, is obviously true for their linear sizes. It should be clarified that the almost complete absence of identified SFRs in NGC 628, which are comparable in size to the SFRs in the galaxies NGC 4395 and NGC 4618, may be a selection effect.

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC628 in its 2 kpc resolved D/M analysis, reports a median D/M of 0.43, and notes small measured CO line-ratio variations.

quotes.quote: We investigate the relationship between the dust-to-metals ratio (D/M) and the local interstellar medium environment at 2 kpc resolution in five nearby galaxies: IC342, M31, M33, M101, and NGC628. A modified blackbody model with a broken power-law emissivity is used to model the dust emission from 100 to 500 observed by Herschel . We utilize the metallicity gradient derived from auroral line measurements in regions whenever possible.

The median D/M and the 16-/84-percentile in each galaxy are 0.41^ +0.11 _ 0.05 , 0.50^ +0.11 _ 0.06 , 0.60^ +0.05 _ 0.06 , 0.48^ +0.06 _ 0.05 and 0.43^ +0.03 _ 0.04 for IC342, M31, M33, M101 and NGC628, respectively.
In M33 and NGC628, the measured variations of 21 are reasonably small SANDSTROM13,DRUARD14 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 628 as a face-on grand-design spiral with significant UV-traced disk star formation, self-regulated star-forming complexes, bright central radio continuum, and an asymmetric extended disk.

quotes.quote: NGC 628 NGC 628 (M 74 or the Phantom Galaxy) is a face-on disk galaxy located at a distance of 7.3 Mpc with inclination 7 ^ . It is a grand design spiral with class 9 type spiral arms Mulcahy2017, Elmegreen1984 . According to Elmegreen1984 , the arm classes 1 to 4 correspond to flocculent spiral arms, and type 5 to 12 correspond to classic grand design spiral structure. Cornett1994 calculated the surface brightness and color profiles for this galaxy in the UV regime and concluded that the disk has significant star formation over the last 500 million years. Zaragoza2019 analyzed the stellar population in NGC 628 using Multi Unit Spectroscopic Explorer (MUSE) optical spectra and found that the spatially resolved SFCs in NGC 628 agree with models of self-regulated star formation.

sources.summary: The PHANGS-HST methods paper uses NGC 628 to illustrate source detection and MCI selection, comparing its cluster candidates against LEGUS in central and eastern fields.

quotes.quote: The familiar face-on disk galaxy NGC 628 (M 74) is used for illustration of our methods in Secs. sec:detect - sec:classification . In order to assess the degree to which DOLPHOT is effective at recovering clusters for our PHANGS-HST distance range, we match the DOLPHOT catalogue against the LEGUS Class 1 and 2 cluster population for NGC 628 Adamo2017 . We find that 98 of LEGUS clusters have a DOLPHOT source within 2 pixels, and for 80 the closest match is within 1 pixel.

In the left panel of Fig. fig:MCIplane we show the distribution of measured MCI _ in versus MCI _ out values for all sources with S/N 10 in our NGC 628-C field (see Sec. sec:results for a description of these data). Figure fig:MCImodelregions shows such a plot for NGC 628-C, and confirms that only a handful of clusters confirmed by LEGUS lie outside our semi-empirical selection region, and only two are missed by our model cluster regions (others may possibly fail general conditions of S/N , of low error bands, etc.).

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 628 as a face-on spiral with magnetic arms and vertical fields, deriving hot-gas and magnetic properties and suggesting halo reconnection heating.

quotes.quote: We search for possible signatures of gas heating by magnetic reconnection effects in the radio and X-ray data for the face-on spiral galaxy NGC 628 (M 74), which presents pronounced magnetic arms and evidence for vertical magnetic fields. The strengths and energy densities of the magnetic field in the spiral and magnetic arms were derived, as were the temperatures and thermal energy densities of the hot gas, for the disk and halo emission. In the regions of magnetic arms, higher order and lower energy density of the magnetic field is found than in the stellar spiral arms. The global temperature of the hot gas is roughly constant throughout the disk. The comparison of the findings with those obtained for the starburst galaxy M 83 suggests that magnetic reconnection heating may be present in the halo of NGC 628.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 628 as an isolated face-on Sc spiral with a large H I disk, optical radius, and recent TRGB-based distance estimates.

quotes.quote: The nearby galaxy NGC 628 (M 74, the Phantom Galaxy) is an isolated Sc spiral galaxy (morphological type code T = 5.2 0.5). NGC 628 is a face-on galaxy, its inclination angle is i = 6 and the position angle of the major axis PA = 25 Kamphuis1992 . The optical radius of this galaxy is R _ 25 = 5.24 arcmin or 314.1 arcsec RC3 . The H i disk extends out to more than three times the optical radius Kamphuis1992 . There are recent distance estimations for NGC 628 through the tip of the red giant branch (TRGB) method based on the Hubble Space Telescope measurements.

sources.summary: The paper uses JWST MIRI, ALMA CO, and H maps of NGC 628 to resolve star-forming regions and measure embedded-star-formation and GMC inert-phase timescales in the galaxy.

quotes.quote: In this Letter, we demonstrate this by measuring the duration of the embedded phase of star formation and the implied time over which molecular clouds remain inert in the galaxy NGC 628 at a distance of 9.8 Mpc, demonstrating that the cosmic volume where this measurement can be made has increased by a factor of >100 compared to Spitzer .

In this Letter, we extend our previous analysis by kim21 by characterizing the duration of the early phase of star formation in one of these initial galaxies, NGC 628, which is the most nearby (yet 3 times further away than the most distant galaxy analyzed in kim21 ), and for which the duration of the CO- and H bright phases have already been obtained in our previous works chevance20, kim22 .

Using novel observations of NGC 628 at a wavelength of 21 m from MIRI on JWST, together with CO from ALMA and narrow-band H emission maps at matched resolution, we have characterized the evolutionary cycle of GMCs from their inert phase, to the onset of embedded massive star formation, the partially exposed star-forming phase, and finally to H ii regions free of cold molecular gas. This is the first time that the start and the duration of the embedded phase of star formation can be characterized at a distance greater than 3.5 Mpc, unlocking the necessary statistics and dynamic range for characterizing the environmental dependence of the physical processes driving the earliest phases of massive star formation. We find that the time during which GMCs in NGC 628 are truly free from massive star formation ( =t_ CO t_ fb, 21 m ) is 18.8_ 3.6 ^ +2.7 Myr.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 628 as nearby, face-on, star-forming Messier 74/The Phantom Galaxy, whose PHANGS observations reveal pervasive feedback-driven bubbles across its main disk.

quotes.quote: In a companion paper in this issue, used a combination of \ and \ observations to study the bubble population across the nearby (9.84 0.63 Mpc; Anand2021a, Anand2021b ), star-forming (1.8 0.5 yr ^ 1 ; ), face-on ( i 9 ^ ; Lang2020 and also see Blanc2013 ), massive ( M_* =10 ^ 10.3 , M_ HI =10 ^ 9.7 , M_ H2 =10 ^ 9.4 ; Walter2008, Querejeta2015, Leroy2019, Leroy2021a_survey ), spiral galaxy Messier 74 (also known as NGC 628 and The Phantom Galaxy).

These authors manually identify bubbles using a combination of 7.7 \ MIRI observations LEE_PHANGSJWST , B band (438 nm) PHANGS– \ observations Lee2021 , and VLT-MUSE \ observations Emsellem2022 . For each bubble, WATKINS_PHANGSJWST fit circular or elliptical apertures to the bubble boundaries (i.e. the shells) seen within this multi-wavelength dataset, and in doing so identify 1700 bubbles with radii ranging between 6 and 500 pc (e.g. left panel of Fig. fig2 ). These structures are clearly pervasive across the galaxy, form a complex nested structure (where smaller bubbles are preferentially located at the edges of large bubbles) , and are among the most striking features in the initial \ images (see WATKINS_PHANGSJWST for a in-depth discussion of the nesting of these structures and their size distributions).

The observations targeting NGC 628 cover the main star-forming disk (containing 50 of the total star formation of the galaxy), which is matched to coverage from Hubble Lee2021 , VLT-MUSE Emsellem2022 , and ALMA Leroy2021a_survey .

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 0628 with PHANGS-JWST MIRI and NIRCam data, cataloging 502 compact 21 micron sources and comparing source behavior to lifecycle modeling.

quotes.quote: We analyze MIRI data from four galaxies in the PHANGS-JWST survey described by LEE_PHANGSJWST : IC 5332, NGC 0628, NGC 1365 and NGC 7496 1 with properties summarized in Table tab:props . The data presented in this paper were obtained from the Mikulski Archive for Space Telescopes (MAST) at the Space Telescope Science Institute The specific observations analyzed can be accessed via 10.17909/9bdf-jn24 http://dx.doi.org/10.17909/9bdf-jn24 . We focus our analysis on the sources detected in the MIRI 21 data (F2100W filter, FWHM: 0 67 ) though we consider all data from the PHANGS-JWST filter set, which includes NIRCam (F200W, F300M, F335M, F360M) for NGC 0628, NGC 1365 and NGC 7496, and MIRI (F770W, F1000W, F1130W, and F2100W) for all four targets IC 5332 NIRCam observations have not been obtained yet. .
Our final catalog consists of 1271 compact sources with 188 in IC 5332, 502 in NGC 0628, 367 in NGC 1365 and 214 in NGC 7496.

This result is similar to the results of kim21 and KIM_PHANGSJWST , 2 who modeled the lifecycle of a typical star forming region in NGC 0628 and other nearby galaxies based on the location and scale dependence of the CO-to-21 and H to-21 ratios. They found that the period of time when a region emits bright 21 emission only slightly precedes the time when it emits bright H (specifically they inferred regions to be H bright for 70 of the total time they emitted bright 21 ), which agrees well with our observations.

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 0628 among the first PHANGS-JWST targets, comparing its mid-infrared maps with CO and H data and using CO velocity-field information for disk emission analysis.

quotes.quote: We study the first four PHANGS-JWST targets: NGC 628, NGC 1365, NGC 7496, and IC 5332 and compare mid-IR emission at 7.7 m, 10 m, 11.3 m, and 21 m obtained as part of the PHANGS-JWST survey PHANGSJWST22 to CO (2-1) observed by ALMA as part of the PHANGS--ALMA survey PHANGSALMA21 and extinction-corrected H obtained as part of the PHANGS--MUSE survey PHANGSMUSE22 . For this comparison, we work with all data sets at a common angular resolution of =1.7'' .

For NGC 0628, 1365, and 7496 we use the low resolution velocity field derived from the CO as a reference, filling in with a predicted local velocity from the rotation curve in the few regions without a detection. After adjusting the cube so that all emission is centered in roughly the same channel, we integrate over a fixed velocity window picked to encompass all readily detected emission in the disk ( v =25 , 35 , 80 , and 55 km s ^ 1 for IC 5332, NGC 0628, 1365, and 7496). The comparison includes Spitzer 8 m and 24 m data, which are available for IC 5332, NGC 0628, and NGC 1365 but not NGC 7496, and WISE 12 m data, which are available for all targets.

sources.summary: The paper uses LEGUS photometry to infer NGC 628 cluster demographics, finding a low-mass truncation, mass-independent disruption, and possible but unconvincing radial variation.

quotes.quote: Here infer the demographics of the cluster population in the benchmark galaxy NGC 628 using data from the Legacy Extra-galactic UV Survey (LEGUS) coupled to a novel Bayesian forward-modelling technique. Our method allows analysis of a total of \ clusters in the LEGUS catalogue, roughly a factor of 4 more than previous studies that required severe completeness cuts to the data. Our results indicate that the cluster mass function is truncated at 10^4 M _ , consistent with proposed relations between truncation mass and star formation surface density. We find that cluster disruption begins early, 10 Myr after formation, but that it is relatively mild, with clusters requiring on average 2-3 times their present age to disrupt; we do not find any evidence for mass dependent disruption. We also do not find convincing evidence for any radial variations in these conclusions, though we find suggestive hints that inner galaxy clusters may be more prone to disruption.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 628 as a nearby spiral with enough star formation, many clusters, IFU data, and YSC catalogs spanning 1 Myr to 3 Gyr.

quotes.quote: To demonstrate and apply our search strategies for SMS, we focus on massive star clusters in the nearby galaxies NGC 628 and M83, for the following main reasons: 1) Both of them are spiral galaxies with ``sufficient'' star formation rates, such that young star clusters are still forming in these galaxies. 2) They are relatively well-studied and multi-band cluster catalogs are available. 3) Both galaxies host a large number of star clusters to search for SMS. 4) Spectroscopic IFU observations are also available for these galaxies.
The young star cluster (YSC) catalogs of NGC628 span an age range of 1 Myr to 3 Gyr with a median age around 14 Myr and a mass range of 433 \ to 3.2 10^ 6 \ with a median mass around 4.5 10^ 3 .

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 628 as a face-on grand-design spiral with integrated SED fits that recover a shallower dust attenuation curve than previous pixel-by-pixel and global CIGALE work.

quotes.quote: NGC 628 is a face-on grand design spiral galaxy. Decleir2019 did pixel-by-pixel as well as global (i.e. integrated flux) SED fitting using CIGALE and found a global attenuation law with =-0.55 0.15 and B=2.24 1.16 . Here, we find between -0.08 and -0.33 and E_b between 2.27 and 2.89. Our SFHs do not match as Decleir2019 used a delayed exponential SFH with added parameters to handle recent quenching or bursts of star formation. However, our derived slopes are shallower with our exponential SFH leading to a much shallower attenuation curve.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 628 as a nearby Sc-type, nearly face-on spiral group member with well-defined spiral arms and no recent interaction in the past gigayear.

quotes.quote: NGC 628 NGC 628 is an Sc-type galaxy with an inclination of 9^ , and at a redshift of z 0.00219, which corresponds to a distance of 9.8 Mpc Anand2021 . NGC 628 is the most prominent member of a small group of galaxies. The group is centered on NGC 628 and the peculiar spiral NGC 660. Two well-defined spiral arms observed in optical and UV images make NGC 628 a typical example of grand design spirals. NGC 628 has not gone through any recent interactions in the past 1 Gyr Kamphuis1992 .

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 628 in the high recent-SF-change group, with large areas of increasing recent star formation, a headlight-cloud comparison, and isolation from recent interactions.

quotes.quote: NGC 628, 4254, 4303, 4321, 4535, and 5068 belong to the high end of the distribution, while NGC 1365, 1512, 1566, 3351, and 3627 belong to its low end.This is also summarised in the last column of Table tab:yes_no . Looking at the distribution across the galactic disks in the 3 ^ rd column of Figure fig:all_maps , we observe large areas of increasing recent SF in NGC 628, 4254, 4303, and 4321, while NGC 1512, 1566, and 3627 exhibit recent SF activity that ranges from roughly unchanged to decreasing. The most active galaxy in terms of recent SF changes is the low-mass galaxy NGC 5068. NGC 1365 shows a region with a very strong recent SF increase, visually similar to the headlight cloud in NGC 628 headlight_cloud .

The non-interacting galaxies include NGC 628, 1365, 3351, 4303, 4321, 4535, and 5068. NGC 628 is considered isolated since it has not experienced any gravitational interactions in the past 1 Gyr Kamphuis_92 .

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 628 as an Sc galaxy with an asymmetric two-armed pattern produced by comparable unstable m=2 and m=3 modes.

quotes.quote: NGC 628. This Sc galaxy has an asymmetric two-armed pattern with the northern arm split into two arms. A linear modal analysis shows the two unstable modes m = 2, and m = 3 of comparable growth rates. The upper right panel of Figure 1 shows a theoretical spiral pattern taken from the nonlinear simulations superimposed onto the B-band image taken by Larsen Richtler (1999). The theory qualitatively correctly predicts the morphology of the spiral pattern showing that the observed pattern is indeed a superposition of two unstable modes. However, the winding of the observed spiral pattern is higher in the central regions compared to the theoretical prediction which might be caused by the uncertainties in the measurements of the velocity dispersion and the rotation curve in the central regions of the galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 0628 as a nearly face-on grand-design spiral whose CO follows spiral arms, while radio continuum is deficient and produces a flat RC-CO correlation.

quotes.quote: NGC 0628 . This galaxy, seen almost face-on, has a clear grand-design spiral structure. The CO emission is strongly confined to the spiral arms regan01 . Our RC high-resolution image (Fig. figura1a ) is deficient in radio emission, except for a bright spot corresponding to an HII region kennicutt80 and an X-ray source colbert04 . The low-resolution RC image shows emission extended over about 6 . The point-by-point correlation for this source is flat and the radial profile shows a decrease of the \ ratio.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 628 ionized-gas kinematics, finding an inner disk-like component, inflow toward blue central regions, and radial motions linked to an m=2 perturbation.

quotes.quote: We study two dimensional Fabry-Perot interferometric observations of the nearby face-on late-type spiral galaxy, NGC 628. We investigate the role of the individual H ii regions together with the large-scale gravitational mechanisms which govern star formation and overall evolution in spiral galaxies. Our kinematical analysis (reinforced by literature maps in HI and CO at lower angular resolution) enables us to verify the presence of an inner rapidly rotating inner disk-like component which we attribute to long term secular evolution of the large-scale spiral arms and oval structure. We find that gas is falling in from the outer parts towards the bluer central regions. This could be an early phase in the formation of a pseudo-bulge. We find signatures of radial motions caused by an m=2 perturbation, which are likely to be responsible for the inflow of material forming the circumnuclear ring and the rapidly rotating inner structure.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 628 as a nearly face-on disk whose H I velocity field, flat gas motions, luminosity-profile truncation, and warp onset coincide.

quotes.quote: NGC 628 is almost completely face-on. The HI-velocity field displays a complicated pattern; in the tilted-ring model the rings actually go through the plane of the sky Sho84 . After subtraction of this rotation field the residuals show that systematic vertical motions in the gas are less than a few km/s, indicating again that the disk is extremely flat. The radial luminosity profiles vdK88 show evidence for a truncation, which again coincides with the onset of the warp.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 628 as a low-inclination SINGS spiral with disk-dominated magnetic fields, polarized-intensity trends, and faint secondary Faraday-depth components offset from the bright emission.

quotes.quote: Classic examples of disk-dominated fields can be seen in galaxies such as NGC 628, 5194, and 6946, although they are present in almost all of our targets with varying detectability.
The pairs of panels in the figure have been ordered by increasing galaxy inclination (top to bottom) extending from less than 10 degrees for NGC 628 to about 85 degrees in NGC 4631.

Solid detections of faint secondary emission components were made in the brightest face-on galaxies of our sample, as shown in Figs. figure:n628smo - figure:n6946smo . In addition to the bright polarized emission that typically resides at a Faraday depth within only a few tens of rad m ^ 2 of the Galactic foreground value, much fainter polarized emission (by a factor of 4 -- 5) is detected in NGC 628, 5194, and 6946, which is offset to both positive and negative Faraday depths by about 200 rad m ^ 2 .

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 628 as a nearby grand-design spiral with well-defined arms and finds no enhanced molecular-gas star-formation efficiency in its arm regions.

quotes.quote: We chose three galaxies, two grand-design (NGC 628 and NGC 5194) and one more flocculent (NGC 6946) on the basis of their proximity, orientation and multiband data. These have coverage in GALEX (NUV+FUV) (Gil de Paz et al., 2007), THINGS (Walter et al., 2008), SINGS (Kennicutt et al., 2003) and IRAM 30 observations (Schuster et al. 2007, Leroy et al., 2009).

We note that the grand-design spirals, NGC 5194 and NGC 628, have spiral arms that were well-defined using only the m=4 component divided by the m=0 component and that moving up to m=6 leads to little, if any difference.
Thus, at least for the two grand design spirals, NGC 628 and NGC 5194, we find that the SFE based on the molecular gas component is not enhanced in the arm region.

sources.summary: The paper uses resolved observations of NGC 628 to test a local metallicity relation, deriving stellar and gas surface densities and finding the resolved data follow a tight stellar-fraction relation.

quotes.quote: In order to test this prediction, we have compiled resolved HI, H _2 , stellar mass, and metallicity observations for the galaxies NGC 628 and NGC 3184. We have estimated stellar surface densities from the surface brightness of the 3.6- m emission detected by the Spitzer Infrared Nearby Galaxies Survey SINGS http://irsa.ipac.caltech.edu/data/SPITZER/SINGS , Kennicutt+03 , using a conversion factor Leroy+08 _* pc^ 2 = 280 i I_ 3.6 MJy sr^ 1 where i denotes the inclination angle of each galaxy (7 and 16 degrees for NGC 628 and NGC 3184, respectively). For the neutral and molecular gas, we have used the moment-0 maps available from The HI Nearby Galaxy Survey THINGS http://www.mpia-hd.mpg.de/THINGS , Walter+08 and the HERA CO-Line Extragalactic Survey HERACLES http://www.mpia-hd.mpg.de/HERACLES , Leroy+09 , estimating the surface densities as _ HI pc^ 2 = 0.020 i I_ 21 K km s^ 1 for atomic hydrogen and _ H_2 pc^ 2 = 5.5 i I_ CO K km s^ 1 for the molecular component, where I_ 21 and I_ CO correspond to the surface brightness of the 21-cm line and the 2 1 transition of carbon monoxide, respectively.

SDSS and XMP galaxies) as well as resolved observations of the stellar and total hydrogen column densities in NGC 628 and NGC 3184 describe a fairly tight relation, well described by equation eq_Z_s .

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 628 as a nearby spiral benchmark, finding its weighted H I profile closely follows the azimuthally averaged profile.

quotes.quote: To illustrate this fact, Fig. fig:hi_density_ha_comp shows the azimuthally averaged H i gas profiles for two large nearby spiral galaxies, NGC 628 and M 101 as black solid lines. The H i maps were taken from 2008AJ....136.2563W . To illustrate the effect of probing the gas density at the location of H ii regions in the outer disks we overplot the weighted H i profiles as orange symbols on top. The open symbols are for annuli where the mean SFR is <10^ 3 \ M _ yr ^ 1 kpc ^ 2 , assuming a Salpeter initial mass function and the L( ) to SFR conversion factor of 1998ARA&A..36..189K . We took the maps from 2001ApJ...559..878H . What is noticeable is that the two galaxies are rather different with the weighted profile in NGC 628 being very close to the straight mean profile. In contrast the weighted profile in M101 is noticeably higher than the mean profile and it is also clear that star formation at large radii is connected to relatively high gas densities.

sources.summary: The paper reports preliminary LEGUS results for NGC628, where inner and outer cluster populations favor a radially decreasing Schechter truncation and wavelength-dependent luminosity-function steepening.

quotes.quote: Preliminary results produced by the analysis of the cluster population in the inner and outer pointing of the spiral galaxy NGC628 (Adamo et al. in prep.) show that a Schechter function with decreasing (from the inner to the outer region) is statistically a better representation of the observed YSC mass function in the galaxy. This evidence is also supported by the study of the cluster luminosity function in the two regions. We find that the luminosity function shows a steepening as function of increasing wavelengths and a steepening at the highest luminosity bins in each band. The observed trend is in agreement with the expectations by , where the author assumes that the mass function has a truncation and cluster disruption has not yet been significant.

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 628 in a nearby star-forming galaxy sample used for spatially resolved UV-to-IR SED modeling and notes its relatively shallow SFR radial gradient.

quotes.quote: We have therefore selected eight galaxies in our sample: NGC 628, NGC 925, NGC 1097, NGC 3351, NGC 4321, NGC 4736, NGC 5055, and NGC 7793. Properties As we can see in Table tab:sample , the sample is made of fairly regular star--forming galaxies in the nearby universe, spanning the Hubble sequence from Sab to Sd. This ensures different levels of star-forming activity (from sSFR=-10.76 yr ^ 1 for NGC 4736 to sSFR=-9.59 yr ^ 1 for NGC 7793) and different levels of diffuse dust emission sauvage1992a .
Conversely NGC 628, NGC 925, and NGC 7793 have much shallower radial gradients.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 628 dust and CO maps to model how the rising CMB biases observations of cold dust, molecular gas, and velocity fields.

quotes.quote: We exploit NGC 628, M 33, and M 31, for which there are high-quality T_ dust maps over most of their extent and whose levels of star-formation activity range from the vigorous, in NGC 628 and M 33 ( _ SFR 10^ 3 10^ 2 \ - ), to the quiescent M 31 ( _ SFR 10^ 4 10^ 3 \ - ).

We simplify the fitting procedures in the literature and repeat the fit to derive cold dust temperature maps for M 31, M 33, and NGC 628, by fixing the emissivity indices for both cold and warm components.

We do not have a multiplicity of CO lines per position within NGC 628 that would allow us to determine n_ H_2 , T_ kin and d v/d (the average gas velocity gradient, which depends on the dynamical state of the H _2 gas). These parameters, once determined, could allow the computation of the emergent CO line intensities under different CMB backgrounds using a typical large velocity gradient (LVG) radiative transfer approach. However, the lack of spatially resolved CO SLEDs necessitates a more conservative approach where we set: kinetic temperature T_ kin = T_ d , H _2 number density n_ H_2 =10 ^3 cm ^ 3 and velocity gradient dv/dr = 1 , which are typical for quiescent non-SF H _2 clouds in local spiral galaxies. We use the available T_ d ^ z maps as input to a standard LVG code to compute the emergent CO line brightnesses scaled from the available local CO J =2--1 map. Unlike the case for dust emission and its optical depths, the optical depths of CO lines do depend on T_ kin ^ z and thus do not cancel out. Archival CO data We adopt the existing fully sampled CO J =2--1 data cube of NGC 628 in the HERA CO-Line Extragalactic Survey (HERACLES) archive http://www.mpia.de/HERACLES .

sources.summary: The paper describes M74 as a grand-design spiral with lower gas surface density than M51, intermediate CO intensity and line-width distributions, and moderate molecular surface density.

quotes.quote: M51 (NGC 5194) and M74 (NGC 628) are both grand design spiral galaxies within 10 Mpc distance. M51, the more massive of the two, is currently undergoing an interaction with its early type companion. It shows a higher surface density of gas in its disk, a higher molecular fraction, and more active star formation than M74.
M74 represents an intermediate case between the Local Group targets and M51.
The large spiral galaxies show higher , with average surface densities of 160 M _ pc ^ 2 in M51 and 50 M _ pc ^ 2 in M74.

sources.summary: NGC 628 is treated as an outlier, with a low isotope ratio, possibly flat or declining radial profiles, and uncertain fractionation effects needing more lines.

quotes.quote: Again NGC 628 represents the outlier, with a low ratio and perhaps a dropping rather than rising profile.
Again, NGC 628 appears as an outlier, while the other targets with resolved gradients show decreasing ^ 13 CO/C ^ 18 O with increasing _ SFR .

In NGC 628 where the radial profiles are flat, there could be older populations where the ^ 13 C has been created more efficiently and well mixed back into the ISM afterwards. This galaxy could also present strong fractionation effects; however more lines are needed to investigate it in detail.

sources.summary: The paper compares its metallicity-gradient modeling to NGC 628, noting prior work found about 50 percent metal loss, an opposite radial loss trend, and a positive stellar metallicity gradient atypical of local galaxies.

quotes.quote: Comparison with NGC 628 In comparing the mass of metals in gaseous and stellar components in the galaxy NGC 628 belfiore2016a found the average fraction of metals that has been lost to be 50 per cent. This is in qualitative agreement with our result when we adopt the empirical N2 calibration for the gas metallicity. However, sanchez2014a,belfiore2016a found the loss fraction to be higher in the central region and to decrease toward larger radii, which is the opposite of what we conclude form the chemical evolution modelling presented here. This discrepancy is likely caused by the positive stellar metallicity gradient of this specific galaxy, which is not representative of the normal galaxy population in the local universe.

sources.summary: The paper uses M74 as an extended low-brightness galaxy example where NVSS sensitivity and search-radius limits can miss total radio flux despite a large 1.4 GHz source.

quotes.quote: The NVSS contours and accurate 2MASX position for the very extended low-brightness galaxy M74 are shown in the middle row, left panel of Figure fig:figure2 . The closest NVSS catalog component is 93 from the 2MASX position, so M74 is not in the list of candidates within the r_ s = 60 search radius.

M74 emphasizes the importance of high surface-brightness sensitivity for identifying reasonably complete radio samples of nearby galaxies. Its total 1.4 GHz flux density is S 180 mJy , but its surface brightness is barely above the NVSS 5 5 0.45 mJy beam ^ 1 2.3 mJy beam ^ 1 detection limit.

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 628 as a strong-spiral EMPIRE target, notes its dense-gas datacube is among the noisiest, and treats it as an exception to central HCN/CO enhancement.

quotes.quote: The sample contains galaxies which show a strong spiral arm structure (NGC 628, NGC 3184, and NGC 5194).
Within our sample, we consistently find the datacubes for NGC 628 and NGC 5194 to be the noisiest, whereas NGC 4254 shows the lowest RMS noise.

In fact, in all galaxies except NGC 628, HCN/CO (blue in the bottom left panel) appears highest in the galaxy center and then decreases with increasing galactocentric radius. On average, HCN/CO decreases by a factor of 2 across the range of radii where we detect it. The decline in HCN/CO appears similar in our barred (NGC 2903, NGC 3184, NGC 3627, NGC 4321 and NGC 6946) and unbarred (NGC 628, NGC 4254, NGC 5055 and NGC 5194) targets.

sources.summary: The review reports Decleir2019's global and resolved attenuation-curve study of NGC628 (M74), describing a steep overall curve with a moderate bump and regional slope-opacity and slope-bump relations.

quotes.quote: Recently, Decleir2019 has applied the SED+LIR method using UV photometry from Swift in addition to GALEX to study the attenuation curve of the low-mass spiral galaxy NGC628 (M74) on global and resolved scales. The overall curve of this A_v=0.26 galaxy is steep ( S=5.3 ), with a moderate bump ( B=0.14 ). Interestingly, individual regions also show slope vs. \ A_V and slope vs. \ bump strength relations. Swift photometry was found to significantly help constrain the curve, especially the UV bump.

sources.summary: NGC 628 has a central decline in small-to-large grain ratio; the paper links its high metallicity and molecular gas fraction to accretion, then coagulation in the innermost disk.

quotes.quote: There is a drop of D_ S /D_ L in the central parts of NGC 628, where the metallicity and the molecular gas fraction are high (see middle panels of Fig. fig:SFR_mol ). In these central areas dust is growing via accretion and D_ tot is increasing (see both panels of Fig. fig:D2Gcomp_Hou17 ) with metallicity. The critical metallicity defined by 2013EP&S...65..213A has been reached and the molecular gas fraction is quite high, which are the conditions for accretion to take place. However, in the most inner parts, at R 0.3 R _ 25 , coagulation is taking over and a high fraction of large grains is forming, causing D_ S /D_ L to decrease.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 628 has a shorter metallicity correlation scale than the other sample galaxies but remains significantly different from randomized metallicity maps.

quotes.quote: In all galaxies except NGC 628, the correlation does not drop below 50 until scales become larger than 300--400 pc and remains above 30 out to kpc scales. This is a significant result in all galaxies (including NGC 628) when compared to the null hypothesis, where we have randomized S_X . This significance persists when including random sampling of the metallicity uncertainties. NGC 628 shows shorter correlation scales, but is still significanctly different from the null hypothesis.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 0628 as a nearly face-on SAc spiral at 9.8 Mpc, observed by LEGUS in two pointings and previously studied for gas, dust, hierarchical star formation, and TPCF structure.

quotes.quote: NGC 0628 NGC 0628 is a nearly face-on spiral galaxy (morphology SAc) located at a distance of 9.8 Mpc Anand_2021 . It was observed by the LEGUS survey with two pointings. This galaxy has been observed extensively by all recent major surveys of interstellar gas and dust in nearby galaxies, including THINGS, HERACLES, SINGS, KINGFISH, EMPIRE, and with ALMA Walter_2008,Leroy_2009,Kennicutt_2003,Kennicutt_2011,Bigiel_2016,Turner_2019 . Elmegreen_2006 investigated hierarchical star formation in this galaxy, and Grasha_2015 report a measurement of its TPCF.

sources.summary: The model gives NGC 628 one of the highest central gas Toomre parameters, a mass-accretion profile that flattens beyond 2 kpc, and unusually short inner viscous timescales.

quotes.quote: The highest central Q_ gas are found in NGC 628 and NGC 5055 ( Q 8 ).
In NGC 628 and NGC 3184 the mass accretion rate increases in the inner 2 kpc and stays approximately constant at larger radii.

For all massive galaxies, except NGC 628, the viscous timescales exceed the depletion timescales for R>R_ d by a factor of about 10. For NGC 628, is particularly small in the inner 2.5 kpc, and close to the gas depletion timescale of 2 3 Gyr.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 628 as a grand-design spiral whose inner disk kinematics require a chosen inclination and show radial-velocity variations and anisotropies.

quotes.quote: NGC 628 NGC 628 is considered an archetypal example of a grand design spiral galaxy; it shows two spiral arms that are visible also in the intensity map. The TRM analysis finds that both the P.A. and the inclination angle are close to constant only for R<300" , a radius corresponding to the inner optical disk Schruba_etal_2011 . While the values of the P.A. of the inner disk from the distribution of determined by , and agree with each other and with its kinematical value, the inclination angle differs greatly between different authors. reported a value of i=6.5^ for the inner disk, similar to that of ( i=8.5^ ) and ( i=8^ ) whereas other authors report different values. In particular, found i=25^ (photometric) and i=26.4^ (kinematical) while found i=15^ for the disk and i=21^ for the optical disk.

sources.summary: The paper uses M 74 as a nearby star-forming galaxy example where SALTUS far-infrared diagnostics could resolve feedback bubbles and ISM heating and cooling.

quotes.quote: Figure fig:FO11 gives an example in a nearby, star-forming galaxy M 74 (The Phantom Galaxy or NGC 628). While new observations from JWST have resolved the galaxy and sites of stellar feedback in stunning detail (Figure fig:FO11 ) Lee2023,Watkins2023,Barnes2023 , the physics of the photodissociation regions surrounding these supernova bubbles is unknown without FIR diagnostics. To understand how ISM heating and cooling vary under such conditions requires high spatial resolution observations of key FIR diagnostics.
In nearby galaxies, the priority is to measure PDRs and feedback in well-characterized prototypes (e.g., M 74) across a range of environments.

sources.summary: The paper notes NGC628 lacks high-precision distance measurements, but adopts brightest-star distances as reliable because independent measurements and group distance moduli agree.

quotes.quote: NGC628. -- There is no distance to NGC628 determined with one of the ``high-precision'' methods. However, the available distances to NGC628 derived with the method of brightest stars seem to be quite reliable. Firstly, the distances derived from two different investigations are in agreement: 7.24 Mpc (Sohn Davidge 1996) compared to 7.32 Mpc (Sharina, Karachentsev Tikhonov 1996). Secondly, the distance moduli estimated by Sharina, Karachentsev Tikhonov (1996) for four galaxies in the group of NGC628 have a mean value of 7.80 Mpc with a small scatter.

sources.summary: The paper treats M74/NGC 628 as a nearby face-on spiral at 8.7 Mpc, observed in X-ray and optical data to study the ULX environment.

quotes.quote: We present results on \ ( ), a ULX in the nearby spiral galaxy M74 (NGC 628), from observations by Chandra and XMM-Newton . The first X-ray imaging observation of M74, which is at a distance of 8.7 Mpc huchra99 , was obtained with the Einstein HRI on 1980 January 5. M74 was also observed by ASCA on 1995 July 28, but no source is detected at the location of . The Chandra observations of M74 were conducted as part of a Cycle 2 large project to study the point source population of nearby, face-on spiral galaxies prestwich01 .

sources.summary: The paper highlights NGC 628 as showing an older-age spike beyond one effective radius on both sides, with independent SAURON observations reporting the same age trend.

quotes.quote: Of particular note is the spike to older ages just beyond 1 r_e for NGC 628. This is precisely the signature noted in intro required to reconcile the discrepant results for the MW bulge. The fact that we observe this signature on both sides of the galaxy (whose spectra were fit entirely independently) renders confidence that this signature is real. Using SAURON observations, Gandia et al. (in prep.) observe the same trend in age for NGC 628 using a fully independent approach.

sources.summary: The paper surveys M74 for planetary nebulae, reports 153 candidates in the six-galaxy sequence, and gives a first reliable PNLF distance estimate of 8.6 Mpc.

quotes.quote: As the first step to determine disk mass-to-light ratios for normal spiral galaxies, we present the results of an imaging survey for planetary nebulae (PNe) in six nearby, face-on systems: IC 342, M74 (NGC 628), M83 (NGC 5236), M94 (NGC 4736), NGC 5068, and NGC 6946. Using Blanco/Mosaic II and WIYN/OPTIC, we identify 165, 153, 241, 150, 19, and 71 PN candidates, respectively, and use the Planetary Nebula Luminosity Function (PNLF) to obtain distances. For M74 and NGC 5068, our distances of 8.6 0.3 Mpc and 5.4^ +0.2 _ 0.4 Mpc are the first reliable estimates to these objects; for IC 342 ( 3.5 0.3 Mpc), M83 ( 4.8 0.1 Mpc), M94 ( 4.4^ +0.1 _ 0.2 Mpc), and NGC 6946 ( 6.1 0.6 Mpc) our values agree well with those in the literature.

sources.summary: The review finds NGC 628 has a strong two-armed spiral pattern and CO-to-24 micron phase shifts that support a long-lived density-wave spiral.

quotes.quote: It was found that two of the objects (NGC 628 and NGC 5194), with strong two-arm patterns, present positive evidence for long-lived spirals.
The results are shown in figure  fig1 , where we can see that only three objects (NGC 628, NGC 3627 and NGC 5194) show signs of strong m=2 spiral patterns.
For NGC 628, the CO- 24 phase shift coincides with the expectations up to the place where the spiral arms seem to end, as inferred from the mass map.

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 628 in a five-galaxy dust-to-gas study, treating it as a spiral with large LEGUS coverage and radial-analysis leverage.

quotes.quote: We present a study of the dust--to--gas ratios in five nearby galaxies NGC 628 (M74), NGC 6503, NGC 7793, UGC 5139 (Holmberg I), and UGC 4305 (Holmberg II).
NGC 628, NGC 6503, and NGC 7793 are all spiral galaxies with a range of masses, though NGC 7793 is categorized as a flocculent.
NGC 628 is one of the most distant galaxies in the sample, and NGC 7793 is relatively nearby, at roughly at the same distance as the two dwarfs.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 0628 observations as an H II-region template and finds spiral-arm CO/H-alpha offsets in its disk.

quotes.quote: To do this, we use an integrated, high signal-to-noise H ii region spectrum from the MUSE observations of NGC 0628 presented in kreckel16 .
Our targets are all relatively massive, relatively face-on spiral galaxies, so we expect the NGC 0628 spectrum to be reasonably representative of the typical H ii region in our sample.
This is most evident along the spiral arms of NGC 0628, NGC 4321, and NGC 5194, but the same pattern can be seen in NGC 4254, and NGC 4535.

sources.summary: NGC628 is used to test GMC lifecycles; its separation length is relatively constant, cloud lifetimes resist simple dynamical-time explanations, and a bright headlight cloud is exceptional.

quotes.quote: For example, \ is relatively constant in some galaxies (e.g. \ NGC628, NGC4303 and NGC4321), but in others it peaks at a certain radius (e.g. \ NGC3627, NGC4254 and NGC5194), or decreases outwards (e.g. \ NGC3351, NGC4535 and NGC5068).

Despite the rough similarity between \ and , in many cases (e.g. \ NGC628, NGC3627, NGC5194) the measured molecular cloud lifetimes cannot be simply explained by the local cloud dynamical time, as visible in Figure fig:radprof .

One of the most striking examples is found in NGC628, with the presence of a very bright cloud (referred to as the `headlight' cloud in Herrera2019 ), located at the intersection of a spiral arm and the co-rotation radius at 3.2 kpc.

sources.summary: NGC628 is treated as a large late-type spiral with moderate star formation, compact outer-disk H II regions, an extended H I disk, and outer-disk dynamical mass exceeding H I mass.

quotes.quote: The first four galaxies NGC628, NGC6946, NGC3184 and NGC4214 are large, late type spirals that have H i to stellar disk mass ratios M(HI)/M(star) < 1 (Table 1) and host star formation in their inner regions.
The galaxies NGC628, NGC6946 and NGC3184 are grand design spirals with moderate star formation rates (SFRs) of values 1 to 5, but NGC4214 has a much lower SFR of 0.05 walter.etal.2008 .

The galaxies NGC628 and NGC6946 also have compact H ii regions in their outer disks ferguson.etal.1998,lelievre.Roy.2000 , which is rare and often attributed to cold gas accretion from the intergalactic medium which can trigger star formation thilker.etal.2007 .

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 628 as an observational comparison for oxygen loss, describing spatially resolved measurements showing roughly half its oxygen lost within 7 kpc and stronger loss in the bulge.

quotes.quote: In more distant galaxies, direct measurements are more challenging since individual stars cannot be resolved, however belfiore16 obtained spatially resolved measurements of oxygen loss in the face-on spiral galaxy NGC 628, with about half of oxygen mass lost from the inner 7 kpc.

belfiore16 analysed NGC 628 in such a way, finding that metal loss was highest in the bulge ( 70 per cent lost) and decreased towards the outskirts, with about half of all oxygen lost out to 7 kpc. This is the opposite trend to Fig. fig:maps , but we note that the stellar mass of NGC 628 is estimated to be 10^ 9.8 , and so may be more susceptible to losing material than the comparatively high mass galaxies of Fig. fig:maps .

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 0628 as a galaxy with well-defined spiral arms; its cloud mass distribution strongly favors a truncated power law, and internal cloud pressure increases with radius.

quotes.quote: Spiral Arm - These clouds are located within a spiral arm of the galaxy with well defined arms (NGC 0628, NGC 1637, NGC 3627, NGC 5643).
The BIC indicates a very strong preference BIC >10 , bayes-factor for the truncated power-law mass distribution in NGC 0628, NGC 2903, NGC 3521, and NGC 3627.
In our sample, there are two galaxies where the internal pressure increases by a factor of 1.5 with radius NGC 0628 and NGC 5068.

sources.summary: The paper singles out NGC628 because its observed satellite stellar mass function is not predicted by the fiducial model despite its rich satellite population for its host mass.

quotes.quote: In one case (NGC628), the well-sampled SMF is not predicted by the model. This host is one of the richest hosts in the ELVES sample in terms of its number of satellites ( N_ sat =13 ) for its host stellar mass ( (M_ , host /M_ )=10.45 ). It would be interesting to investigate what might be causing this disagreement (e.g. an unusual growth history, etc.).

sources.summary: The paper cites NGC 628 as a Swift/UVOT case study whose median attenuation curve is steep with a sub-Milky-Way UV bump and regional variations tied to higher A_ V.

quotes.quote: We note that the Swift /UVOT data have been used to study the spatial resolved dust attenuation curve and UV bump for a handful of nearby galaxies, e.g. M81 and Holmberg IX by Hoversten-2011 , SMC by Hagen2017 and NGC 628 by Decleir2019 . Both M81 and Holmberg IX can be best fit with a Milky Way extinction curve with a prominent UV bump, while the median attenuation curve of NGC 628 and SMC is fairly steep, with a sub-MW-type UV bump. In addition, intriguing variations are observed among different regions in NGC 628 and SMC, with regions of higher A_ V found to have shallower attenuation curves and weaker UV bumps.

sources.summary: The paper maps PAH fraction across NGC 628 with JWST/MIRI, reporting its average PAH ratio and especially clear depressions in MUSE-traced regions.

quotes.quote: We find average R _ PAH = (F770W+F1130W)/F2100W values of 3.3, 4.7, 5.1, and 3.6 in IC 5332, NGC 628, NGC 1365, and NGC 7496, respectively.
In this Letter, we investigate the variations of the PAH fraction traced by a combination of JWST/MIRI filters across the full disk of four nearby galaxies, IC 5332, NGC 628, NGC 1365, and NGC 7496.
In all cases, it appears that shows clear depressions within regions, though NGC 628 and NGC 7496 show this contrast most clearly.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 628 as a 10 Mpc grand-design spiral with moderate star formation and dust, placed between the low- and high-SFR pilot galaxies.

quotes.quote: The galaxies are NGC 1433 (a relatively dust-free, low SFR barred spiral), NGC 3351 (a barred spiral with low dust content and SFR in the outer region, but a high SFR ring with extensive dust in the inner region), NGC 628 (a grand-design spiral with moderate SFR and dust), and NGC 1365 (a dusty barred galaxy with very high SFR). Galaxy distances range from 10 Mpc (NGC 628 and NGC 3351), to 18 Mpc for NGC 1433, and 20 Mpc for NGC 1365 gagandeep21 . The four galaxies are shown in Figure fig:4x2_image in the order they will be discussed in this paper, from the lowest SFR (0.56 M _ /yr), least dusty galaxy (NGC 1433) to the highest SFR (16.90 M _ /yr) dustiest galaxy (NGC 1365).

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC0628 as a spiral with typical UV-to-infrared scale-length ratios and no notable mass-weighted age increase within the optical radius.

quotes.quote: NGC0628 and NGC5457 have ratios of the scale lengths that are consistent with the mean ratios of spiral galaxies: h_ FUV /h_ 3.6 m (NGC0628) = 1.87 ( 1.67) h_ NUV /h_ 3.6 m (NGC0628) = 1.59 ( 1.42) and h_ FUV /h_ 3.6 m (NGC5457) = 1.75 ( 1.67) h_ NUV /h_ 3.6 m (NGC5457) = 1.58 ( 1.42) . The h_ FUV /h_ 3.6 m and h_ NUV /h_ 3.6 m values are clearly higher than the reference values (1.67 and 1.42) for the galaxies NGC0628 and NGC5457. This is consistent with our results: We do not find a particular increasing trend for the mass-weighted age within the optical radius for NGC0628 and NGC5457.

sources.summary: The atlas describes NGC 628 as having an extended H i envelope, diffuse disk radio emission, bright spiral arms, and a radio-deficient optical center despite central molecular gas.

quotes.quote: This galaxy possesses a very extended H i envelope, most likely fed by infalling intergalactic gas (Kamphius Briggs ). The radio continuum map shows diffuse disk emission as well as the two main spiral arms at higher surface brightness. Curiously, the optically bright central region is deficient in the radio continuum, although the CO map of Wakker Adler ( ) suggests the presence of copious molecular gas within the central 30 \ region, albeit without any concentration towards the very center.

gmw_00000040.name: NGC 3741
gmw_00000040.overview: NGC 3741 is a dark-matter-dominated dwarf irregular galaxy [arXiv:0711.2153, arXiv:1308.3029] featuring an exceptionally extended neutral hydrogen disk [arXiv:0711.1807, arXiv:1811.05260] and a slow gaseous bar [arXiv:1308.3029, arXiv:2202.11734]. Its peculiar central star formation and young stellar structures may be driven by a recent merger or ongoing gas accretion [arXiv:2202.11734].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3741 as an extremely gas-rich dwarf with an HI disk extending far beyond the optical body, a flat outer rotation curve, and a very high mass-to-light ratio.

quotes.quote: In this paper we discuss two very gas rich galaxies that were observed as part of the FIGGS survey, viz. NGC 3741 and AndIV. NGC 3741 ( M_B 13.13 ) has M _ HI /L _ B 5.8 . GMRT observations of this galaxy have been presented in Begum et al. (2005).

The GMRT observations (Begum et a. 2005) showed that NGC 3741 had an HI disk that extends to 8.3 times its Holmberg radius. This makes it probably the most extended gas disk known. Our observations allowed us to derive the rotation curve (which is flat in the outer regions) out to 38 optical scale lengths. NGC 3741 has a dynamical mass to light ratio of 107 and is one of the "darkest'' irregular galaxies known.

sources.summary: The paper highlights NGC 3741 as a compact-stellar, extended-gas galaxy whose rotation curve favors DC14 and needs a larger coreNFW core radius to match the data.

quotes.quote: For NGC 3741 this might be justifiable. Indeed, while the stellar distribution of NGC 3741 is unusually compact, the DC14 model finds sufficient stellar mass (i.e. supernova feedback energy) to form a substantial core that is in agreement with the data. Furthermore, the distribution of the gas is much more extended than that of the stars.

Since the gravity of the outflowing gas after a supernova explosion drives the formation of a dark matter core, we could interpret the extended gas distribution in NGC 3741 as a sign that the dark matter core radius is actually larger than that inferred from the half-mass radius of the stars. Therefore we performed a second core NFW fit for NGC 3741 with as an additional free parameter (using a flat prior such that 0 < r_ (kpc) < 7). The decomposed rotation curve is given in Figure fig:N3741_cnfw_free . With a larger core radius of 4.06 kpc ( = 11.2 ) the core NFW model is now in excellent agreement with the data and with the DC14 model.

sources.summary: The paper reports young elongated stellar structures and an HI bar in NGC 3741, proposing recent merger or gas accretion to explain star formation, spiral arms, and dense central gas.

quotes.quote: Using Large Binocular Telescope deep imaging data from the Smallest Scale of Hierarchy Survey (SSH) and archival Hubble Space Telescope data, we reveal the presence of two elongated stellar features contiguous to a bar-like stellar structure in the inner regions of the dwarf irregular galaxy NGC 3741. These structures are dominated by stars younger than a few hundred Myr and collectively are about twice as extended as the old stellar component. These properties are very unusual for dwarf galaxies in the nearby Universe and difficult to explain by hydro-dynamical simulations.

From the analysis of archival 21-cm observations, we find that the young stellar ``bar'' coincides with an HI high-density region proposed by previous studies to be a purely gaseous bar; we furthermore confirm radial motions of a few km/s, compatible with an inflow/outflow, and derive a steeply-rising rotation curve and high HI surface density at the center, indicating a very concentrated mass distribution. We propose that the peculiar properties of the stellar and gaseous components of NGC 3741 may be explained by a recent merger or ongoing gas accretion from the intergalactic medium, which caused gas inflows towards the galaxy center and triggered star formation a few hundred Myr ago. This event may explain the young and extended stellar features, the bar-like structure, the very extended HI disc and the central HI spiral arms. The high central HI density and the steeply rising rotation curve suggest that NGC 3741 may be the progenitor or the descendant of a starburst dwarf.

sources.summary: The paper measures NGC 3741 as an extremely gas-rich, dark-matter-dominated dwarf with an extended H I disk and a slow H I bar.

quotes.quote: Using the Tremaine-Weinberg method, we measure the speed of the HI bar seen in the disk of NGC 3741. NGC 3741 is an extremely gas rich galaxy with an H i disk which extends to about 8.3 times its Holmberg radius. It is also highly dark matter-dominated.

Our calculated value of the pattern speed _p is 17.1 3.4 km s ^ 1 kpc ^ 1 . We also find the ratio of the co-rotation radius to the bar semi-major axis to be (1.6 0.3), indicating a slow bar. This is consistent with bar models in which dynamical friction results in a slow bar in dark matter dominated galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper highlights NGC3741 as the best rotation-curve dataset, with fractional gravity preferred and a halo-dominated rotation curve extending to large radii.

quotes.quote: Specifically, we consider the 8 galaxies in SPARC that are classified as dwIrr and are flagged as having high quality data on the rotation curve: D631-7, DDO64, DDO161, UGC731, UGC5005, UGC5414, UGC7608, NGC3741. The latter is the galaxy with the best dataset, and actually constitutes the object with the most extended rotation curve (relative to the half-light radius) measured to date.

In particular, for D631-7, DDO64, DDO161, UGC5005, UGC5414, and NGC3741 there is a clear preference for FG both in terms of _r^2 and of the BIC. In such cases, the fractional index takes on typical values s 1.2-1.3 , the stellar mass-to-light ratios M_ /L are slightly larger than for the Newtonian case and more in line with the value around 0.5 expected from stellar population synthesis models, and the DM masses M_ 200 are appreciably larger of factors a few than in the Newtonian fit.

The bottom panel of the same Figure shows that for NGC3741 and DDO64 the halo component largely dominates the rotation curve, with the baryonic contribution being relevant only in the innermost region within a few r_ 1/2 ; this situation is shared by all dwIrr in the analyzed sample. Therefore the shape of the rotation curve strongly constrains the halo mass/density profile; in particular, the rising trend of the rotation velocity out to large radii is difficult to be reproduced with a NFW profile in standard gravity, while the task can be easily achieved in the FG framework.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 3741 as a nearby dwarf irregular with an exceptionally extended H I disk, non-circular motions, and a rotation curve fit better by a Burkert halo.

quotes.quote: NGC 3741 is a dwarf irregular galaxy at 3.5 Mpc distance. The HI observations reveal the largest disk in terms of optical scale length, viz. 42 R_ opt .
NGC 3741 exhibits non-circular motions of up to 10 km s ^ 1 , most likely due to a bar in the inner region and to gas inflow in the outer one.
The rotation curve of NGC 3741, too, fits the Burkert halo very well, while CDM halo profiles produce worse fits.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 3741 as a late-type dwarf irregular with an extended HI disk, rotational support, and a dense compact dark-matter halo suppressing small-scale spiral structure.

quotes.quote: We select five late-type dwarf irregular galaxies, namely, DDO 154, NGC 3741, DDO 43, NGC 2366 and DDO 168 in which we study the constraining influence of dominant dark halo on the formation of small-scale spiral arms. Two of these (DDO 154, NGC 2366) are a part of the THINGS (The HI Nearby Galaxy Survey) survey by Wal08 , whereas DDO 43 and DDO 168 are a part of the LITTLE (Local Irregulars That Trace Luminosity Extremes) THINGS (The HI Nearby Galaxy Survey) survey by Hun12 . Our sample galaxies have the following characteristics: These have a large HI disk (extending to several optical radii), and in the inner regions as well as in the outer regions the larger part of the baryonic contribution comes from HI gas, and not stars. The galaxies are rotationally supported. We find that for these low luminosity galaxies, the corresponding dark matter halo is dense and compact, i.e., the central density ( _0 ) is few times 10^ 2 M _ pc ^ 2 and the core radius (R _ ) is less than two-three times the exponential disk scalelength (R _ d ).

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 3741 as an extremely gas-rich galaxy whose observed rotation curve is explained in MOND by its central stellar disk and extended neutral gas disk.

quotes.quote: The case of an extremely gas rich galaxy, NGC 3741, is shown in Fig, 4 where again we see the calculated rotation curves with and without the contribution of the stellar disk compared to the observations (Gentile et al. 2007). For this object the gas disc extends 40 exponential scale lengths beyond the luminous disk and is 23 time more massive; i.e., the stellar disk is roughly 4 of the total baryonic mass and, based upon M/L_K = 0.3 , is consistent with stellar population synthesis. The calculated rotation curves, with and without stars, agree in the outer regions but deviate somewhat in the inner regions where the stellar disk contributes. The observed rotation curve suggests the presence of two components, and in the MOND prediction these are the two baryonic components: the central luminous disk and extended neutral gas disk. But the overall agreement of the calculated rotation curves with and without stars demonstrates the independence of the calculated rotation curve from the M/L of the stellar disk.

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 3741 as a CVn I galaxy observed with WFPC2, describing its stellar population or morphology and deriving a TRGB-based distance.

quotes.quote: Like two previous objects, NGC 3741 lies at the NW periphery of the CVn I cloud. The galaxy has an asymmetric ``cometary'' shape. Its size in H i exceeds its optical diameter significantly (Haynes Giovanelli 1991). Georgiev at al. (1997) derived its distance to be 3.5 0.7 Mpc from the brightest blue stars, while the TRGB distance from our measurements is 3.03 0.33 Mpc.

sources.summary: The FIGGS survey highlights NGC 3741 as a faint dwarf irregular with an exceptionally extended H I disk, a rotation curve to 38 disk scale lengths, and very high dynamical mass-to-light ratio.

quotes.quote: Serendipitous discoveries are an added bonus -- for example, FIGGS has discovered some very extended HI disks around dwarf galaxies e.g. GMRT HI images of NGC 3741 (M _B 13.0 ) showed it to have an HI extent of 8.3 times R _ Ho (Holmberg radius) (follow-up WSRT+DRAO+GMRT observations found 8.8 R _ Ho ) NGC 3741 has the most extended HI disks and we could derive a rotation curve upto a record of 38 times the disk scale length. NGC 3741 has M _D/L_B 107 which makes it one of the ``darkest'' irregular galaxies known (Begum et al. 2005).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3741 as a dwarf spiral with an exceptionally extended HI rotation curve decomposed into stellar, gaseous, and Burkert dark-halo components.

quotes.quote: NGC 3741 is a dwarf spiral with the most extended available HI disk rotation curve in terms of the galaxy stellar disk size lenght scale, R_D . This galaxy has been observed in the HI line with the Westerbork Synthesis Radio Telescope out to 42 R_D i.e. 13.5 \ R_ opt . The rotation curve, accurately derived from HI data cubes, is decomposed into its stellar, gaseous and dark components, the latter represented by a Burkert halo, necessary, in this object, for a successful fit.

gmw_00000041.name: AndIV
gmw_00000041.overview: AndIV is a background gas-rich dwarf galaxy located beyond the Local Group [arXiv:0711.2153]. It features an extended regular neutral hydrogen disk that exhibits systematic rotation and possesses an extremely high dynamical mass-to-light ratio [arXiv:0711.2153].

sources.summary: The paper argues AndIV is a background gas-rich dwarf beyond the Local Group, with an extended regular HI disk, systematic rotation, and an extremely high dynamical mass-to-light ratio.

quotes.quote: And IV, was originally thought to be a satellite of the Andromeda (M31) galaxy. However, based on HST imaging Ferguson et al. (2000) argue that it is likely to be a background galaxy that happens to lie in projection close to the disk of M31. Consistent with this interpretation, they derive a distance of 6.11 Mpc for it (using the Tip of the Red Giant Branch technique) -- this places And IV beyond the confines of the local group. The HI velocity measured for this galaxy (i.e. 234 km/s; Braun et al. (2003)) is also substantially different from that of the nearest portion of the disk of M31. The galaxy has blue magnitude of 12.37 , which implies that M _ HI /L _ B 13 .

For AndIV, the GMRT observations show that its gas disk extends out to 6 Holmberg radii. Fig. fig:jchengalur1 shows the integrated HI emission from AndIV at 44 '' 38'' resolution, overlayed on the digitised sky survey (DSS) image. Fig. fig:jchengalur2 shows the velocity field of AndIV at 26 ^ '' 23^ '' resolution. The velocity field is regular and a large scale velocity gradient, consistent with systematic rotation, is seen across the galaxy. From the rotation curve Fig. fig:jchengalur3 the ratio of the dynamical mass to the blue luminosity is M_ dyn /L_ B 237 .

gmw_00000042.name: NGC 1292
gmw_00000042.overview: NGC 1292 is an Sb spiral disk galaxy that hosts a pseudobulge [arXiv:0802.0610, arXiv:0806.2988]. This pseudobulge is characterized by an intermediate stellar age of approximately 3 Gyr, low metallicity, and ongoing star formation [arXiv:0806.2988].

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 1292 as an Sb disk galaxy with a likely pseudobulge whose structure, kinematics, intermediate-age population, and ongoing star formation support secular buildup.

quotes.quote: The photometric and kinematic properties of Sb NGC 1292 suggest it hosts a pseudobulge. The properties of the stellar population of such a pseudobulge are consistent with a slow buildup within a scenario of secular evolution.

The bulge of the Sb spiral NGC 1292 has a S ersic index n = 0.52 and an apparent flattening which is similar to that of the galaxy disk ( q=0.6 ). The measured V_ max / _0 is consistent with being significantly larger than that derived for an oblate spheroid (Fig. fig:VSigma_FJ , left panel). Moreover, the bulge of NGC 1292 is not consistent with the R- band Faber-Jackson relation built from L ^ 3.92 forpon99 as done by magu05 . It is characterized by a lower velocity dispersion (or equivalently a higher luminosity) with respect to its early-type counterparts (Fig. fig:VSigma_FJ , right panel). NGC 1292 is also a low- (or a high- L ) outlier with respect to the relationship found for faint early-type galaxies by L ^ 2.01 magu05 .

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 1292 as the sample’s most reliable pseudobulge, with intermediate age, low metallicity, low alpha enhancement, and ongoing star formation.

quotes.quote: According to the prescriptions by korken04 the bulge of NGC 1292 is a pseudobulge. The properties of its stellar population are consistent with a slow buildup within a scenario of secular evolution. Indeed, the bulge of NGC 1292 has a intermediate age (3 Gyr) and low metal content ( =-0.7 dex). The / Fe enhancement is the lowest in our sample ( =-0.12 dex) suggesting a prolonged star formation history. The presence of emission lines in the spectrum is a signature of ongoing star formation.

gmw_00000043.name: 1H 0419-577
gmw_00000043.overview: The active galaxy 1H 0419-577 is classified as a Seyfert 1.5 galaxy [arXiv:1305.6232, arXiv:1106.2084] or a radio-quiet, low-luminosity quasar [arXiv:1106.2084, arXiv:1310.2770]. It hosts a supermassive black hole [arXiv:2308.08528, arXiv:1908.05177] and drives a kiloparsec-scale biconical ionized outflow [arXiv:1710.04714, arXiv:1509.05773] that acts as a weakly ionized warm absorber [arXiv:1305.6232].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes 1H0419-577 as a Seyfert 1.5 galaxy, finding a thin weakly ionized warm absorber tied to UV outflow components at host-galaxy scale.

quotes.quote: Verbunt 1,5 SRON Netherlands Institute for Space Research, Sorbonnelaan 2, 3584 CA Utrecht, The Netherlands L.di.Gesu@sron.nl Department of Physics, Virginia Tech, Blacksburg, VA 24061, USA Osservatorio Astronomico di Roma (INAF), Via Frascati 33, I--00040, Monteporzio Catone (Roma), Italy XMM-Newton Science Operations Centre, ESA, PO Box 78, 28691 Villanueva de la Canada, Madrid, Spain Department of Astrophysics/IMAPP, Radboud University, PO Box 9010, 6500 GL Nijmegen, The Netherlands In this paper we analyze the X-ray, UV and optical data of the Seyfert 1.5 galaxy 1H0419-577, with the aim of detecting and studying an ionized-gas outflow. The source was observed simultaneously in the X-rays with and in the UV with . Optical data were also acquired with the Optical Monitor. We detected a thin, lowly ionized warm absorber ( 0.03 , 19.9 ) in the X-ray spectrum, consistent to be produced by the same outflow already detected in the UV. Provided the gas density estimated in the UV, the outflow is consistent to be located in the host galaxy, at kpc scale.

Absorber distance and energetics The spectral analysis of the RGS spectrum of 1H0419-577, together with the analysis of the simultaneous COS spectrum (E11) revealed that the X-ray and the UV emission spectra of are both absorbed by a thin, weakly ionized absorber. As pointed out in Sect. cfr_abs the ionization parameter and the ionic column densities measured independently in the UV and in the X-ray agree with each other. Thus, it is likely that the UV and X-ray warm absorber are one and the same gas. In the UV the kinetic structure of the warm absorber is resolved, with three outflowing components detected.

sources.summary: The paper models 1H0419-577's simultaneous optical/UV/X-ray spectrum with Comptonized disk emission plus variable partial covering absorption, interpreting its obscuration and variability geometrically.

quotes.quote: Simultaneous XMM- Newton and HST-COS observation of 1H0419-577 II. Broadband spectral modeling of a variable Seyfert galaxy. L. Di Gesu 1 E. Costantini 1 E. Piconcelli 2 J. Ebrero 1 M. Mehdipour 3 J.S. Kaastra 1 SRON Netherlands Institute for Space Research, Sorbonnelaan 2, 3584 CA Utrecht, The Netherlands L.di.Gesu@sron.nl Osservatorio Astronomico di Roma (INAF), Via Frascati 33, I--00040, Monteporzio Catone (Roma), Italy Mullard Space Science Laboratory, University College London, Holmbury St. Mary, Dorking, Surrey, RH5 6NT, UK Received October 30, 2013; accepted January 20, 2014 In this paper we present the longest exposure (97 ks) EPIC-pn spectrum ever obtained for the Seyfert 1.5 galaxy . With the aim of explaining the broadband emission of this source, we took advantage of the simultaneous coverage in the optical/UV that was provided in the present case by the Optical Monitor and by a HST-COS observation. Archival FUSE flux measurements in the FUV were also used for the present analysis.

We successfully modeled the X-ray spectrum together with the optical/UV fluxes data points using a Comptonization model. We found that a blackbody temperature of T 56 eV accounts for the optical/UV emission originating in the accretion disk. This temperature serves as input for the Comptonized components that model the X-ray continuum. Both a warm ( T_ wc 0.7 keV, _ wc 7 ) and a hot corona ( T_ hc 160 keV, _ hc 0.5 ) intervene to upscatter the disk photons to X-ray wavelengths.

sources.summary: The paper resolves 1H 0419-577's extranuclear soft X-ray halo with Chandra, relates it to a known kpc outflow, and finds a spheroidal morphology possibly from low-density thermal plasma.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: The source centroid found by wavdetect is 4:26:00.7, -57:12:02, which is consistent with the nominal coordinates of 1H 0419-577.

quotes.quote: The Seyfert 1 galaxy 1H 0419-577 hosts a kpc extended outflow that is evident in the [ O iii ] image and that is also detected as a warm absorber in the UV/X-ray spectrum. Here, we analyze a 30 ks Chandra-ACIS X-ray image, with the aim of resolving the diffuse extranuclear X-ray emission and of investigating its relationship with the galactic outflow. Thanks to its sub-arcsecond spatial resolution, Chandra resolves the circumnuclear X-ray emission, which extends up to a projected distance of at least 16 kpc from the center. The morphology of the diffuse X-ray emission is spherically symmetrical. We could not recover a morphological resemblance between the soft X-ray emission and the ionization bicone that is traced by the [ O iii ] outflow.
Our spectral analysis indicates that one of the possible explanations for the extended emission is thermal emission from a low-density ( n_ H 10^ 3 cm^ 3 ) hot plasma ( T_ e 0.22 keV).
The source centroid found by wavdetect is 4:26:00.7, -57:12:02, which is consistent with the nominal coordinates of 1H 0419-577. We use these coordinates in the remainder of the analysis.
