For A1795Sy1 and A1367 we considered an energy range of (1-5) keV and for the other sources (0.5-7) keV . A1795Sy1, A1367 and A1795Quasar have been fitted with a power law with additional absorption from neutral hydrogen, Coma1 only with a power law and Coma2 with a power law and a Fe K line ( E=6.4 keV ).

Furthermore, in cases where the test statistic of the real data is very large (e.g. for the resid data of A1367) and the single coupling method does not return bounds, we are able to report bounds with the ApBC method.

sources.summary: The atlas gives 3C 264's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: 3C 264 is a FR I radio source coinciding with the elliptical galaxy NGC 3982 at a redshift of z= 0.0217 ( D 103 Mpc), containing a little-studied radio-loud AGN. do not give any optical classification for this object. Based on the absence of broad emission lines buttiglione_optical_2009 , we assume a tentative Sy 2/LINER classification for 3C 264.

sources.summary: The paper identifies 3C 264 as a distant TeV-detected radio galaxy and reports VERITAS observations yielding a 7.8-sigma detection and power-law VHE spectrum.

quotes.quote: Among TeV-detected radio galaxies, 3C 264 ( z=0.0217 , ) is the most distant, and is only the fourth known radio galaxy at these energies. VERITAS has collected 57 h good quality data between February 2017 and May 2019, which yielded a detection with a statistical significance of 7.8 . The VHE ray spectrum was well described by a power law with an index of 2.20 ± 0.27 and a flux of 0.7 of the Crab Nebula flux above 315 GeV .

gmw_00000057.name: 3C 285
gmw_00000057.overview: The double-lobed FR-II radio galaxy 3C 285 is a brightest cluster galaxy currently undergoing a major merger [arXiv:0908.4391, arXiv:0912.3001] that hosts a Seyfert 2 nucleus [arXiv:1310.2770]. It exhibits a high star-formation rate [arXiv:1410.8367] that may proceed within a compact molecular ring or disk [arXiv:1410.8367].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 3C 285 as a double-lobed FR-II radio galaxy with jet-associated star formation and a compact molecular ring or disk supporting a high star-formation rate.

quotes.quote: 3C 285 is a double-lobed powerful FR- 2 radio galaxy where both lobes have a complex filamentary structure. In the eastern radio lobe, there is a radio jet with unresolved radio knots vanBreugel_1993 . A slightly resolved object is located near the eastern radio jet (3C 285/09.6; vanBreugel_1993 ). 3C 285/09.6 is a small, kiloparsec-sized object where star formation seems to be triggered by the jet from the radio source 3C 285 vanBreugel_1993 .

Surprisingly for this kind of source, 3C 285 has a fairly high SFR of 15 M_ .yr^ 1 . With a depletion time of less than one Gyr, this source looks like typical star forming galaxies, as shown by its position in a KS-diagram. The simple analytical models that reproduce the CO line profiles are also the ones where the gas is lying in a compact molecular ring/disk of 1-2 kpc. So star formation may proceed in this very compact region hidden inside a larger dust-lane seen in the optical image and which crosses the entire galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper treats 09.6 as a compact star-forming object near the 3C 285 jet, with CO non-detection, galaxy-like depletion time, and possible small-galaxy remnant origin.

quotes.quote: A spot (named 09.6) aligned with the 3C 285 radio jet, at a projected distance of 70 kpc from the galaxy centre, shows star formation, detected in optical emission.

Interestingly, the spot 09.6 and MO are not detected in CO. The cold gas mass upper limits are consistent with a star formation induced by the compression of dense ambient material by the jet. The depletion time scale in 09.6 and MO are of the order of and even smaller than what is found in 3C 285, NGC 541 and local spiral galaxies ( 10^9 yr ). The molecular gas surface density in the 09.6 spot follows a Schmidt-Kennicutt law if the emitting region is very compact as suggested by the H emission, while MO is found to have a much higher SFE (very short depletion time).

09.6 could be the remnant of a small galaxy that has lost most of its gas in a tidal interaction and that is being compressed by the interaction with the 3C 285 radio-lobe. In the case of MO, the presence of atomic gas, the short depletion time scale and the very high sSFR may indicate a recent star forming event that has not produced many stars yet. While 09.6 has a stellar mass of 10^9 M_ , typical of a small galaxy, the MO is 100 time smaller with a stellar mass that only reaches 10^7 M_ .

sources.summary: The paper describes 3C 285 as a HEG with dust-affected line ratios, disturbed ionized gas associated with star formation or jet shocks, and extended [O III] emission beyond H.

quotes.quote: We find that such components are evident in three of the galaxies in our sample, 3C 33, 3C 136.1 and 3C 285. To further investigate the effects of dust on our emission line maps, we used the H and H flux values from B09+ to calculate the H /H ratio, which is sensitive to reddening. Assuming Case B recombination theory, this line ratio is 3 if reddening is present. We found line ratios significantly higher than this value for three of the galaxies in our sample (3C 136.1, 3C 285 and 3C 319) due to the dimmed H . For 3C 319 the spectrum is intrinsically weak and H and [ O III ] are not detected, probably due not to absorption. Since dust attenuation is more important at shorter wavelengths, the [ O III ] /H line ratios presented in that figure for 3C 33, 3C 136.1, and 3C 285 can be considered as lower limits.

The extended disturbed patches of ionized gas observed in 3C 33, 3C 234 and 3C 285 seem to be associated with regions of intense star formation, induced by jet shocks or by a tidal merger/interaction, as suggested by the HST images madrid06,floyd10,baldi08 . These sources are all classified as HEGs.

However, for at least 5 ( 26 ) galaxies in our sample, all HEGs (3C 33, 3C 180, 3C 227, 3C 234, 3C 285), [ O III ] extended emission is clearly observed at larger scales (on kpc scale) than that of H .

sources.summary: The review discusses 3C 285 as a candidate jet-induced star-formation radio source, but argues its jet knots do not coincide with or appear caused by the proposed environmental interaction.

quotes.quote: One place to look for such jet knots would be in sources where there is an independent suggestion of a jet/external medium interaction in the shape of `jet-induced star formation'. Well-studied candidates for this process include 3C 285 vd93 and 3C 34 blr97 . However, there is little evidence in any of these sources that the interaction, if present, affects the jet itself. In 3C 285, for example, the images of vd93 show the jet to contain several knots, but none is spatially coincident with the position of the star-forming object that is believed to be interacting with the jet (Fig. \ 285 ). Thus not only are the knots in the jet not apparently related to environmental interactions, but the proposed environmental interactions do not apparently produce knots.

sources.summary: The paper identifies 1319+42 with a group BCG and classical double-lobed radio galaxy, detecting a symmetric parsec-scale structure aligned with its kiloparsec jets.

quotes.quote: 1319+42 (3C 285) -- The host galaxy of 3C 285 has been identified with the BCG of a group of galaxies ( ). 3C 285 is a classical double-lobed radio galaxy of 190 arcsec total extension at 4.86 GHz, with two hotspots and an eastern ridge showing curvature roughly along the line to the optical companion ( ). In our VLBA image, the source is slightly resolved at the same PA as the kiloparsec-scale jets (Fig. f1319.1 ) with a symmetric structure. The total flux density is 4.7 mJy; very little arcsecond core flux is lost on the milliarcsecond-scale (S _ VLBA /S _ 80 )

sources.summary: 3C 285 is described as undergoing a major merger; the authors fit its X-ray ridge and link its starlight agreement to the ISM-optical correlation.

quotes.quote: 3C 285 ( z=0.079 ) : 3C 285 is currently experiencing a major merger, and instead of a well-defined ISM, we fit an ellipse to the ``ridge'' structure described in hardcastle07a . The ridge appears to be a unified structure (its X-ray properties do not differ along its length) and hardcastle07a argue that it is not produced by the interaction of the radio galaxy with its environment. They reason that the agreement with the starlight (and, in 3C 442A, the flow of material from tidal tails into a similar ridge) could not be generally predicted by the interaction of the radio galaxy with its environment. This is in agreement with our ISM--optical light correlation.

sources.summary: The atlas gives 3C 285's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: 3C 285 is a FR II radio source identified with the irregular galaxy LEDA 46625 (e.g., allen_ultraviolet_2002 ) at a redshift of z= 0.0794 ( D 378 Mpc), containing a radio-loud Sy 2 nucleus veron-cetty_catalogue_2010 . Its supergalactic-scale biconical radio lobes extend an in the east-west directions (PA 75 ; e.g., leahy_bridges_1984 ). The first successful MIR detection of 3C 285 was achieved with /IRAC, IRS and MIPS, where it appears nearly unresolved.

sources.summary: The paper favors significant star formation in 3C 285, obtaining an excellent YSP fit and modeling MIR/FIR dust components.

quotes.quote: 3C 285. Although a power law would be enough, there are good reasons to assume that significant star formation occurs (see Sect. discussion ). An excellent fit is obtained with a YSP, and according to Tadhunter et al. ( ), a YSP is present. MIR and FIR data are available, which can be fitted by a mix of dust with temperatures of 15 K ( M_d = 3.0 10^9 M_ ), 70 K ( M_d = 3.0 10^5 M_ ), and 180 K ( M_d = 4.7 10^2 M_ ).

gmw_00000058.name: 3C 293
gmw_00000058.overview: The radio galaxy 3C 293 is a post-merger remnant [arXiv:1609.06539, arXiv:1608.07057] hosting a LINER active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2303.07098, arXiv:1310.2770] and a rotating molecular disk [arXiv:2305.02007, arXiv:0811.2658]. It exhibits restarted jet activity [arXiv:1411.0361, arXiv:0903.5408] that drives a fast, shock-excited gas outflow [arXiv:1411.0361, arXiv:2303.07098].

sources.summary: The paper describes 3C 293 as a restarted CSS/FRI source likely merger-activated, with rotating H I absorption plus a broad blueshifted outflow driven by its western radio jet.

quotes.quote: 3C 293 This object is a compact steep spectrum (CSS) radio source, whose emission is divided in multiple knots Beswick2004 . It is a restarted radio source, possibly activated by a recent merger event   Heckman1986 . classify the radio source as FRI.

WSRT observations  Morganti2005 show an extremely broad absorption component at blueshifted velocities ( FWZI=1400 ). VLA-A array observations, with 1.2 1.3 arcsec of spatial resolution, identify this feature as a fast \ outflow pushed by the western radio jet, located at 500 pc from the core  Mahony2013 . The radio jet is thought to inject energy into the ISM, driving the outflow of \ at a rate of 8-50   . The broad shallow outflowing component is also detected. The fit of the spectrum with the BF identifies the rotating component, while it fails in fitting the shallow wings, highlighting the different nature of these clouds.

sources.summary: The paper presents 3C293 as a peculiar radio galaxy with restarted, two-sided jet activity traced from parsec to arcsecond scales.

quotes.quote: 1350+31 -- 3C293 This peculiar source has been studied in detail in the radio and optical bands. Recent results are discussed in and , where the source structure is analyzed from the sub-arcsec to the arcmin scale.

In Giovannini et al. 2005 ( , we presented a VLBI image at 5 GHz where a possible symmetric two-sided jet structure is visible at mas resolution. Because of the complex inner structure we observed again this source with the VLBA at 1.7 GHz in phase reference mode to properly map the inner region.

In agreement with the previous VLBA image at 5 GHz, the source shows a symmetric structure with two symmetric jets. The sub-arcsec VLA image at 5 GHz shows the bright inner two-sided jet with a more external fainter diffuse emission (Fig. 2). The brightness distribution suggests a two phase activity: the source emission after some time re-started with a higher activity with respect to the older emission.

sources.summary: The paper identifies 3C 293 as a post-coalescent radio-galaxy merger with distorted morphology, a close companion, a stellar bridge, and extended low-surface-brightness tail structure.

quotes.quote: 3C 293 Figure fig:3C293 shows the V-band image of 3C 293. The high-surface-brightness inner region displays a disc-like morphology, but with prominent dust-lanes better visualized in the HST imaging by bes02 . The low-surface-brightness envelope shows a distorted morphology, and a faint bridge connecting 3C 293 with the nearby small companion `A' at 13 kpc west-south-west of the nucleus of 3C 293.

The low-surface-brightness envelope of the optical emission around 3C 293 is extended in the direction of this companion A. About 70 kpc further to the west (beyond companion A) the low-surface-brightness tail shows a `zig-zag' pattern and ends in two knots (indicated as `C' in the top-right plot of Fig. fig:3C293 ). It is interesting to speculate that these two knots could represent star forming regions at the tip of the low-surface-brightness tail. No \ emission is detected, down to a 5 limit of 7.0 10^ 19 cm ^ 2 across 100 , outside the central 45 kpc region where the \ absorption-signal dominates.

sources.summary: 3C 293 is used as a radio-galaxy comparison case; the paper derives SPIRE CO fluxes, estimates low AGN fraction, and links enhanced warm CO to radio-jet-driven shocks.

quotes.quote: The fourth object is 3C 293, a radio galaxy rich in warm H _2 , possibly excited by radio jet-driven shocks (Ogle et al. 2010). From the archival SPIRE spectrum of 3C 293 (Obs. ID: 1342238242; PI: P. Papadopoulos), we derived the fluxes of 1.69, 2.91 and 2.14 10^ 18 W m ^ 2 for CO (6 5), CO (7 6) and CO (8 7), respectively, all at S/N 3 , but only the 3 upper limits of 5.49 and 3.15 10^ 18 W m ^ 2 for CO (5 4) and CO (10 9), respectively. Its F_ IR 3.07 10^ 14 W m ^ 2 , based on an 12 \ flux from Siebenmorgen et al. (2004) and IRAS flux densities at 25, 60 and 100 \ from Golombek, Miley Beugebauer (1988). The galaxy 3C 293 is shown in Fig. 4 by its possible range in R_ midCO .
In both NGC 1266 and 3C 293, our estimated f_ AGN < 10 , based on [ O iv ] / [ Ne ii ] from Dudik, Satyapal Marcu (2009) or Ogle et al. (2010).
Indeed, both NGC 1266 and 3C 293, where the warm CO emission might be enhanced respectively by the AGN- and radio jet-driven shocks, show a higher R_ midCO in Fig. 4.

sources.summary: The paper includes 3C 293 in the 3CR radio-galaxy polarimetry sample, maps its near-IR jet knots, measures lower knot polarizations than earlier work, and flags possible non-thermal core contribution.

quotes.quote: In addition, we have analysed archival observations of 3C 293, 3C 305 and 3C 405 (Cygnus A) extracted from the Mikulski Archive for Space Telescopes (MAST). They are included because these are the only other 3C PRG at similar redshifts observed with HST /NICMOS at 2.05 m in a similar way to our complete sample. These three additional objects, along with the 10 objects in the main sample, comprise an `extended sample' of 13 3CR radio galaxies, all located in the northern hemisphere.

For the case of 3C 293, the near-IR jet comprises three knots running from west to east, following the radio jet counterparts Bridle:1981 . We measured slightly lower polarisation than the reported by Floyd:2006 for the three knots: whereas Floyd:2006 measured 15 per cent polarisation for the three knots, we measured 12 per cent for the farthest knot and 5 per cent for the other two (see below).

This consistency suggests that the high nuclear polarisation is due to dichroic extinction (except for 3C 293, which is more likely to have a non-thermal component), the product of elongated dust grains aligned in a magnetised torus in the nuclear region of the galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper presents 3C 293 as a radio MOHEG where Chandra data show shock-heated nuclear and inner-jet gas; jet power can heat the host ISM, matching warm molecular gas.

quotes.quote: We present a 70 ks observation of the radio galaxy 3C 293. This galaxy belongs to the class of molecular hydrogen emission galaxies (MOHEGs) that have very luminous emission from warm molecular hydrogen.

Both the nucleus and the inner jets show evidence of 10^ 7 K shock-heated gas. The kinetic power of the jets is more than sufficient to heat the X-ray emitting gas within the host galaxy. The thermal X-ray and warm luminosities of 3C 293 are similar, indicating similar masses of X-ray hot gas and warm molecular gas. This is consistent with a picture where both derive from a multiphase, shocked interstellar medium (ISM).

sources.summary: The review describes 3C 293 as a restarted radio galaxy with multiple H I structures, large velocity gradients, complicated gas kinematics, and a fast outflow against the western inner lobe.

quotes.quote: In the case of the restarted radio galaxy 3C 293, at least two structures with different velocity widths, both having large-scale velocity gradients, are seen against the kpc-sized continuum Beswick04 . One is seen against the eastern radio jet co-spatial with the location of dust lanes observed by the HST and which has a small velocity gradient of 50 \ arcsec ^ 1 , consistent with the velocity gradient observed for the ionised gas. The second structure, with possibly a larger velocity gradient, could be due to a circumnnuclear disk on scales of 400 pc. Also in the restarted, giant galaxy 3C 236 (see Tremblay10 for an overview of its properties) a velocity gradient is observed which, also in this case, seems to correspond to the dust-lane, the ionised gas disk and the molecular gas disk. Clouds of \ are also detected with masses of a few 10^4 \ see recently confirmed by Schulz et al. 2018 Struve12 . In both 3C 236 and 3C 293 the overall distribution and kinematics of the gas is complicated by the presence of fast \ outflows, see sec:outflows .
Also in the case of 3C 293 (see Fig. \ fig:3C293outflow right), the strongest outflow of 1000 \ is seen against the western inner radio lobe, about 0.5 kpc in size Morganti03,Mahony13 .

sources.summary: The paper presents 3C 293 as a gas-rich AGN host with broad H I absorption, molecular absorption components, and CO morphology suggesting jet-ISM interaction and outflow.

quotes.quote: Recently, very broad absorption extending to the blue wing was observed in the HI line towards 3C 293 by Morganti et al (2003): the total width of 1400km/s absorption implies neutral gas entrained in the radio jet towards the observer. This observation is confirming theoretical expectations of AGN feedback on the interstellar medium of the host galaxies. The 3C 293 host galaxy has already been observed in the CO line, and found quite rich in molecular gas (Evans et al 1999).

Garcia-Burillo et al (2006) have observed this strong radio source at and with the IRAM interferometer, and found several absorption components, in CO, HCO ^+ and HCN. The high resolution helps to disentangle absorption from emission in the nucleus. The shape of the CO emission map suggests an interaction between the jet and the ISM, able to redirect the jet, and produce the HI outflow.

sources.summary: The paper treats 3C 293 as a restarting radio galaxy with recurrent nuclear activity, compact inner emission peaks, and unusually abundant molecular gas relative to the other restarting sources.

quotes.quote: We have included from the literature four radio galaxies (NGC 315, 4C 29.30, 3C 293 and NGC 6251) that were observed in the CO J(1--0) and CO J(2--1) line transitions. Two are normal giant radio galaxies (NGC 315, NGC 6251) that do not show evidence for restarting of nuclear activity and two, 4C 29.30 and 3C 293 exhibit strong evidence for a recurrence in their nuclear activity (Jamrozy et al. 2006, submitted; Akujor et al. 1996).

3C 236 and 3C 293 are found to have such a pair of emission peaks well within their central compact cores (Schilizzi et al. 2001; Akujor et al. 1996). The restarting radio galaxies all have upper limits that are lower than the molecular gas mass found in 3C 293. If they possessed gas masses at a level of or above that found in 3C 293 they would have been detected. With 3C 293, the only example of a restarting radio galaxy, setting the standard for fuel content in this class of sources, that of abundant molecular gas content, our observations have clarified that 3C 293 may be atypical and that abundant molecular gas content of the level of 10^ 9 M_ does not necessarily accompany the nuclear restarting phenomenon.

sources.summary: The paper revisits 1350+31 with 1.6 GHz VLBA data, confirming a symmetric two-sided parsec jet and relating its inner structure to restarted AGN activity.

quotes.quote: 1350+31 ( 3C 293 ) This peculiar source has been studied in detail in the radio and optical bands. Recent results are presented in and , where the source structure is discussed from the sub-arcsec to the arcminute scale. In , we presented a VLBI image at 5 GHz where a possible symmetric two-sided jet structure is present at mas resolution. Because of the complex nature of the inner structure, we re-observed the source with the VLBA at 1.6 GHz in phase reference mode. We show the new 1.6 GHz image of the core in Fig. f1350.1 .

sources.summary: The paper includes 3C 293 among nearby radio galaxies with fast ionized and atomic outflows, warm turbulent H2 emission, and blue-shifted mid-infrared line wings linked to jet feedback.

quotes.quote: We present mid-IR Spitzer spectroscopy of 8 nearby radio-galaxies where fast outflows of ionized and atomic gas were detected by previous optical and radio observations. Our main results and conclusions are the following: In all of the sources, we detect high equivalent width H _2 line emission from warm (100-5000 K) molecular gas, implying warm H _2 masses ranging from 10^8 to 10^ 10 M _ . The observed H _2 to-PAH luminosity ratios are all above the values predicted from photo-ionization models, and we suggest that the H _2 emission is associated with the dissipation of a fraction of the kinetic energy provided by the radio jet.

In 5 sources (3C 236, 3C 293, 3C 459, 4C 12.50 and PKS 1549-79), we securely detect blue-shifted wings (up to 3000 km s ^ 1 ) on the [ Ne ii ] (and [ Ne iii ] for the highest SNR spectra) that matches remarkably well with the blue-shifted, broad H i absorption associated with the outflow. All but one of these sources have resolved and very broad mid-IR rotational H _2 lines with FWHM 500 km s ^ 1 (and up to 900 km s ^ 1 ), compared to only 2 overall amongst the 298 AGN with such spectra in the Spitzer archive Dasyra2011 .

sources.summary: The paper models 3C 293/UGC 8782 as a post-merger winged radio galaxy whose outer and inner lobes imply very recent jet realignment.

quotes.quote: Here we analyse the complex radio structure of 3C 293 winged source hosted by the post-merger galaxy UGC 8782, which uniquely displays a significant asymmetry between the sizes (and therefore the ages) of the two pairs of lobes, indicating that an episode of jet realignment took place only very recently. This allows us to tightly constrain the corresponding timescales, and therefore to discriminate between different models proposed for the formation of X-shaped radio galaxies in general. Based on all the available and carefully re-analysed radio data for 3C 293, we have performed a detailed spectral modelling for the older and younger lobes in the system, using the existing evolutionary DYNAGE algorithm. In this way we derived the lobes' ages and jet energetics, which we then compared to the accretion power in the source. We found that the 200 kpc-scale outer lobes of 3C 293 are 60 Myr old and, until very recently, have been supplied with fresh electrons and magnetic field by the jets, i.e., jet activity related to the formation of the outer lobes ceased within the last Myr.

sources.summary: The paper maps UGC 8782, 3C 293, as a radio-loud LINER merger remnant with disturbed gas kinematics, jet-driven outflows, and AGN/shock excitation.

quotes.quote: We use optical Integral Field Spectroscopy (IFU) to study the gas emission structure and kinematics in the inner 3.4 4.9 kpc ^2 region of the galaxy UGC 8782 (3C 293), host of a radio loud Active Galactic Nucleus (AGN). The observations were performed with the GMOS-IFU on the Gemini North telescope, resulting in a spatial resolution of 725 pc at the distance of the galaxy. While the stars present ordered rotation following the orientation of the large scale disc, the gas shows a disturbed kinematics. The emission-line profiles present two kinematic components: a narrow ( 200 km s ^ 1 ) component associated with the gas in the disc of the galaxy and a broad ( 200 km s ^ 1 ) component produced by gas outflows. Emission-line ratio diagrams indicate that the gas in the disc is excited by the AGN radiation field, while the emission of the outflow includes additional contribution of shock excitation due to the interaction of the radio jet with the environment gas.

sources.summary: The paper treats 3C293 as a compact misaligned double-double radio galaxy with young inner lobes, older outer lobes, a short jet-interruption timescale, and detected CO gas.

quotes.quote: The size of the outer doubles is usually large, often over a Mpc, although the highly misaligned DDRG 3C293 has an overall size of only 190 kpc and the possibility that the GPS object CTA21 with an overall linear size of 0.3 kpc might exhibit evidence of episodic activity has been explored .

Considering smaller sources, the total projected linear size of the inner double of of the highly misaligned DDRG 3C293 (J1352+316) is 4.2 kpc, while that of the outer double is 190 kpc. The spectral ages of the inner and outer doubles, and the existence of a hot-spot suggest that the time scale of interruption of jet activity is less than 0.1 Myr .
The only detection was 3C293, which was the only FRII radio galaxy known to have CO in an earlier survey of IRAS flux-density limited survey of radio galaxies .

sources.summary: J1352+3126 is described as a postmerger Seyfert 2, consistent with a late-type spiral, whose 3C 293 radio source has a one-sided FR II jet emitting across bands.

quotes.quote: J1352+3126 This is a Seyfert 2 galaxy whose optical spectrum increases toward longer wavelengths. It is a postmerger object, with the merged object being consistent with a late-type spiral galaxy. The radio source is also known as 3C 293, and it exhibits a one-sided jet. The latter shows emissions in optical, NIR, and UV and has a FR II structure .

sources.summary: The paper re-detects broad, multi-component H I absorption in 3C 293, an FR II radio galaxy with a disk, optical jet or tidal tail, companion, and radio-jet-driven outflow.

quotes.quote: 3C 293 is a Fanaroff and Riley type II (FR II) 10.1093/mnras/167.1.31P radio galaxy, with a compact core of steep spectrum and multiple knots in the radio lobe 1999ApJ...511..730E,2004MNRAS.352...49B . Both emission and absorption in CO have been detected 1999ApJ...511..730E , showing that 3C 293 is a disk galaxy with an optical jet or tidal tail toward its companion galaxy. The outflow of had also been detected, which is probably driven by the radio jet 2013MNRAS.435L..58M .
Besides, an extremely broad and multi-components absorption feature has been detected 1981ApJ...243L.143B,2004evn..conf..147B,2004MNRAS.352...49B , indicating a rotating disk.

sources.summary: The atlas identifies 3C 293 with UGC 08782, describing it as a merger remnant with optical and infrared distortion and prominent dust lanes partly covering an infrared-detected optical jet.

quotes.quote: 3C 293 (UGC 08782); z=0.045034 A merger-remnant with significant distortion at optical and IR wavelengths. The prominent optical dust lanes partially cover an optical jet that is clearly seen in the infrared. This object was studied in detail by floyd+06a .

sources.summary: The paper presents 3C 293 as a nearby FR II radio galaxy with a massive, regularly rotating molecular disk and dynamically relaxed molecular-gas kinematics.

quotes.quote: We also report on the detection of a massive (M _ gas 10 ^ 10 M _ ) regularly rotating 7 kpc-size disk in the FR II radio galaxy 3C 293.
3C 293 is a nearby ( z 0.045) moderately large ( 200 kpc) FR II radio galaxy located at the nucleus of the elliptical vv5-33-12.
In the case of 3C 293, the distribution and kinematics of molecular gas are reminiscent of a regularly rotating and dynamically relaxed disk.

sources.summary: 3C 293 has weak X-ray emission associated with its radio galaxy, distinct hot spots, small extended ISM emission, and a merging two-nucleus host.

quotes.quote: 3C 293 ( z=0.045 ) : The X-ray emission associated with the radio galaxy is quite weak, but the hot spots are distinct and there is a small amount of extended ISM emission. Unfortunately, the spatial extent of this diffuse emission is small compared to the optical isophotes, so the disagreement between the _ X-ray and _ DSS/SDSS (measured at a somewhat larger radius) is not especially surprising. Notably, the host galaxy has two nuclei and is in the process of merging martel99 .

sources.summary: The atlas gives 3C 293's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: 3C 293 is a FR I radio source coinciding with the irregular galaxy UGC 8782 at a redshift of z= 0.045 ( D 311 Mpc) and an AGN optically classified as a LINER veron-cetty_catalogue_2010 . It features a complex radio morphology with asymmetrical supergalactic-scale radio lobes and a misaligned inner two-sided jet (PA 55 and PA 85 respectively; e.g., akujor_two-sided_1996,beswick_high-resolution_2004,giovannini_bologna_2005 ). The jet is even visible in the UV, optical and near-infrared floyd_jet_2006 .

sources.summary: B2 1350+31/3C 293 is described as having a young stellar population, MIR/FIR dust components, and enhanced star formation despite LEG classification.

quotes.quote: B2 1350+31 (3C 293). According to Tadhunter et al. ( ), a YSP is present. MIR and FIR data are available, which can be fitted by a mix of dust with temperatures of 60 K ( M_d = 1.0 10^7 M_ ), 155 K ( M_d = 6.5 10^2 M_ ) and 800 K ( M_d = 0.4 M_ ).
We note, however, that B2 1141+37, B2 1430+25, B2 1350+31 (3C 293), and 3C 321 (Holt et al. ; Tadhunter et al. ) are LEGs with enhanced star formation (see Table tab:sfagn ).

gmw_00000059.name: 3C 305
gmw_00000059.overview: 3C 305 is a nearby Seyfert 2 radio galaxy [arXiv:1310.2770, arXiv:1504.02772] and merger remnant [arXiv:0712.0595, arXiv:1205.0962] featuring a disturbed host with a nuclear dust lane [arXiv:0712.0595, arXiv:1504.02772] and two tidal arms [arXiv:1609.06539]. Its double-sided radio jets [arXiv:1310.2770, arXiv:1504.02772] interact with the interstellar medium [arXiv:1001.2389, arXiv:1307.7489], driving a multiphase outflow of neutral, ionized, and molecular gas [arXiv:1201.1503, arXiv:1205.0962].

sources.summary: The paper describes 3C 305 as an HEG with a narrow rotating-gas H I component, detected jet-driven H I outflow, high outflow column density and mass, X-ray jet power, and molecular gas.

quotes.quote: 3C 305 - IC 1065 The \ profile of this source shows a deep, narrow component, which could be associated with rotating gas. Furthermore, reported the presence of a jet-driven \ outflow in this galaxy. The outflow is also detected in our observations, and it is successfully fitted by the BF. The column density of the outflow is N( ) = 2 10^ 21 , assuming T_ spin = 1000 K, and the corresponding \ mass was estimated to be M( ) = 1.3 10^ 7 M _ Morganti2005 . X-ray observations suggest that the power supplied by the radio jet to the \ outflow is 10^ 43 erg s ^ 1 Hardcastle2012 .

Molecular \ gas was also detected in this source by . However the molecular phase of the gas is inefficiently coupled to the AGN jet-driven outflow. classified this source as a high excitation galaxy (HEG).

sources.summary: The paper presents 3C 305 as a disturbed radio galaxy with tidal arms, changing major-axis orientation, central absorption/outflow evidence, and a possible gas-rich companion interaction.

quotes.quote: 3C 305 Figure fig:3C305 shows the V-band image of 3C 305. The low-surface-brightness image shows two tidal arms stretching in east-west direction. The major axis of the galaxy seems to turn from 145 ^ in the low-surface-brightness plot (top-middle) to 70 ^ in the high-surface-brightness plot (bottom-middle). The inner region also shows a distorted morphology, with arc- or spiral-like isophotes.

North-east of 3C 305, at 133 kpc distance, a companion galaxy is detected in \ emission (M _ HI = 4.2 10 ^ 9 M _ ). The \ velocity of this companion is v = 12,560 . This is very close to v _ sys = 12,550 \ for 3C 305 mor05 . Part of the \ appears marginally elongated in the direction of 3C 305, as shown by the lowest contours in the position-velocity plot of the \ gas in Fig. fig:3C305 (right). If confirmed, it would suggest that an interaction occurred between 3C 305 and this gas-rich companion.

sources.summary: 3C 305 is described as a radio galaxy where broad H I absorption and ionized gas outflows occur off-nucleus at the northeast radio lobe, indicating jet-ISM interaction.

quotes.quote: One of the best cases is the radio galaxy 3C 305 (Morganti et al. 2005b), shown in Figure fig4 . The broad \ absorption is found 1.6 kpc off-nucleus at the location of the northeast radio lobe. This is the same location where an outflow of ionized gas occurs (Jackson et al. 1995).

Further support for this hypothesis comes from the Chandra detection of hot, X-ray emitting gas (Massaro et al. 2009) that has been interpreted as thermal emission due to the interaction between matter outflows and shock-heated environment gas. The fact that the two phases of the gas (cold neutral and warm ionized) show similar kinematics, suggests that these outflows are produced by the same mechanism. This is a recurrent characteristic in all the best-studied cases. The characteristics and the location of the outflow in 3C 305 --- but also in other cases like 3C 293 and IC 5063 (Morganti et al. 2007) --- strongly suggests that the outflow is produced by the interaction between the radio jet and the ISM .

sources.summary: The paper presents Gemini spectra of 3C 305's EELR, concluding that jet-driven shocks disturb the gas and that low-density auto-ionizing shock models explain the emission and pressure balance.

quotes.quote: We present new Gemini spectroscopical data of the Extended Emission-Line Region of 3C 305 radio galaxy in order to achieve the final answer of the long-standing question about the ionizing mechanism. The spectra show strong kinematic disturbances within the most intense line-emitting region. The relative intensities amongst the emission lines agree with the gas being shocked during the interaction of the powerful radio jets with the ambient medium. The emission from the recombination region acts as a very effective cooling mechanism, which is supported by the presence of a neutral outflow. However, the observed intensity is almost an order of magnitude lower than expected in a pure shock model.

So auto-ionizing shock models, in low-density and low-abundance regime, are required in order to account for the observed emission within the region. This scenario also supports the hypothesis that the optical emitting gas and the X-ray plasma are in pressure balance.

sources.summary: The paper includes 3C 305 among nearby radio galaxies with fast outflows and reports broad, resolved H2 emission showing turbulent warm molecular gas associated with jet energy deposition.

quotes.quote: We present mid-IR Spitzer spectroscopy of 8 nearby radio-galaxies where fast outflows of ionized and atomic gas were detected by previous optical and radio observations. Our main results and conclusions are the following: In all of the sources, we detect high equivalent width H _2 line emission from warm (100-5000 K) molecular gas, implying warm H _2 masses ranging from 10^8 to 10^ 10 M _ . The observed H _2 to-PAH luminosity ratios are all above the values predicted from photo-ionization models, and we suggest that the H _2 emission is associated with the dissipation of a fraction of the kinetic energy provided by the radio jet.

All the sources, except OQ 208, have at least one broad H _2 line with intrinsic FWHM > 430 km s ^ 1 . 3C 293, 3C 305, 3C 459, 4C 12.50, and IC 5063 show two or more resolved H _2 lines. These results show that the warm H _2 gas is very turbulent in most of the sources.

sources.summary: The paper presents 3C 305 as a young, merger-driven radio galaxy whose jets shock the host ISM, producing X-ray gas, depolarization, and a multiphase outflow.

quotes.quote: We present Chandra X-ray and VLA radio observations of the radio galaxy 3C 305. The X-ray observations reveal the details of the previously known extended X-ray halo around the radio galaxy. We show using X-ray spectroscopy that the X-ray emission is consistent with being shock-heated material and can be modelled with standard collisional-ionization models, rather than being photoionized by the active nucleus.

3C 305 is a low-redshift radio galaxy which has been extensively studied because of its peculiar radio structure (Heckman \ 1982, hereafter H82): its bright jets and hotspots are embedded in a pair of lobes which are much longer perpendicular to the jet axis than the core-hotspot distance. Its 178-MHz radio luminosity is 5.5 10^ 24 W Hz ^ 1 sr ^ 1 , which puts it right on the FRI/FRII luminosity boundary of Fanaroff Riley (1974).

3C 305 could be considered as a prototypical example of a young, galaxy-scale powerful radio source driven by galaxy-galaxy interactions, as expected in models of galaxy formation in the early universe, and, as emphasised by Morganti \ (2005b), the presence of both warm and cold phases of the gas in the fast outflow already represents a challenge for models describing the interaction between radio jets and their environment.

sources.summary: The review describes 3C 305 as a radio galaxy where broad H I absorption arises against the eastern lobe, linking neutral and ionized gas to a jet-driven outflow.

quotes.quote: High-spatial resolution 21-cm \ VLA observations were obtained for the radio galaxy 3C 305 (Morganti et al. 2005c). These new high-resolution data show that the 1000 \ broad \ absorption, earlier detected in low-resolution WSRT observations, is occurring against the bright, eastern radio lobe, about 1.6 kpc from the nucleus (see Fig. 9). We also used new optical spectra taken with the WHT to make a detailed comparison of the kinematics of the neutral hydrogen with that of the ionised gas (see Fig. 10).

The striking similarity between the complex kinematics of the two gas phases suggests that both the ionised gas and the neutral gas are part of the same outflow. Earlier studies of the ionised gas (Heckman et al. 1982; Jackson et al. 2003) had already found evidence for a strong interaction between the radio jet and the ISM at the location of the eastern radio lobe. These results show that the fast outflow produced by this interaction also contains a component of neutral atomic hydrogen. The most likely interpretation is that the radio jet ionises the ISM and accelerates it to the high outflow velocities observed.

sources.summary: The appendix reports 3C 305 as a broad CO(1-0) detection with disturbed optical dust and continuum structure possibly related to obscured near-nuclear emission.

quotes.quote: 3C 305: IC 1065. In the CO(1-0) spectra, the detection is very clear, with a broad velocity width of 600 km/s. We have no data for the CO(2-1) emission line. The HST image presented by shows filamentary and disturbed swaths of dust stretching across the western side of the galaxy and a twisting ``arm'' of emission in the eastern side. noticed a 1 scale cone-line extension of the continuum emission north of the core, in a direction almost 90 ^ from the radio axis.

sources.summary: The paper identifies 3C 305 with IC 1065/UGC 09553 as a nearby FR I merger remnant with nuclear dust lane and a large elongated disturbed elliptical host.

quotes.quote: 3C 305 (IC 1065; UGC 09553); z=0.041639 A nearby FR I and merger remnant, with prominent dust lane across the nucleus in the optical. Studied in detail by jackson+03 . The host galaxy is a large, elongated elliptical, with clear signs of disturbance in the optical and the IR. See jackson+03 for original publication of the data, and a detailed study of the object.

sources.summary: The paper describes 1448+63 as a well-known double radio source, undetected in its nucleus at 1.6 GHz but showing short-baseline components likely corresponding to hotspots.

quotes.quote: 1448+63 (3C 305) -- A well known double source, recently studied in detail by , it was only marginally detected in previous VLBA 5 GHz observations ( ). In our new observations at 1.6 GHz the nuclear source is not detected, but there is a clear detection on the shortest baselines. The visibilities are well described by a two component model, separated by 1.5 . These are probably the two hot spots visible in the MERLIN images (see ).

sources.summary: The atlas gives 3C 305's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: 3C 305 is a steep-spectrum FR I radio source coinciding with the peculiar galaxy IC 1065 at a redshift of z= 0.0416 ( D 192 Mpc) and a radio-loud Sy 2 nucleus veron-cetty_catalogue_2010 . It possesses a compact radio morphology with both FR I and FR II characteristics, featuring a two-sided jet extending along a north-east axis to the biconical radio lobes, which are separated by 4 3.4 kpc (PA 54 ; heckman_optical_1982,jackson_observations_2003 ). The first successful MIR N band observation was performed with /ISOCAM siebenmorgen_isocam_2004 and followed up with /IRAC, IRS and MIPS observations.

sources.summary: The paper reports a strong point source in 3C 305, negative X-ray-luminosity extinction, and likely underestimated Compton-thick X-ray luminosity.

quotes.quote: It is notable that, in the case of 3C 305 and 3C 433 the point sources are so strong that other features of the PSF are clearly visible.

It is interesting that 3C 305 gives a negative optical extinction based in the X-ray luminosity ( A_V( L_ X-ray )=-21.3 9.0 mag) consistent with the unabsorbed power law fitted to its X-ray spectrum (see next subsection).
It is very likely, based on its emission line properties, that the X-ray luminosity of 3C 305 is substantially underestimated due to it being Compton thick (see Subsection X-ray:luminosity ).

sources.summary: J1449+6316 is described as a Seyfert 2 whose disturbed host has twisted isophotes, spiral arms, a nuclear dust lane, and double-sided FR I radio jets.

quotes.quote: J1449+6316 This is a Seyfert 2 galaxy. Its host has a disturbed morphology, and shows isophotal twists and two spiral arms, with a thin dust lane that crosses the nuclear region. The radio source has an FR I morphology with double-sided jets .

sources.summary: The paper uses 3C-305 as a nearby radio-galaxy example where a jet appears to strike a disk, dislodging cold gas and accelerating ionised and neutral hydrogen.

quotes.quote: The observations show ionised and neutral hydrogen co-spatial with the radio cocoon of 3C-305. The radio morphology suggests that the jet is hitting a disc and knocking off some cold gas. This seems to be accelerated to high outflow velocities, where both, neutral and ionised gas is observed.

gmw_00000060.name: 3C 317
gmw_00000060.overview: UGC 9799, which hosts the radio source 3C 317, is a central cD elliptical galaxy [arXiv:1105.4572, arXiv:1110.1275] located in the Abell 2052 cluster [arXiv:1801.05435, arXiv:1110.1275]. The galaxy features a LINER-like active nucleus [arXiv:1303.0884, arXiv:1310.2770], a modest star formation rate [arXiv:1612.08089], and a peculiar ultraviolet filament likely triggered by a recent merger [arXiv:0712.0595].

sources.summary: The paper reobserves UGC09799 with deeper IFU data, finding centrally condensed continuum emission, multi-component forbidden-line profiles, H detection, gas rotation, and LINER-like central emission.

quotes.quote: UGC09799 IFU observations of this galaxy (around H and H ) was previously presented by Edwards et al. \ (2009). The observations presented in this study are improved in that it was observed with triple the integration time of the previous observations. The morphology of the continuum emission of this galaxy (see Figure fig:Thumbnails ) is centrally concentrated and condensed, as was also found by Edwards et al. \ (2009). Just fitting one single Gaussian per emission line resulted in very poor fits (as shown in Figure fig:UGC_before_after ). We also tried fitting Voigt profiles to the [ OIII ] lines, for example, as well as slightly offset blue and red velocity wings.

The best fits were consistently achieved using 2 Gaussians for each individual line in the [ OIII ] , [ OI ] , [ NII ] and [ SII ] doublets, of which one of the Gaussians were broader than the other. The H lines (and therefore also H ) however required no additional Gaussian to achieve a good fit. Edwards et al. \ (2009) could not detect H emission above the 1 level for this galaxy.

sources.summary: MUSE observations of 3C317/UGC 9799 show ionized filaments around the northern radio lobe, strong north-south line-emission asymmetry, split-line kinematics, and a 265 km s^-1 expanding nebula linked to AGN radio feedback.

quotes.quote: We report observations of the radio galaxy 3C317 (at z=0.0345) located at the center of the Abell cluster A2052, obtained with the VLT/MUSE integral field spectrograph. The Chandra images of this cluster show cavities in the X-ray emitting gas, which were produced by the expansion of the radio lobes inflated by the active galactic nucleus (AGN). Our exquisite MUSE data show with unprecedented detail the complex network of line emitting filaments enshrouding the northern X-ray cavity.

MUSE data have provided new and detailed insights into the physical properties of the WIM of UGC 9799, the central galaxy in the Abell cluster 2052, with full 3D coverage of the X-ray cavities. The ionized gas forms a filamentary structure surrounding the northern radio lobe. No emission is seen instead on the southern side with an asymmetry between the sides higher than a factor 75. The gas velocity field is indicative of an expanding nebula and we found regions where the emission lines are split, marking the locations where the two sides project onto each other. The maximum observed velocity is 265 .

sources.summary: The paper describes 3C317 as a cD-associated FRI core-halo radio galaxy in A2052, with a steep halo hiding a flat-spectrum active nucleus and a bent parsec-scale jet.

quotes.quote: 3C317 is one of the best studied radio sources in our VLBI sample. It is associated with the cD galaxy UGC 9799, at the centre of the cooling flow cluster of galaxies A2052. Its radio power is typical of FRI radio galaxies. The source was imaged with the VLA in a wide range of frequencies and studied in detail by Zhao et al. (1993). According to its morphology it can be classified as core-halo, in that it is characterised by a compact region surrounded by a steep spectrum low brightness radio halo.

At milliarcsecond resolution the source is dominated by a compact component and a jet, bending at 20 mas from the nucleus (i.e. 20 pc). The images shown in Figures fig:3c317_6l and fig:3c317_6h are very similar to the arcsecond scale ones presented in Zhao et al. (1993), i.e. the jet bends in an anti-clockwise direction on both scales.
We conclude therefore that the steep spectrum radio halo ``hides'' an active nucleus with flat spectrum.

sources.summary: UGC 9799 is described as Abell 2052's central brightest galaxy, with gas, nuclear activity, star formation, and an extremely rich globular cluster system of about 18,000 clusters within 110 kpc.

quotes.quote: UGC 9799 is the central and brightest galaxy in Abell 2052 at a distance d = 150 Mpc (for H_0 = 70 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 ).

The core of UGC 9799 shows clear evidence for gas and (modest) nuclear activity. As a radio source it is 3C 317, and Chandra observations reveal a compact core X-ray source donato_etal2004,balmaverde_etal2006 . At the center, GALEX ultraviolet profiles and infrared excess lead to a deduced present-day star formation rate of 1 M_ y ^ 1 hicks_etal2010,hoffer_etal2012 , while nuclear optical emission lines and a small star-forming filament visible in the near-UV further confirm star-forming activity buttiglione_etal2010,martel_etal2002 ; the filament is also visible in our F475W image.

In UGC 9799, the total population of GCs out to R = 150'' (the limit of the ACS field of view, as well as the limit of the halo-light photometry) is n = 9650 190 brighter than I = 27.0 from integration of the radial profile. This limit is 0.12 mag fainter than the GCLF peak (Paper I) and thus should include 54 of the total over all luminosities assuming the GCLF is Gaussian in number per unit magnitude, and using the parameters from Paper I. We therefore obtain N_ tot = 18000 400 for R < 150'' (110 kpc).

sources.summary: The paper presents 3C317 as a radio source in A2052 whose radio plasma creates X-ray cavities, extends beyond them, and contains a strong core with a pc-scale jet.

quotes.quote: 3C317 in A2052 is a striking example of cavities in the X-ray loud gas (apparent in Fig. 1) which have been created by the radio source (shown in Fig. 2). The clear cavities tell us that the X-ray and radio plasmas are separated rather than well mixed. The brighter north-south regions of the radio halo coincide with the X-ray cavities, but the radio halo also extends beyond the cavity edges, into the less disturbed regions of the cooling core. This radio source has a strong radio core and a pc-scale jet. No jet has been detected on VLA scales (kpc or greater), but the bright radio ``tail'' to the south is suggestive of a semi-collimated flow from the core into the radio halo.

sources.summary: The paper observes Abell 2052-BCG as a brightest cluster galaxy, mapping its central ionized gas and deriving an upper-limit supermassive black hole mass near 7.3e9 solar masses.

quotes.quote: We observed a sample of three Brightest Cluster Galaxies (BCGs), Abell 1836-BCG, Abell 2052-BCG, and Abell 3565-BCG, with the Advanced Camera for Surveys (ACS) and the Imaging Spectrograph (STIS) on board the Space Telescope. For each target galaxy we obtained high-resolution spectroscopy of the H and [ N II ] 6583 emission lines at three slit positions, to measure the central ionized-gas kinematics. ACS images in three different filters (F435W, F625W, and FR656N) have been used to determine the optical depth of the dust, stellar mass distribution near the nucleus, and intensity map.
For the SBH of Abell 2052-BCG, we found M_ 7.3 10^9 M _ .
In the case of Abell 2052-BCG we find M_ 7.3 10^9 M _ , following the method by Sarzi et al. (2002).

sources.summary: The paper identifies 3C 317 with Abell 2052/UGC 09799, describing an elongated elliptical with unresolved nucleus, nearby ellipticals, and a UV filament linked to merger-triggered star formation.

quotes.quote: 3C 317 (Abell 2052; UGC 09799); z=0.034457 3C 317 is a slightly elongated elliptical with an unresolved nucleus. Two small elliptical galaxies are present in the field of view, one is located 7 to the northeast, and the second one 9 to the northwest. The image of this galaxy taken by martel+02 shows a peculiar UV filament 4 kpc south of the nucleus. This filament is described as a region of active star formation likely triggered by a recent merger by martel+02 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies UGC 09799 as the central cD galaxy hosting 3C 317, describes its optical orientation, places inner X-ray bubbles within it, and notes its peculiar velocity.

quotes.quote: Its central radio source, 3C 317, is hosted by the central cD galaxy, UGC 09799.
The central cD galaxy is oriented in the NE-SW direction in the optical. The inner bubbles seen in the X-ray emission are within the cD galaxy.

There is also evidence of dynamical activity in the cluster from optical spectroscopy: the central cD has a fairly large peculiar velocity ( 290 90 km s ^ 1 ) relative to the cluster mean (Oergerle Hill 2001).

sources.summary: The paper describes UGC9799/3C 317 as the cD central elliptical of Abell 2052, with X-ray excess, an AGN/radio source, outer halo, and Sersic fits.

quotes.quote: UGC9799 (3C 317) is the central elliptical galaxy (cD morphological class; cf. tex:SG07 ) of the cooling flow cluster, Abell 2052. It features a central X-ray excess and is host to an active galactic nucleus (AGN) evident as a bright, steep-spectrum radio source (e.g. tex:ZS93 ) with a compact optical counterpart ( tex:RG03,tex:SG07 ). tex:SG07 and tex:DC07 also identify a distinct outer halo to UGC 9799 (consistent with its classification as a cD morphological type) in Jacobus Kapteyn Telescope R band and HST NICMOS H band (F160W) imaging respectively. After masking the inner 1-2 arcsec (to exclude the central AGN) and modelling the outer halo with an exponential light distribution, these authors recovered S e rsic index fits of n=1.2 and 2.3 to its major-axis surface brightness profile respectively Both tex:SG07 and tex:DC07 derived their major-axis surface brightness profiles via fitting of `perfect' (i.e., non-boxy/disky) elliptical isophotes to their galaxy images using IRAF ELLIPSE .

sources.summary: The atlas gives 3C 317's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: 3C 317 is an ultra-steep spectrum FR I radio source in the early-type galaxy UGC 9799 at a redshift of z= 0.0345 ( D 160 Mpc), the central galaxy of the cluster Abell 2052. It contains a radio-loud AGN, optically classified either as Sy 2 veron-cetty_catalogue_2010 or LINER simpson_emission-line_1996 . Instead of twin jets or lobes, 3C 317 displays an amorphous radio structure with a bright core zhao_3c_1993 .

gmw_00000061.name: 3C 321
gmw_00000061.overview: The double-nucleus radio galaxy 3C 321 is an extended Fanaroff-Riley II source [arXiv:astro-ph/0005228, arXiv:0908.4391] undergoing a merger that has triggered starburst activity [arXiv:0908.4391]. This interaction with a companion galaxy has disrupted one of its radio jets [arXiv:0707.1866] and produced extended dust absorption [arXiv:1001.5009].

sources.summary: The paper treats 3C 321 as a radio galaxy where a moving gas-rich galaxy sharply bent the western jet, leaving both active and relic lobe-like structures.

quotes.quote: An additional impetus for our pursuing the jet--shell collision scenario for XRGs comes from the clear detection of a jet--cloud collision in the radio galaxy 3C 321 at a redshift z = 0.096 (Fig. \ 1 of Evans et al. \ 2008). It is seen that the western jet of this powerful RG, while being impacted by the ISM of a northward moving gas-rich galaxy, has undergone a sharp bend (by 40^ ). Both this deflected jet plume, which lacks a terminal hot spot, and the collimated main jet which has, post-interruption, resumed its original course and terminates in a radio/X-ray hot spot, are presently visible in this source. Evans et al. have estimated that the jet's interruption by the moving galaxy lasted for <2 10^6 yr. Thus, within the XRG terminology, the western half of this RG exhibits both an active (primary) radio lobe and a relic secondary lobe (wing), closely resembling the XRG type radio structure.

3D simulations of relativistic jets striking massive clouds also show structures similar to that seen in 3C 321 forming under suitable circumstances (Choi, Wiita Ryu 2007) and lasting for significant times.

sources.summary: The paper uses spatially resolved spectroscopy of 3C 321 to test photoionization models, favoring mixed-media clouds and finding little variation in line properties across the source.

quotes.quote: To this end, we present high-quality long-slit spectroscopy of the powerful double-nucleus radio galaxy 3C 321. The data have good enough spatial resolution to be able to trace the variation in emission-line properties on kpc scales. Continuum modelling and subtraction enables the faint emission line fluxes to be measured in several regions across the emission line nebula. We plot diagnostic line-ratio diagrams and compare them with the predictions of various photoionization models, finding that the data is best fit by models which assume a mixture of optically thin and thick clouds illuminated by a power-law continuum. The emission line kinematics, line ratios and deduced physical conditions show remarkably little variation across the source.
As a by-product of the continuum subtraction we infer that young stellar populations account for 0.4 of the visible stellar mass in the galaxy, and that these populations are spatially extended.

sources.summary: The review presents 3C 321 as an FRII source where a companion-galaxy encounter disrupts one jet, producing an FRI-like structure and compact jet knot near the companion.

quotes.quote: One FRII source where a jet-environment interaction apparently does affect the jet is 3C 321 (Evans , ApJ submitted). Here an otherwise normal FRII source has a jet that is disrupted downstream of a close passage between the jet and a companion galaxy, forming an FRI-like jet structure on one side. The clear compact hotspots on the same side suggest that this is a temporary interaction that has been going on for only 10^6 years. Immediately adjacent to the companion galaxy is a compact jet knot. However, this knot is relatively faint and is not a source of any detectable optical or X-ray emission, unlike some other jet knots in FRIIs:

sources.summary: The paper describes 1529+24 as an FR II narrow-line radio galaxy with merging double nuclei, merger-triggered starburst activity, and faint complex VLBA emission.

quotes.quote: 1529+24 (3C 321) -- An extended FR II narrow line radio galaxy, with optical evidence of double nuclei. The two components are clearly merging galaxies with evidence for a merger-triggered starburst activity ( ). The kiloparsec-scale nuclear emission has been discussed by . With the VLBA we detect at 1.6 GHz some faint, diffuse emission, difficult to either clean or modelfit. A tentative visibility model fit reveals 7 mJy at 1.6 GHz, in two nearly equal components separated by 0.03 (see Fig. f.1529 ). A faint and complex structure is expected from the large difference between the point-like mas structure at 5 GHz ( 3 mJy) and at 1.6 GHz ( 7 mJy) and the arcsecond core flux density at 5 GHz ( 30 mJy).

sources.summary: The paper presents GMRT/VLA observations of 3C321, finding resolved redshifted H I absorption toward its core, estimating column density, and describing its FRII radio morphology and large projected size.

quotes.quote: We report the results of Giant Metrewave Radio Telescope (GMRT) observations of H i absorption towards the FRII radio galaxy 3C321 (J1531+2404), which is associated with an active galaxy interacting with a companion. The absorption profile towards the radio core is well resolved and consists three components, of which the two prominent ones are red-shifted by 186 and 235 km s ^ 1 relative to the optical systemic velocity. The neutral hydrogen column density towards the core is estimated to be N (H i )=9.23 10 ^ 21 ( T _ s /100)( f_ /1.0) cm ^ 2 , where T _ s and f_c are the spin temperature and covering factor of the background source respectively.

The radio galaxy 3C321 is an FRII radio galaxy at a redshift of 0.0961 (luminosity distance = 436 Mpc) so that 1 arcsec corresponds to 1.759 kpc in a Universe with H _0 =71 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 , _ m =0.27 and _ vac =0.73. Our observations show that it has two prominent hot-spots at the outer edges with an overall angular size of 286 arcsec, which corresponds to a projected linear size of 503 kpc, similar to that found in the literature (e.g. Baum et al. 1988; Lal, Hardcastle Kraft 2008).

sources.summary: The appendix describes 3C 321 as a CO(1-0)-detected galaxy with extended dust absorption, broad velocity width, and a nearby possible interacting companion.

quotes.quote: 3C 321: The HST optical image shows an extended dust absorption in the galaxy. It is clearly detected in the CO(1-0) transition with a velocity width of 500 km/s. For the CO(2-1) emission we have no data. The HST image reveals a nearby galaxy that might be interacting with this galaxy.

sources.summary: The atlas gives 3C 321's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: 3C 321 is a FR II radio source coinciding with the peculiar galaxy LEDA 55317 at a redshift of z= 0.0961 ( D 460 Mpc). It contains two apparent nuclei at optical wavelengths with a projected nuclear separation of 3.5 ( 6.5 kpc) in the south-east direction (PA 54 ; roche_optical/ultraviolet_2000 ). Note that, in addition, there is a foreground star 12 to the north of the system.

sources.summary: The paper reports no unresolved near-infrared core in 3C 321, unusually high X-ray-column extinction, and extinction discrepancies across methods.

quotes.quote: Despite the fact that we have not detected an unresolved core source in 3C 192, 3C 321 and 3C 405, in these cases we have estimated an upper limit to the 2.05 m fluxes applying the same procedure explained above (fitting a PSF plus a S ersic profile with galfit ), and then we fixed the free parameters of the S ersic profile to subtract a PSF only using a trial and error approach, until an unphysical hole appeared owing to over-subtraction.

The optical extinctions based in the X-ray column density using the standard dust to gas ratio Bohlin:1978 , are considerably higher than the optical extinctions estimated from the near-IR SED and X-ray luminosity: 2 to 8 times higher in most cases (even up to 15 and 26 times for 3C 321 and 4C 73.08, respectively).
Looking closely at this figure, the extinction for most of the sources (12 sources) is consistent with the extinction based on the X-ray/near-IR except three cases: 3C 321, 4C 73.08 and 3C 405.

sources.summary: 3C 321 has an excellent UV YSP fit, abundant MIR/FIR dust data, and is grouped with LEG radio galaxies showing enhanced star formation.

quotes.quote: 3C 321. An excellent fit in the UV is obtained with a YSP, in agreement with Tadhunter et al. ( ). There is an abundance of MIR and FIR data, which can be fitted by a mixture of dust with temperatures of 50 K ( M_d = 5.5 10^6 M_ ), 120 K ( M_d = 8.0 10^4 M_ ), and 400 K ( M_d = 40 M_ ).
We note, however, that B2 1141+37, B2 1430+25, B2 1350+31 (3C 293), and 3C 321 (Holt et al. ; Tadhunter et al. ) are LEGs with enhanced star formation (see Table tab:sfagn ).

gmw_00000062.name: 3C 327
gmw_00000062.overview: 3C 327 is a narrow-line radio galaxy [arXiv:0810.3764] identified with a flattened elliptical host [arXiv:1310.2770] that features a complex, possibly double nucleus [arXiv:1008.2683] and a bifurcated dust lane [arXiv:1008.2683]. The galaxy exhibits extended line emission spanning 18 kiloparsecs [arXiv:1903.10768] and double radio lobes with structured hotspot complexes [arXiv:0810.3764, arXiv:1310.2770].

sources.summary: The paper reports no optical hotspot detection for 3C 327, no compact X-ray counterpart to its western hotspot, and two offset compact X-ray components near its eastern hotspots.

quotes.quote: 3C 327 is the only object in our sample to have no optical hotspot detection: as pointed out by mpb03 , a nearby bright disk galaxy makes it hard to detect potentially faint counterparts to the bright double eastern radio hotspots. In the new Chandra data, although there is nearby X-ray emission (Fig. \ 327-l ) there is no detection of a compact component corresponding to the W hotspot. There is, however, a clear detection of compact components near the double E hotspots (Fig. 327-hsex ). Neither of the two X-ray components detected bears a very obvious relation to the radio structure. The component (denoted SX1) closest to the primary hotspot (S1) is separated from it by 2 4 0 1 (4.5 kpc) along the line connecting S1 and the core, while the other X-ray component (SX2) is at 0 8 0 1 (1.5 kpc) from the nearest peak of the radio emission in the S2 region, and considerably further from its center, in the sense of being further away from the primary hotspot than the peak of the radio emission.

sources.summary: The paper presents 3C 327 as a narrow-line radio galaxy with structured eastern and western hotspot complexes, diffuse emission, and possible jet-related southern structure.

quotes.quote: 3C 327 is a Narrow-Line Radio Galaxy (Simpson et al. 1996) at redshift z=0.1039 (Fig. 6a). The eastern hotspot complex consists of a primary knot and a more diffuse secondary one north of it. Both features are embedded in a region of diffuse extended emission (Fig. 6b). The dominant feature in the western hotspot is the slightly curved structure at the very end of the hotspot complex. Leahy et al. (1997) mention the possibility that the brighter structure in the south is part of the radio jet (Fig. 6c).

sources.summary: MUSE finds 3C 327 line emission extending 18 kpc, ordered central velocities, large outer velocity amplitude, and enhanced dispersion along the line of nodes.

quotes.quote: 3C 327: FR II/HEG, 1 = 1.94 kpc. The line emission extends in the NE-SW direction for 18 kpc. The velocity field in the central regions is apparently ordered, with a line of node inclined with respect to the geometrical axis by 40^ . The gas velocity at radii larger than 3 kpc shows a full amplitude of 600 . The velocity dispersion is enhanced along the line of nodes, likely due to the unresolved velocity gradients in this region.

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS 1559+02 as a powerful radio source whose GMOS-S morphology shows disturbed structure, companions, shells, fans, dust, tails, bridges, or multiple nuclei indicating interaction or merger activity.

quotes.quote: The WFPC2/HST images of this NLRG/FRII source reveal a bifurcated dust lane structure extending 3 kpc to the NE of the galaxy nucleus, with the radio axis lying roughly perpendicular to the dust lanes. The nuclear structure, as revealed by the HST image, appears to be complex and possibly double deKoff96,deKoff00 . The presence of a bright star close to the galaxy complicates its morphological classification. However, by looking at our processed images ( unsharp-masked and specially smoothed galaxy-subtracted ; Figure pks1559_online ) the presence of dust lanes becomes clear.

sources.summary: The atlas gives 3C 327's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: 3C 327 is a FR II radio source identified with the flattened elliptical galaxy LEDA 56797 at a redshift of z= 0.1048 ( D 505 Mpc) with a Sy 1 nucleus veron-cetty_catalogue_2010 . 3C 327 features supergalactic-scale double radio lobes in the east-west directions without prominent jets (PA 100 ; e.g., baum_extended_1988,leahy_study_1997 ). 3C 327 was first detected in the MIR with , and was followed up with /IRS and MIPS observations.

gmw_00000063.name: 3C 353
gmw_00000063.overview: 3C 353 is a nearby Fanaroff-Riley class II radio galaxy [arXiv:0801.4315, arXiv:2403.15471] hosted by a giant elliptical galaxy [arXiv:1003.2467, arXiv:1310.2770] with a Seyfert 2 nucleus [arXiv:1310.2770]. It features a pair of large-scale radio jets [arXiv:0806.4240] and a 22-kiloparsec S-shaped gas filament [arXiv:1903.10768] embedded at the edge of a galaxy cluster [arXiv:1101.1807].

sources.summary: The paper treats 3C 353 as a powerful FR II radio source with resolved two-sided jets, Chandra X-ray imaging, and synchrotron X-ray emission rather than an IC/CMB origin.

quotes.quote: 3C 353 ( z = 0.0304 ) is the fourth strongest radio source in the 3C catalog, with a total flux density S_ 57 Jy at 1.4 GHz, and a projected size 4.5 ' . It exhibits hotspots and a pair of large-scale FR II-type jets, clearly visible within filamentary lobes ( ). The jets in 3C 353, constituting about 1 of the entire source luminosity, are well collimated trains of knots with an average width 4'' and jet-counterjet radio brightness asymmetry 2 ( ). Both total and polarized intensity profiles across the jets indicate that the bulk of the jet radio emission is produced at the jet edges, and so presumably within a boundary shear layer. Since these jets are among only a very few FR II jets wide enough to be resolved in X-rays, we planned and conducted a deep Chandra observation of 3C 353, in order to investigate the multiwavelength structure of powerful FR II outflows.

Note that constrained the jet inclination to the line of sight in 3C 353 to be 60^ < _ j < 90^ ; these large angles to the line of sight are strongly supported by the observed two-sidedness of both the radio and X-ray jets. Accordingly, we can approximately substitute _ j 1 / _ j , obtaining the observed luminosity ratio L_ ic/cmb / L_ syn independently of the jet kinematic factors. The resulting value, 10^ 3 , is in strong disagreement with the observed X-ray-to-radio luminosity ratio L_ X / L_ R 1 . Thus, we conclude that the IC/CMB model cannot explain the observed X-ray emission of the 3C 353 jets, unless very large departures from energy equipartition, B 100 _ j ^ 1 G, are invoked. The only possibility left is therefore that the observed X-ray emission of 3C 353 results from the synchrotron radiation of some flat-spectrum high-energy electron population, most likely separate to the one producing the observed radio emission.

sources.summary: The paper describes 3C 353 as an extended FR II D-galaxy on a cluster edge, with asymmetric radio lobes, a brighter eastern jet and hot spot, and banded plus isotropic RM structure.

quotes.quote: 3C 353 is an extended FR II radio source identified with a D-galaxy embedded at the periphery of a cluster of galaxies. The best estimate for the inclination of the jets is 90 Swain98 . The eastern jet is slightly brighter and ends in a well-defined hot spot. The radio lobes have markedly different morphologies: the eastern lobe is round with sharp edges, while the western lobe is elongated with an irregular shape. The location of the source within the cluster is of particular interest for this work and might account for the different shapes of the lobes.

We present detailed imaging of Faraday rotation and depolarization for the radio galaxies 0206+35, 3C 270, 3C 353 and M 84, based on Very Large Array observations at multiple frequencies in the range 1365 to 8440 MHz. All of the sources show highly anisotropic banded rotation measure (RM) structures with contours of constant RM perpendicular to the major axes of their radio lobes. All except M 84 also have regions in which the RM fluctuations have lower amplitude and appear isotropic.

sources.summary: The paper studies 3C 353 as a bright, under-studied FR II radio galaxy whose lobes show inverse-Compton X-rays, filamentary radio structure, and X-ray/radio variations requiring magnetic-field changes.

quotes.quote: Using six frequencies of new and archival radio data and new XMM-Newton observations of the Fanaroff Riley class II radio galaxy 3C 353, we show that inverse-Compton emission is detected in the radio lobes of this source at a level consistent with what is seen in other objects. We argue that variations in the X-ray/radio ratio in the brighter eastern lobe require positionally varying magnetic field strength.

3C 353 is a FR II radio galaxy associated with the cluster Zw 1718.1-0108. Although it is one of the brightest extragalactic sources in the sky at low frequencies, it is relatively poorly studied, presumably due to its low declination and low Galactic latitude. swain96jlg observed 3C 353 at four radio frequencies with the NRAO Very Large Array (VLA) and published a study of the polarisation variation across the jets, which favoured a spine-sheath model swain98jlg . Their VLA observations revealed filamentary structure within the lobes making 3C 353 an excellent target for a spatially resolved study of electron distribution and magnetic fields. X-ray emission from the cluster associated with 3C 353 was first detected by iwasawa00jlg in ASCA and ROSAT images.

sources.summary: The paper describes 3C 353 with ordered central gas rotation and a 22 kpc S-shaped filament whose velocity field varies with radius.

quotes.quote: 3C 353: FR II/LEG, 1 = 0.60 kpc. In the central region the gas is in ordered rotation with a line of node oriented at PA 30^ . An S-shaped filament extends for 22 kpc in the north-south direction. Its velocity field is rather complex: on the north side the gas is initially blueshifted by 100 , but its velocity then steadily increases at larger radii before falling back to the systemic velocity for > 10 kpc. In the central 3 kpc both the velocity dispersion and the / \ ratio are larger than in the regions at larger radii.

sources.summary: The paper presents 3C 353 as a LINER-spectrum FR II radio source hosted by a round giant elliptical with smooth extinction gradients and a small central dust lane.

quotes.quote: 3C 353 is an FR II radio source with a LINER spectrum simpson+96 hosted by a round giant elliptical with M_H( Host )=-22.99 at z=0.03 . There are no signs of disturbance, with very circular ( e 0.04 ) isophotes on the optical image martel+99 . The color-map shows a smooth gradient in extinction, with the highest values up to V=H=2.7 at the center, where a small dust lane is visible near the core dekoff+00 . We estimate A_H 0.1 mag for this source in the central region, assuming a diffuse dust model after mathis90 .

sources.summary: The paper describes 3C 353 through three jet knots, a large viewing-angle estimate, measured redshift and scale, and multi-wavelength data used for modeling.

quotes.quote: 3C 353: The jet of 3C 353 has three significant knots, E23, E88, and W47 Kataoka2008 . Swain1998 estimate a rather large viewing angle of 60 < <90 for the whole jet based on Very Large Array (VLA) observations at 8.4 GHz. The measured redshift of the host galaxy is z = 0.0304 , corresponding to a conversion scale of 1 = 0.60 kpc Kataoka2008 . The radio, optical, and previous data are taken from Kataoka2008 .

sources.summary: The paper uses 3C353 as a radio flux calibrator, describing it as a nearby stable radio galaxy with 1.42 GHz flux used to derive calibration factors.

quotes.quote: For the majority of the observations since September 2020, a transit scan of the radio galaxy 3C353 at the time of observation is available. 3C353 is a nearby radio galaxy and is expected to have a very stable flux, which makes it a suitable source for calibration purposes. Its flux density at 1.42 GHz is 56.7 Jy , as reported by baars1977 , which translates into a source temperature of 5.67 K for the Stockert telescope. Using the flux density before and after 3C353 is in the beam (OFF) in machine units and the flux density when 3C353 is fully in the beam (ON), the flux calibration factor, K, and the system temperature, T_ sys , can be estimated for flux calibrating the observed data.

sources.summary: The appendix reports 3C 353 as a CO(1-0)-detected galaxy whose inner optical isophotes are peanut-shaped or affected by a dust lane or true double nucleus.

quotes.quote: 3C 353: It is clearly detected in the CO(1-0) transition line, with a velocity width of 200 km/s. propose that the outer isophotes of 3C 353 are very circular while the inner isophotes are elongated in a southeast to northeast direction and are roughly peanut-shaped. also stated that this may result from a small-scale dust lane bifurcating the nucleus in a rough north-south direction or from a true double nucleus.

sources.summary: The atlas gives 3C 353's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: 3C 353 is a FR II radio source identified with the giant elliptical galaxy LEDA 60102 madrid_hubble_2006 at a redshift of z= 0.0304 ( D 138 Mpc) with a Sy 2 nucleus veron-cetty_catalogue_2010 . It features supergalactic-scale biconical radio lobes in the east-west directions with both jets being visible (PA 85 ; e.g., morganti_radio_1993,swain_internal_1998 ). 3C 353 was not detected with and appears as a weak compact source in the images.

gmw_00000064.name: 3C 382
gmw_00000064.overview: The broad-line radio galaxy 3C 382 is a distorted elliptical galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/9805154, arXiv:1310.2770] featuring a Fanaroff-Riley II radio morphology with two lobes and an inclined jet [arXiv:0704.2756]. Its active nucleus exhibits a strong soft X-ray excess [arXiv:2308.08528], a warm absorber [arXiv:1104.1812], and weak reflection features [arXiv:2401.16665, arXiv:1408.5281].

sources.summary: The paper treats 3C 382 as a broad-line radio galaxy with weaker reflection features, where combining multi-epoch XMM-Newton and NuSTAR spectra improves inclination fits.

quotes.quote: The broad-line radio galaxy 3C 382 exhibits a WA, a soft excess, and an Fe K line, but no Compton hump torresi2010,ballantyne2014 . According to ursini2018 , the soft excess comes from a warm Comptonization component with a photon index 2.4-2.5 , a temperature of 0.6 keV, and a corresponding optical depth of 20 . These parameters are all in general agreement with those in radio-quiet Seyfert galaxies e.g., jin2012,petrucci2018 .
Compared to I Zwicky 1, 3C 382 has weaker reflection features and less complex outflow structures. It was observed simultaneously five times in 2016 (PI: F. Ursini; Table tab:3c_spec_divid ).

For 3C 382, the result from epoch is selected to represent a case 3 fit. We conduct a case 1 fit with the XMM-Newton observation from epoch , a case 2 fit with the XMM-Newton spectra from epoch and NuSTAR spectra from epoch b , and a case 4 fit with epochs b and and NuSTAR data from epochs a , d , and e . The broadband model fitting in the previous section is a case 5 fit.

sources.summary: 3C 382 is described as a two-lobe radio source and distorted elliptical with very broad Balmer lines; its X-ray spectrum is hard, nearly absorption-free, and shows little Compton reflection.

quotes.quote: 3C 382 The radio morphology of 3C 382 is typical for two-lobe sources with characteristic hot spots (McDonald, Kenderdine Neville 1968). Optically, it appears as a distorted elliptical (Smith Heckman 1989) with extremely broad Balmer lines reaching FWZI 25,000 km s ^ 1 (Osterbrock, Koski Phillips 1975). observed 3C 382 several times during 5 days in 1989 July (Kaastra, Kunieda Awaki 1991).
